Course Title: Doctor of Medicine (MD)
Course Description:
The Duke-NUS Doctor of Medicine (MD) programme is developed with the mission to enable graduates to not only practice medicine, but also improve the practice of medicine in an evolving healthcare environment.
This programme spans 4 years, and graduates are awarded a joint Doctor of Medicine (MD) degree by Duke University and the National University of Singapore (NUS).

Pre-Clerkship Experience
The hallmark of the Duke-NUS pre-clerkship curriculum is in both the content and the TeamLEAD pedagogy. In alignment with the Duke University School of Medicine curriculum, Duke-NUS integrates the basic science content into one year of study rather than two as in most graduate medical schools. We value class time as an opportunity for students to apply knowledge obtained from preparatory materials (eg. Video-recorded lectures, readings etc.), in a way that fosters the critical skills and aptitudes necessary for future leaders of medicine: teamwork, leadership, and the pursuit of curiosity. Our dedicated teaching faculty are drawn from our Signature Research Programs and clinical affiliates.

Clerkship Experience
In their first clinical year, our students learn the fundamentals of patient care such as establishing rapport, respect and trust, and develop their clinical skills in history taking, physical exam and management of patients. During this broad-based exposure to the core specialties, students will experience the fundamentals of Pediatrics, Obstetrics and Gynecology, Neurology, Psychiatry, Surgery, and Medicine. Our hospital affiliates have a long and distinguished history of clinical teaching.

Research Experience
Duke-NUS students participate in a 9-month mentored research activity that allows them to explore cutting-edge scientific issues, and helps build the skills fundamental to becoming leaders in healthcare.

Course Title: Doctor of Philosophy
Course Description:
The MSc and PhD programmes are research-intensive programmes that emphasise both the mastery of research skills and the acquisition of core knowledge in a specified area of interest. Students will complete coursework and conduct independent research under the supervision of a faculty member. From Year 1, both MSc and PhD students will embark on their research projects, complete core courses and choose from a range of elective courses that cater to individual research interests. In Year 2, MSc students will start writing their thesis, while PhD students will complete a PhD Qualifying Examination (PQE) which assesses their ability to complete PhD requirements based on their academic progress and quality of their research. MSc students who wish to transfer to the PhD programme need to complete the PhD coursework requirements, and pass the PQE within 2 years.

In Years 3-5, PhD students will execute the rest of their research plans and submit and defend the thesis.

Course Title: Master of Clinical Investigation
Course Description: The primary aim of this programme is to grow the local clinician scientist / investigator talent pool by training clinicians with interest and aptitude in research.  The MCI programme provides essential didactic training for clinical research and will equip these clinicians with the basic analytical knowledge and skills needed to design and conduct clinical investigations that are relevant to patient care within their areas of specialisation.  This training will also allow practising clinicians to collaborate more effectively with other investigators in translational research projects.

Course Title: Master of Public Health
Course Description:
The Master of Public Health (MPH) is a professional degree that seeks to provide a rigorous and interdisciplinary approach to learning, with an emphasis on finding evidence-based and innovative solutions to the current and future public health challenges in Asia.
Leveraging on the NUS system and our close links with the regional health clusters, social service sectors, government ministries and non-profit organisations, our School ensures that research and teaching provided is both progressive and comprehensive.

MPH graduates are equipped to address both traditional and emerging public health issues, assuming leadership and administrative positions in diverse public health disciplines in local as well as international settings.

Course Title: Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery
Course Description: The undergraduate medical programme is a five-year course leading to the degrees of Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery (M.B.,B.S.) offered by the Yong Loo Lin School of Medicine. The curriculum establishes a firm foundation in the basic medical sciences while providing students with high quality clinical experiences. Throughout the course, the curriculum emphasises the clinical relevance the basic sciences. The first year of the course emphasises normal structure and function of the human body, underscoring the clinical relevance of what is taught. Progressively in the second year, the course focuses on abnormal structure and function including principles of pharmacotherapy, general pathology including cancer biology, and the body?s defences (immunology and infection). The primary focus from the third to the fifth year is high quality clinical experience in medical and surgical-related disciplines with underpinning medical sciences. Students also have the opportunity to take local and overseas electives during this period.

Course Title: Master of Science (Audiology)
Course Description:
On completion of the course, students will obtain a strong theoretical foundation of hearing sciences, develop practical competencies and possess a good appreciation of the following:

-Assessment, diagnosis, and management of hearing impairment and disordered auditory related systems in adults and children.
-Assessment, diagnosis, and management of balance dysfunctions in adults.
-Hearing aid evaluation, verification, and validation for adults and children.
-Programming in implantable devices (e.g. cochlear implant, bone anchored hearing aids, and middle ear implant) for adults and children.
-Research skills necessary for service development and evaluation.
-Basic counseling skills for working with hearing impaired individuals and families.
-Communication skills for multi-disciplinary teamwork.
-Public health challenges and effective hearing care.
-Uniquely Singaporean and Asian education, research and clinical focuses.
-Strong spirit of excellence, continued learning, innovation and community service.

The students will learn the critical care nursing fundamentals and management of ethical and legal issues, patient and family education, and alterations in psychosocial, sleep, nutrition, comfort and state of consciousness. The students also will learn the management of critically ill patients based on organ system-based alterations, and critical care nursing procedures.

Course Title: Social Psychology
Course Description:
Develop a better understanding of the social behaviour of the self
Describe the important theories and concepts of social psychology
Examine how the basic principles and theories of social psychology apply to our daily lives.
Discuss how the different types of interpersonal relationships that people are involved in their daily lives develop and affect us.
Apply the theories of social psychology to explain various real-life social phenomena, such as conformity, prejudice, persuasion, aggression, and helping behaviour.
Analyze how certain theories of social psychology apply to the local Singapore context, and/or may not apply universally to all cultures.

Course Title: Personality and Individual Differences
Course Description:
Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of the perspectives in personality psychology.
Discuss the major theories and relevant research findings.
Compare the strengths and limitations of the major theories.
Differentiate among the different perspectives and evaluate how key assumptions in each approach differentially account for individual differences.
Examine how new insights gleaned from the various approaches contribute to an understanding of human behaviour and mental processes.
Recognise the extent to which traditions and major theories in personality direct trends in current research.
Apply theoretical concepts from personality to case studies.

Course Title: Organisational Psychology
Course Description:
Discuss key theories/models/findings in organisational psychology.
Compare different ways of conducting research in organisational psychology.
Examine key trends/issues/challenges faced by companies and I/O psychologists.
Illustrate different approaches to recruit/select/appraise employees.
Explain how to motivate and lead employees to be productive and satisfied with their work.
Show different strategies for employees to cope with stress and engage in positive work attitudes/behaviours (vs negative work attitudes/behaviours).

Course Title: Developmental Psychology
Course Description:
Outline key milestones and changes in the physical, cognitive and psychosocial development of human beings
Explain how the different developmental theories contribute to our understanding of human development
Examine various issues and challenges of different age groups or stages of development
Discuss key theories in developmental psychology across various aspects of development
Apply different developmental theories to various aspects of development
Illustrate the various stages of development

Course Title: Emotion
Course Description:
Analyse the key affective, cognitive, behavioural, and biological components of emotions
Demonstrate knowledge of the key theoretical perspectives, methodological approaches, and empirical evidence on emotions
Judge the implications of emotion on psychological, behavioural, and life outcomes based on theoretical perspectives and empirical evidence on emotions
Design research studies to investigate key questions of interest in the field of emotions
Propose strategies for regulating emotions and using emotions to achieve specific objectives in real world situations
Appraise how emotions can be applied to understand and explain real-world events
Critique existing research in a domain by drawing together theories and findings from various sources

Course Title: Health Psychology
Course Description:
Describe common terms, concepts, and theories of health psychology
Explain the risks and protective factors of various health-related disorders and diseases
Examine the models of behaviour change
Develop different intervention methods for health promotion and prevention
Apply different intervention methods for health promotion and prevention
Appraise the effectiveness of different intervention methods for health promotion and prevention

Course Title: Cognitive Psychology: Exploring the Mind
Course Description:
Examine major theories, principles, and concepts in cognitive psychology.
Discuss relevant theories and research findings to explain real-life behaviour and cognitions.
Appraise cognitive theories and how hypotheses are verified through empirical testing.
Illustrate how cognitive psychology provides evidence of the link between cognitive functions of the mind and behaviour.
Apply cognitive theories and findings to everyday mental-life and behaviour.
Prepare an experimental report based on a pre-programmed experiment and the interpretation of experimental results.

Course Title: Cultural Psychology
Course Description:
Develop an overview to Cultural Psychology
Assess the range of epistemological approaches to Cultural Psychology
Propose why certain epistemological approaches are preferable under certain contexts
Analyse psychological issues and phenomena using a cultural psychological framework
Examine how the cultural psychological approach can be applied to key subfields in psychology (e.g., identity, emotion, and cognition and perception)
Demonstrate an understanding of the dynamics of cross-cultural scenarios within a multicultural context

Course Title: Group Dynamics
Course Description:
Examine the principles and concepts that guide the study of group dynamics.
Discuss the necessity and importance of studying human behaviour in the context of their groups.
Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of the major theories and models of group dynamics.
Apply knowledge in understanding the transformation from personal identity to social identity.
Develop practical research skills in doing research in group dynamics.
Appraise case studies and research findings based on the understanding of group dynamics.

Course Title: Personnel Assessment and Selection
Course Description:
Illustrate the challenges of personnel selection procedures and assessments.
Discuss the reliability and validity of assessment and selection measures
Demonstrate the complexity of assessing employee performance
Examine the use of recruitment, selection and assessment measures.
Criticize selection technique and principles
Propose appropriate selection and assessment strategies

Course Title: Positive Psychology
Course Description:
Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of the social psychological research and theories on well-being.
Examine the various social psychological research and theories and on well-being.
Analyse the cognitions, behaviors, and factors which enhance or undermine optimal human growth.
Evaluate the theories regarding the factors that influence well-being.
Apply positive psychology theories to your own lives.
Propose recommendations for improving well-being using the theories and findings from positive psychology.
Assess what constitutes well-being or a “good life” based on the empirical evidence in the literature.

Course Title: Psychology of Creativity

Analyze how the environment affects creativity.
Propose ways to measure creativity in different contexts.
Appraise different processes and stages of creativity

Course Title: Psychology of Consumer Behaviour

Appraise the factors related to consumer decision making.
Discuss the factors that influence how consumers process information.
Evaluate the influence of the social environment and contemporary strategies for marketers.
Demonstrate critical and analytical skills in analysing the effectiveness of techniques and strategies used in the psychology of consumer behaviour.
Apply principles from the psychology of consumer behaviour in conducting a qualitative study.

Course Title: Work Stress
Course Description:
Analyze stress theories.
Criticize stress theories and their associated research methods .
Apply stress theories to real world situations.
Demonstrate how people can cope with stress in the workplace.
Formulate practical recommendations to solve organizational challenges using stress theories.
Propose real-world examples of stress theories in action.

Course Title: Biological Basis of Psychology

Discuss biopsychological theories of human behaviour.
Examine the brain structure and their connectivity.

Appraise biopsychological models of human behaviour.
Illustrate the study of the biological basis of behaviour using various research methods.
Apply biopsychological concepts to real-life situations

Course Title: Medical Device Regulation in ASEAN and Asia-Pacific(Synchronous e-learning)
Course Description:
"This course provides a comprehensive review of medical device regulation in the ASEAN countries, China and the Asia-Pacific. The emergence of harmonization in the ASEAN community will be discussed and students will see how harmonization efforts translate into regulatory requirements. Regulatory and submission strategy for the ASEAN market will be a key focus of this course with a blend of practical and applied concepts to include the review of a sample submission.                                    1. To gain an understanding of ASEAN countries Device Regulation like Singapore, Malaysia, Thailand etc 
2. To gain an understanding of AP countries Device Regulation like China, Australia, Japan, South Korea, Taiwan etc
3. To learn to develop regulatory strategy in these different countries
4. Be able to work in a team
5. Be conversant in individual and group presentation and report writing
"

Course Title: Pre-Market Requirements and Post Market Surveillance(Sync e-learning)
Course Description:
"Premarket approval is conducted by appropriate regulatory authorities and Conformity Assessment Bodies bodies to evaluate the safety and effectiveness of medical devices prior to marketing. The process may vary from jurisdiction to jurisdiction. The students will learn about the essential principles of safety & performance of medical devices including various design and manufacturing parameters and requirements. This module will outline the various strategies and components of such post-market surveillance systems including vigilance reporting, handling of customer complaints and product recalls.                                          1. To gain an understanding of the pre-market approval process and post market obligations
2. To be conversant with the essential principles of safety and performance of medical devices.
3. To gain an understanding of the requirements of postmarket vigilance including reporting, and complaints handling and recalls.
Understanding conformity assessment of medical devices and how it is related to quality management system.
"

Course Title: Next-Generation Device Regulations(Synchronous e-learning)
Course Description: This module involves supervised self-study in the field of medical device regulatory science. The work will typically entails the assignment of medical devices developed using novel advanced technologies so that the student will spend the semester to explore the challenges of regulation of such devices and to develop innovative framework requirement for regulatory approval. During the course, the student will be presented with different confronted with scenarios including product design failure, clinical trials mishaps, IP issues, manufacturing non-compliance. These scenarios present to the student real-life case studies that challenges the student ability to solve/remedy the problems. Depending on the nature of the independent study, the student will develop appropriate skills in critical evaluation of various regulatory pathways, considering the various compounding factors like regulatory requirements from different countries, the clinical evaluation requirements, testings standards, reimbursement models, pre-market submission, post market survellience etc.

Course Title: Basic First Aid (BFA) + AED Provider Course
Course Description: The Basic First Aid (BFA) + AED Provider Course enables participants to learn how to determine unresponsiveness, activate emergency medical services as well as perform chest compressions-only CPR and use of AED on adult unconscious casualties. Course Description:

b)	Equip learners with effective communication skills, cultural awareness and person-centred care when caring for patients.
c)	Equip learners to identify common types of mental illnesses and signs of early mental health issues.
d)	Prepare learners to assist patients with Activities of Daily Living, including use of equipment and performing nursing interventions.

g)	Prepare learners to comply with infection control procedures and use Personal Protective Equipment during patient care delivery.
h)	Equip learners with knowledge of reporting procedures.
i)	Equip learners to assist patients during admission and discharge procedures.

Course Title: Local Herbs for Health Series 1
Course Description: This course introduces the common herbal plants found in Singapore. It covers the classification, types and species of the herbal plants, their tastes, properties, indications, methods of use, therapeutic effects and prescription for treatment of various chronic diseases.

Course Title: Local Herbs for Health Series 2
Course Description: This course introduces the common herbal plants found in Singapore. It covers the classification, types and species of the herbal plants, their tastes, properties, indications, methods of use, therapeutic effects and prescription for treatment of various chronic diseases.

Course Title: Digital Health and Health Informatics - Synchronous elearning
Course Description:
The Digital Health and Health/Nursing Informatics is a 1-day programme that aims to equip learners with an understanding of digital health trends and Health/Nursing Informatics.

Upon successful completion of this course, the participants will be able to: 
• Learn about Singapore’s Smart Nation initiatives and appreciate the impact of technological advancement in healthcare.
• Understand nursing informatics and discuss the various applications of information systems. 
• Investigate the utility of social media within the contexts of consumer health information, client education, and professional practice.
• Identify the applications in connected care and enabling technologies for future living.

Course Title: Digital Health and Health Informatics
Course Description:
The Digital Health and Health Informatics is a 1-day programme that aims to equip learners with an understanding of digital health trends and Health Informatics.
Upon successful completion of this course, the learner will be able to:

•  Understand health informatics and discuss the various applications of information systems.
•  Investigate the utility of social media within the contexts of consumer health information, client education, and professional practice.
•  Identify the applications in connected care and enabling technologies for future living.

Course Title: Technology in the war against diabetes - Synchronous elearning
Course Description: Diabetes is on the rise globally. In this course, participants will learn about the latest technology in diabetes management. Topics such as novel blood glucose monitoring devices (eg: smartwatch) will be covered. In addition, participants will discover the various digital tools and platforms that may be used to provide better blood glucose monitoring and information on diabetic diet.

Course Title: Basic Principles of ATP (Adenosine Triphosphate) bioluminescence system in hygiene / sanitation monitoring
Course Description: The purpose of this course offers an urban solution, to equip users from different agencies or organisation with the skills and ability to use ATP bioluminscence reader and swab as a rapid technology to qualify their cleaning efficacy. This allows them to establish an autonomous hygiene monitoring practice and system for workplace health and safety. In this one day program, participants will understand the Science to cleaning and sanitisation, use the ATP reader to check condition of contact surface and interpret results to have a more thorough view of the quality of the cleaning and housekeeping. ATP reader is a quick assessment/ rapid tool so that troubleshooting and corrective actions related to hygiene and cleanliness (if required) can be implemented.

Course Title: Sanitisation technology and the management of infectious disease threat - Synchronous elearning
Course Description: This course will cover an overview of utilizing technology on sanitation improvement to prevent infectious diseases sch as Influenza infection, Hand-Foot-Mouth Disease and conjunctivitis. In addition, various informative digital resources on viruses and bacteria, different serotypes of infectious agents, herd immunity and how certain strains are more pathogenic will also be covered.

Course Title: Writing Effective Grant Proposals
Course Description:
This course is designed for researchers, scientists and those involved in R&D projects to help them understand the fundamentals of writing a successful research grant paper to secure funding from government agencies and private enterprises to support their research. 

			 

An overview of the grant writing process is given followed by instructions on how to write the different parts of the grant proposal. Participants will learn strategies for writing persuasive grant applications such as analysis of requests for proposals (RFPs) to understand their requirements and developing a writing plan which addresses these in their grant proposals. They will also learn how to develop proposals with good flow, tying together research objectives, methodologies and applicability of the research. Essential writing skills for writing clearly, concisely, coherently and convincingly will be taught and tips on how to make their proposals stand out. 



At the completion of the course, learners will be able to:

 

1. Understand the grant writing process and develop a strategic approach to writing a successful grant application based on addressing the requirements, needs and priorities of the grant-awarding agency.

2. Identity the characteristics of successful and non-successful grant proposals. 

3. Analyse a request for a grant proposal to determine the requirements, needs and priorities of the funding organisation and work out how to align ones research with these. 

4. Ensure logical flow in proposals by tying together the requirements of the funding agency, objectives, methodology and application of research.

5. Master key language skills required for writing grant proposals that are clear, concise, coherent and convincing.

Course Title: Analytics for Better Health
Implementation Science for Health Services
Course Description:
This module is designed to introduce health care professionals to the principles of translating evidence for better services into a clinical setting. 
Participants will learn processes and factors associated with successful integration of evidence-based interventions within a particular setting, assess whether the core components of the original intervention were faithfully transported to the real-world setting and gain new knowledge about the adaptation of the implemented intervention to the local context.

Course Title: Data Science plus Healthcare
Course Description: This module will expose students to the foundation concepts, case studies and applications, some mathematics behind data science models and algorithms. There will also be practical sessions for model development, training, validation and tests. Students will acquire new knowledge of data science techniques. The new knowledge from this course will enable predictions to be made about likely diagnoses, prognoses of health conditions and risks of adverse events. There will also be a mini-project and healthcare case studies to demonstrate the applicability of data science as a key enabler for improving the delivery of health services.

Course Title: Introduction to Global Medical Device Regulation (Synchronous e-learning)
Course Description:
"This introductory course provides key foundational information related to the global regulation of medical devices with emphasis placed on ASEAN, EU and Asia-Pacific countries. Additionally, the role of the global regulatory professional will be examined in the context of these regulatory frameworks. Covering pertinent subtopics such as harmonization, ethics and legal perspectives, the course will prepare students for more in-depth examinations of submissions and the development of regulatory strategy.                                            
1. To be able to understand the role of the Regulatory Profession and Professionals
2. To understand Medical Devices: Definition and Lifecycle
3. To understand the practice of Professional Ethics in Medical Device Regulation
4. To increase the students’ understanding of the critical role quality and compliance have in supporting good regulatory practices
5. Be able to work in a team
6. Be conversant in individual and group presentation and report writing. 
"

Course Title: Medical Device Regulatory Process Strategy and Planning (Synchronous e-learning)
Course Description:
"In this module, the students take a prototype through a regulatory pathway in one of the regions such as US, EU, Asia Pacific, ASEAN and China based upon their understanding of the medical device regulatory process. Students will have both team and individual activities and deliverables for this module culminating in a simulated submission to be carried out by student teams. 
The project module brings together the knowledge acquired from the earlier three modules i.e., BN5511, BN5512 and BN5513, to enables the students to apply the concepts learned.                                                                1. To learn to strategize a Regulatory plan or proposal based on an existing clinical prototype medical device
2. To be familiar with the key elements of a good regulatory plan
3. Be conversant in individual and group presentation and report writing

"

Course Title: Informatics for Health
Course Description:
Upon completion, students will be able to use knowledge of patient care combined with
understanding of informatics concepts, methods, and tools to:
1. Assess information and knowledge needs of health care professionals and patients.
2. Characterize, evaluate, and refine clinical processe
3. Develop, implement, and refine clinical decision support systems, and lead or participate in t
procurement, customization, development, implementation, management, evaluation, and continuous
improvement of clinical information systems.

Course Title: Medical Device Regulation in the US and EU (Synchronous e-learning)
Course Description:
"The module delves into medical device regulation in the US, with an emphasis on the product lifecycle and an extended examination of the submissions process in the US. Key sub-topics include interactions with the US Food and Drug Administration (FDA), submission types (e.g., PMAs and 510(k)s) and postmarketing.
This course provides a comprehensive review of medical device regulation in the EU, with emphasis on product lifecycle and an extended examination of the submissions process in the EU. Key sub-topics include clinical assessment of medical devices, Medical-Devices Directive (MDD), conformity assessment pathways and the impact of harmonization efforts on the region.
1. To gain an understanding of US Medical Device Regulation
2. To understand the basics of Clinical Evaluations of Medical Devices
3. To gain an understanding of EU Medical Device Regulatory Structure, including details in Medical Devices Directives (MDD) In Vitro Diagnostic Devices Directive (IVDD) and Active Implantable Medical Devices Directive (AIMDD)
4. Be able to work in a team
5. Be conversant in individual and group presentation and report writing
"

Course Title: Fundamentals of Simulation-Based Healthcare Education
Course Description: This two-day workshop is designed to be interactive; consisting of short didactic sessions and hands-on practice of the essential skills needed for simulation-based healthcare education. Participants will work in groups to run their simulation scenarios, discuss the common pitfalls encountered, and receive feedback on these sessions. They will also have an opportunity to share their experiences and exchange ideas on integrating simulation-based learning into their existing curricula. This workshop is aimed at faculty who are interested in using simulation methodologies to enhance their curriculum.

Course Title: Navigating Medical Law and Ethics in the Current and Future Landscape
Course Description: "This course seeks to refresh and update clinicians, patient safety officers, nursing staff, and clinical pharmacists with a current understanding of medical law and statutes involving ethical and legal considerations affecting clinical care practices in an evolving litigious landscape. Primary outcome of this course is to achieve higher-order critical thinking skills (thought leadership) towards enhanced risk management capabilities of healthcare professionals."

Course Title: Postgraduate Course in Palliative Medicine for Doctors (Synchronous & Asynchronous)
Course Description:
"This is a 3-day foundational course, designed especially for doctors across all settings. This course covers the basic principles and practice of palliative medicine. The trainers in this course include experienced doctors, nurses and allied health professionals from the palliative care field. They will cover a comprehensive range of palliative care topics through interactive lectures, case discussions and role play activities. The aim is to build / increase professional confidence and competence in participants by equipping them with the necessary knowledge and skills to work with patients and families facing life-threatening illnesses. Some of the topics covered within this course include an introduction to palliative care, pain and symptom management, communication with patients and families, ethical dilemmas, dealing with psychological problems and use of existing hospice services in Singapore
"

Course Title: Integrated Clinical Decision Making and Management I
Course Description:
"At the end of the courses the learners will be able to:
- provide patient-centered care to patients with cardiovascular, respiratory and gastrointestinal system disorders
- develop clinical reasoning and health assessment skills , shared decision-making  and effective communication."

Course Title: Integrated Clinical Decision Making and Management II
Course Description:
"At the end of the courses the learners will be able to:
- provide patient-centered care to patients with central nervous, endocrine and renal system as well as general medicine and psychological disorders
- develop clinical reasoning and health assessment skills , shared decision-making  and effective communication."

Course Title: Regulation of Cell, Tissue and Gene Therapies (Synchronous e-learning)
Course Description:
"1. To be able to define advanced therapy products and describe how they are different to chemical and biological entities.
2. To be able to describe the lifecycle for advanced therapy products and compare how it is different from more traditional pharmaceuticals.
3. To be familiar with the regulatory frameworks governing advanced therapy products and appreciate different regulatory requirements set by major national regulatory agencies."

Course Title: Core Module (Healthcare Ethics Law and Professionalism) (Asynchronous and Synchronous e-learning)
Course Description: This one day course is to train healthcare professionals to identify and address common ethical and moral issues in the clinical setting. This course is designed to be interactive - consisting of short didactic sessions and online learning. The online learning will provide you the foundation of ethical core values while the didactic sessions will hone your understanding of these values through group discussions, analysis of cases and application of ethical values and principles. You will have an opportunity to share your experiences and exchange ideas with other learners on the application of ethical knowledge to existing work practices.

Course Title: Clinical Design and Evaluation of Medical Devices (Synchronous e-learning)
Course Description:
"This course provides a comprehensive review of clinical design and evaluation for medical devices. It focuses on the roles of clinical evaluations in product development including the roles of various pivotal trials and the postmarket study. The students will learn to design a clinical study taking into consideration the various aspects of the study like the ethics, treatment regimen, study population, the product effectiveness and safety endpoints. In addition, the course will guide the students through a rigorous and systematic review of and integration of clinical data follow by the drafting of a clinical evaluation report.                     
1. To gain an understanding of the general principles of clinical design, evaluation and strategy
2. To understand the key elements and terms of clinical study design and evaluation, including various statistical tools available
3. To be able to perform systematic review of and integration of clinical data and the drafting of a clinical evaluation report
4. To understand the clinical investigations application process and reimbursement
5. Be conversant in individual and group presentation and report writing
"

Course Title: Early Childhood Development - The Touchpoints Approach (Classroom and Synchronous e-learning)
Course Description:
"Brazelton Touchpoints is a practical approach for building strong family-child relationships from birth through the earliest years, laying the foundation for children's early learning and healthy development. Research and experience have shown that simply 'telling' parents what to do is not effective. Touchpoints is a model of practice that enables providers (including caregivers and educators) to establish collaborative and encouraging relationshps with families based on a shared interest in the healthy development of their child. Touchpoints provides for transformation through:                                   
1) changing practice from prescription to collaboration   
2) building capacity within communities through engagement and promoting strengths within families.      
After completion of this training, participants will be able to:
• Understand and describe the Touchpoints Approach
• Apply the Touchpoints Approach in the Early Childhood Care & Education (ECCE) setting to understand the themes of 'gatekeeping' between the provider and the family
• Understand and apply the Touchpoints Principles and Assumptions through Reflective Practice
• Understand and apply the Touchpoints Developmental and Relational Frameworks
• Use ‘The Behaviour of the Child as Your Language’ as the cornerstone of the Touchpoints Approach"

Course Title: Medical Device Design, Development and Testing (Synchronous e-learning)
Course Description:
"This course provides a comprehensive coverage of the initial phases of the product lifecycle, mainly the design, development and testing of the medical device. Although not part of the ISO13485 or 21 CFR 820, they constitute crucial parts of the product lifecycle. Here the students will explores the ideation process considering a myriad of factors including the product market size, the competing products, the regulatory pathway, intellectual property issues, reimbursement model etc. The course will also cover the understanding of various testing standards (US ASTM, China YY, EU ISO etc) for verification and validation of different medical devices.                               
1. To gain an understanding of the medical device lifecycle
2. To demonstrate understanding of the various GLP, GMP, GLP requirements for product development
3. To demonstrate understanding of the manufacturing requirements for product development
4. To be familiar with the roles of standards in the V&V of medical devices
To be familiar with the multiple factors like product market size, the competing products, the regulatory pathway, intellectual property issues, reimbursement model etc during the product development stage
"

Course Title: Diploma in Nursing

Course Title: Pharmaceutical inventory management
Course Description:
The demand for healthcare and eldercare services is set to grow due to the rapidly ageing population. Retail pharmacies will play increasingly important roles to promote preventative healthcare, and continuing care at home and in the community. In order to make sure the retail pharmacies are sustainable, the retail personnel will also need to manage the operations and business in addition to providing healthcare advice.

Through this course, participants will gain an overview to the challenges of managing the pharmaceutical products with short shelf lives and solutions provided by the inventory management systems. Participants will be able to apply tactics used in retail pharmacies and hospital pharmacies to manage the inventory using digital tools to reduce revenue loss due to expiration.

• Acquire an analytical thinking mindset.
• Understand the foundation of data, data analysis and data related concepts.
• Clarify the difference between analytics and benchmarking of best practices/metrics tracking.
• Understand the need for data capture in healthcare analytics and develop effective data capture techniques (what to collect, how to collect/format of collection, access rights and cleaning of data etc).
• Undertake descriptive and predictive analytics within the healthcare industry.
• Understand various kinds of outcomes in cost-outcome analysis.
• Undertake the analysis of predictive models and basic cost-benefit analysis of interventions in healthcare (shorter hospital stays, reduced re-admissions etc).

• Conceptualize and propose a collaborative working model to share/exchange information between healthcare providers and insurers with strong safeguards to protect patient/customer privacy.
• Foster a culture of collaboration and innovation that will drive better/efficient care and lower costs for patients/customers.

Course Title: Graduate Certificate in Digital Health Module 3: Analytics for Healthcare (Advanced Level)
Course Description:
At the end of the module, participants will be able to:
• Employ data more effectively for healthcare decision making such as patient experiences, costs and benefits of healthcare interventions etc.
• Undertake various types of analyses for describing what is happening, predicting what would happen with different strategies and prescribing the next course of actions.
• Generate insights across functional areas such as patient care & experience and human capital management practices (such as qualities of healthcare professionals that are most suited for various patient groups/right persons to right jobs etc) (Note: Various analytical software will be used). 
• Presentation of insights to healthcare stakeholders effectively.
• Address compliance to healthcare data security.

Course Title: Modular Certificate in Applied Pathophysiology & Pharmacology (part of Diploma in Health Sciences (Paramedicine))
Course Description:
The course aims to deepen students’ knowledge of medical sciences and research to enable greater understanding of paramedic treatment protocols, so as to better manage complex medical emergencies. The students are equipped to apply relevant clinical skills in the management of patients in paramedic practice.

The course will also equip them with transferable skills such as communication, leadership, logistics and crisis management, to improve operational effectiveness and work efficiency. At the completion of this diploma, the participant will be able to:
1.Critically evaluate patients and deliver care according to patient assessment model (PAM) and according to local pre-hospital emergency care protocols.
2.Evaluate and document with accuracy, patient care and outcomes.
3.Function as independent crew leaders in the ambulance using their expertise in emergency medical skills.

Course Title: Design Thinking for Healthcare Innovation: Focusing on Identifying and Inventing for the Unmet Need
Course Description:
By the end of this module, you should be able to

-Demonstrate ability to apply design thinking and needs analysis to unmet healthcare/clinical needs.
-Articulate the necessary needs research by drafting a preliminary needs specification document
-Apply ideation strategies to brainstorm possible healthcare solutions and where it would meet gaps in the patient/user journey 
-Screen concepts using relevant parameters pertinent to the innovation. 
-Assess technology to design and develop functional proof-of-concept prototypes

Course Title: Design Thinking for Healthcare Innovation: An Overview
Course Description:
By the end of this module, you should be able to
-Demonstrates ability to apply design thinking and needs analysis to unmet healthcare/clinical needs.
-Understand the importance of a well-crafted user needs specification and design a preliminary user needs specification document.
-Apply ideation strategies to brainstorm possible healthcare solutions and where it would meet gaps in the patient/user journey 
-Recognize pertinent nuances towards health and medtech innovation that are unique to this specialty.

Course Title: Principles and Applications of Technology Enhance Learning (Synchronous and Asynchronous e-learning)
Course Description: Serves as the foundation for teaching and learning with technology. The module will provide students with the theoretical foundation and considereation that is important when designing courses with technology. Students will need to complete this course before proceeding with other advanced TEL modules that will be offered as part of this program. This will be the first time a course on the use of technology for teaching and learning will be offered by the school.

Course Title: Business Model Innovation for Healthcare Innovations
Course Description:
By the end of this module, you should be able to
Construct and execute basic customer and market survey for concept or product validation and to articulate key value proposition
Analyze market positioning by conducting in-depth market landscaping (competitive landscape distribution channel, existing models) to develop a business plan with clear value proposition
Examine and articulate key stakeholders along the value chain and the jobs to be done
Prepare a preliminary market analysis (top-down and bottom up)
Apply understanding of  healthcare value chain to elucidate identify the key stakeholders and factors influencing commercial viability of a product in target market (e.g., reimbursement infrastructures, key decision makers) and/or adoption into healthcare system
Articulate value proposition in relation to initial pricing and business model

Course Title: An Introduction to Product Development and Quality Management Systems for Health/MedTech Innovations
Course Description:
By the end of this module, you should be able to

-Analyse and articulate key design requirement specifications
-Assess technology to design and develop functional proof-of-concept prototypes, and determine what tests are required to de-risk the project 
-Respond and manage potential product development risks, develop a mitigation plan and manage risks
-Collaborates with different internal stakeholders (e.g., engineers and  scientists) to define product requirements, develop prototype and  validate results
-Understands the required  regulatory and quality requirements, i.e., guidance documents, standards for different kinds of innovation projects; Able to design and carry out necessary tests and documentation.
-Apply basic quality management standards to ensure adherence to ISO standards in the early product development stages

Course Title: Business Implementation for Health/MedTech: Project to Market Overview
Course Description:
By the end of this module, you should be able to
Business Planning
-Analyze market positioning by conducting in-depth market landscaping (competitive landscape distribution channel, existing models) to develop a business plan with clear value proposition 
-Demonstrates confidence in defending and pitching the business, networking and basic negotiation skill to initiate investment and partnership discussions
Market Access Competency 
-Apply understanding of healthcare value chain to elucidate identify the key stakeholders and factors influencing commercial viability of a product in target market (e.g., reimbursement infrastructures, key decision makers) and/or adoption into healthcare system 
-Apply principles of coding, coverage and payment. 
-Evaluate and analyse pricing and reimbursement schemes and healthcare economics across key target markets 
-Examine key channels in key target markets (e.g., public hospitals and clinics via tender and group procurement, private hospitals via private healthcare insurance and private procurement) 
-Prepare a preliminary health technology assessment for quick compare and contrast to identify key target markets and value proposition to enter 
-Derive initial pricing model and test deployment 
Intellectual Property Competency
-Define the inventive  concept and analyze its  positioning to determine its strength and weaknesses to develop a good  competitive IP advantage and filing strategy 
Regulatory
-Understands the required  regulatory and quality requirements, i.e., guidance documents, standards for different kinds of innovation projects; Able to design and carry out necessary tests and documentation.
Clinical Trial Planning 
-Analyze different types of clinical trial strategies to meet clinical trial objectives
Operational Planning
-Explain the operational aspects and financial aspects required for a health and medtech company to function, including processes, people, resources, budget

Course Title: Patient Data Security and Privacy
Course Description:
This course will enable participants to:

1.  Understand the differences between privacy, confidentiality, information privacy, and information security and the relationships among them.
2. Discuss how information system security (or cybersecurity) affects privacy, confidentiality, and security.
3. Recognize potential threats to cybersecurity.
4. Identify several security measures designed to protect information and discuss how they function.
5. Discuss the impact that Internet technology has on the security of health-related information.

Course Title: Regulation of Biotherapeutics and Biosimilars (Classroom & Asynchronous e-learning)
Course Description: This course will align participants with the good practices and standards in managing biotherapeutics and biosimilars. At the end of the course, participants will be able to describe the regulatory requirements at various development stages of the biologics, as well as articulate the differences in managing original biotherapeutics and the generic biologics. Through practicals, participants will gain basic skills in the assessment of dossier materials relating to the clinical data, benefit-risk assessment and post-market monitoring of these innovations.

Course Title: Antimicrobial resistance and drug discovery
Course Description: Antimicrobial resistance presents a critical threat to public health and medicine, projected to account for ten million annual death and a costing a GDP loss of US$100 trillion by 2050. This module which addresses both antimicrobial resistance and drug discovery, is essential in preparing young scientists to address these issues. Lectures on emerging medical technologies are addressed, including principles on mechanisms of action and resistance of antimicrobials as well as the process of drug discovery in Pharma, Biotech and Academia. Topics in drug discovery include conventional conventional screening of small molecules as well as antibody and immunotherapy, CAR-T cell therapy, phage therapy and microbiome based-therapy. Examples using bacteria, parasites and viruses are included.

Course Title: Fundraising Fundamentals for Healthcare Innovations(Synchronous & Asynchronous)
Course Description:
At the end of this course, participants are expected to
- Develop a broad understanding of the fundraising landscape (angels, VCs, family offices, incubators/accelerators) for HealthTech innovations
- Get familiarized with fundraising terms and learn how to prepare financial and fundraising plans 
- Understand the psyche of investors and use this knowledge to best position their HealthTech innovations to get successful investments
- Gain perspectives of investors vs founders/companies in getting a term sheet on the table and negotiation approaches towards closing deals

Course Title: Post Diploma Certificate in Applied Sciences in Advanced Nursing - Part of Advanced Diploma in Perioperative Nursing (ADPO1)
Course Description: The certificate equips the students with comprehensive knowledge and skills required for better understanding of individual patient journey across setting-based and population-based health care. The modules covered are Health Assessment and Clinical Decision Making, Applied Pathophysiology and Pharmacology, Evidence-Based Practice, and Care Management across Life Span. The modules aim to develop the students to be more adapt at managing the complexity of patients related to ageing, common acute and chronic diseases, common psychosocial issues and preventive care.

Course Title: Post Diploma Certificate in Essential Clinical Knowledge in Palliative Care - Part of Specialist Diploma in Palliative Care Nursing (SDPCN)
Course Description: This PDC provides comprehensive education on cancer biology, diagnosis, and treatment, including clinical trials and specific common cancers, as well as pain and symptom management in life-limiting illnesses like cancer. It also addresses the role of palliative care in non-cancer illnesses, focusing on end-stage organ failure. Additionally, the program enhances communication and psychosocial skills essential for oncology and palliative care nurses, emphasizing psychosocial interventions and support strategies.

Course Title: Post Diploma Certificate in Essential Clinical Skills in Palliative Care - Part of Specialist Diploma in Palliative Care Nursing (SDPCN)
Course Description: This PDC equips students with skills in history taking, physical assessment, and interpreting lab results, as well as managing oncology emergencies and treatment-related symptoms. It refines clinical nursing skills for providing comfort to terminally ill patients and explores complementary therapies commonly used locally. The program also includes clinical consolidation, emphasizing therapeutic communication, counseling, and ethical decision-making in end-of-life care.

Course Title: Post Diploma Certificate in Clinical Practicum in Advanced Nursing - Part of Advanced Diploma in Perioperative Nursing (ADPO1)
Course Description: The certificate provides students with learning opportunities to apply and synthesize the knowledge and practical skills they learnt in the Post-Diploma Certificate 1 (Post-Diploma Certificate in Applied Sciences in Advanced Nursing) when they provide care to the patients, and support to their families in 2-week cross-training clinical postings.

Course Title: Post Diploma Certificate in Advanced Clinical Practicum in Perioperative Nursing - Part of Advanced Diploma in Perioperative Nursing (ADPO1)
Course Description: The certificate equips students with skills to assist in surgical procedures, and apply the nursing process in the management of patient undergoing surgery. The students will learn to apply clinical reasoning and decision-making skills for a patient, and implement measures to maintain the physical, microbiological and radiation safety of the patient undergoing surgery.

Course Title: Post Diploma Certificate in Clinical Practicum in Advanced Nursing - Part of Advanced Diploma in Nursing (Chronic Disease) (ADNC1)
Course Description: The certificate provides students with learning opportunities to apply and synthesize the knowledge and practical skills they learnt in the Post-Diploma Certificate 1 (Post-Diploma Certificate in Applied Sciences in Advanced Nursing) when they provide care to the patients, and support to their families in 2-week cross-training clinical postings.

Course Title: Post Diploma Certificate in Applied Sciences in Advanced Nursing - Part of Advanced Diploma in Nursing (Chronic Disease) (ADNC1)
Course Description: The certificate equips the students with comprehensive knowledge and skills required for better understanding of individual patient journey across setting-based and population-based health care. The modules covered are Health Assessment and Clinical Decision Making, Applied Pathophysiology and Pharmacology, Evidence-Based Practice, and Care Management across Life Span. The modules aim to develop the students to be more adapt at managing the complexity of patients related to ageing, common acute and chronic diseases, common psychosocial issues and preventive care.

Course Title: Post Diploma Certificate in Advanced Chronic Disease Nursing - Part of Advanced Diploma in Nursing (Chronic Disease) (ADNC1)
Course Description: The certificate equips students with the knowledge and skills on holistic care of patient with chronic diseases in a multidisciplinary approach. The use of person-centred therapy, motivational interviewing, cognitive behavioural therapy, and health education strategies to change the healthcare behaviours of clients will be emphasized. In-depth knowledge to the key concepts, interventions and principles of case management, rehabilitation and palliative care in nursing practice across the continuum will be covered.

Course Title: Post Diploma Certificate in Advanced Clinical Practicum in Chronic Disease Nursing - Part of Advanced Diploma in Nursing (Chronic Disease) (ADNC1)
Course Description: The certificate equips students with the opportunities to provide direct patient or family care and gain clinical experience in diverse health care settings. Clinical attachments include specialised areas in training such as case or care management in the primary health care, acute to home, community and palliative care facilities with interdisciplinary experience in caring of patients with chronic and life-limiting conditions.

Course Title: Specialist Diploma in Palliative Care Nursing (SDPCN)
Course Description: The course aims to broadens and deepens the knowledge and skills of the in-service Registered Nurses (RNs) in palliative care setting. By completing the SDPCN course, RNs will gain the necessary expertise to provide quality and coordinated care to the end-of-life or terminally ill patients.

Course Description:
At the end of this module, participants will be able to:
• Understand their duties, obligations and liabilities based on both law and ethics.
• Examine/question the kind of data their institutions are collecting, why they need it and if they have a right to collect and use it the way they are using.
• Assess the risks involved in the deployment of digital health solutions.
• Develop a risk management framework to anticipate/transfer/mitigate the risks involved.

- undertake the analysis of predictive models and basic cost-benefit analysis of interventions in healthcare (shorter hospital stays, reduced re-admissions etc).

Course Title: Graduate Certificate in Digital Health Module 3: Analytics for Healthcare (Advanced Level) (Synchronous E-Learning)

- undertake various types of analyses for describing what is happening, predicting what would happen with different strategies and prescribing the next course of actions. (Note: Participants are expected to achieve and will be assessed for competency in a wide range of analytical methods)
- generate insights across functional areas such as patient care and experience and human capital management practices (such as qualities of healthcare professionals that are most suited for various patient groups/right persons to right jobs etc). (Note: Various analytical software will be used) 
- present insights to healthcare stakeholders effectively.

Course Title: The Collaborative Practitioners Prescribing Programme
Course Description:
The specific objectives are to equip the nurses and pharmacists with skills and knowledge to
•	Assess patients and perform the diagnostic phase of care, and
•	Prescribe pharmacological and non-pharmacological treatment and perform the therapeutic phase of care independently 
in the context of team-based care that promotes care accessibility and affordability, and under the governance of institution-based Collaborative Practice Agreements. 

The instructional methods in The Collaborative Practitioners Prescribing Programme (CP3) are predicated on adult learning theories to ensure an appropriate balance of cognitive knowledge and hands-on clinical skills practice that are relevant to the APNs and pharmacists. The programme of learning uses a hybrid of delivery methods: 
•	Lectures and case discussions via teleconferencing that enhance convenience and efficiency without compromising quality of learning, 
•	Medical interview and communication skills practice with standardised patients (aka role-players) via teleconferencing and face-to-face in classroom with immediate feedback under the guidance of experienced facilitators, and
•	Physical examination practice with standardised patients and real patients face-to-face in classroom with immediate feedback under the guidance of experienced facilitators. 
The lecturers and facilitators are doctors, senior pharmacists, and APNs, who are knowledgeable and nurturing in the way they guide the trainees.

Course Title: Post-market vigilance: Regulatory activities for monitoring In Vitro Diagnostic Devices (IVD) and Software as Medical Devices (SaMD)

Key recommendations from the International Medical Device Regulators Forum (IMDRF) will form the foundational understanding, in addition to requirements from major reference regulators and the regional ASEAN directive. With an insight into the interactions among key regulatory organisations, participants will equip themselves with knowledge of the good practices in post-market surveillance.

Course Title: Holistic Care for School-Aged Children
Course Description:
"Knowledge and Insight (Understanding)
• GP’s role in prevention and early identification of common learning, emotional, behavioural and health problems in school-going children, and how this role complements our school health service and specialist care
• Typical social emotional development in children, the concept of resilience, and how parents, teachers, our community & society can help to develop these skills
• Expected literacy and numeracy skills in school children
• Up-to-date epidemiology of common learning, emotional, behavioural and health problems in school-going children, and how this impacts epidemiology of chronic diseases in our future adult population, and changes public health priorities (The concept of “Healthy Weight, Healthy Children; Healthy Child, Brighter Future”)
• Up-to-date local epidemiology of adverse childhood events (ACEs) and non-accidental injury, and associated biopsychosocial, emotional and behavioural issues in children; governmental, community and social support/resources
Biopsychosocial challenges in lifestyle modification in school-going children and their families; how socioeconomic status contributes to change sustainability & stages of behavioural change

Application of Knowledge & Insight
• With the Touchpoints approach (Module GCP1004), continue to utilize opportunistic touchpoints to screen for learning, emotional, behavioural and health issues as the pre-schooler advances into primary school education

Appraisal (formation of a judgement)
• School readiness for both parent and child:
o Cognitive ability of child, child’s adaptive skills (e.g. independence in activities, social skills, communication skills), behaviours and attention span, self-management
o School readiness beyond academics; awareness of local educational pathway and Compulsory Education Act (including when and who to refer to) 
o How to enable and empower parents to prepare their children for school
• Lifestyle-related health issues in sch

Course Title: Adolescent Health
Course Description:
"Knowledge and Insight (Understanding)
• Erikson’s psychosocial tasks for adolescents: identity formation versus confusion; relations with parents, peers, partners.
• Biopsychosocial approach to sexuality and sexual health. 
• Biopsychology of sexual orientation versus gender dysphoria.
• Biopsychosocial developmental effects of chronic conditions on adolescents, including effects on autonomy and compliance.


Application of Knowledge & Insight
• Psychosocial assessment using H.E.A.D.S.S.(home, education, activities/employment, drugs, sexuality, and suicide’) framework (used in ideal settings versus time-pressured busy clinic settings). 
• Sleep physiology, sleep hygiene and identifying risks for sleep disorders.
• Mental health screening: spirituality, moods, anxiety, stress, bullying/cyberbullying, depression, suicidal threats, including maladaptation, coping mechanisms and resilience building.  
• Addiction screening: gaming, social media, internet, smoking, alcohol, substance use, other risk-taking behaviours.

Appraisal (formation of a judgement)
• Growth and development: pubertal stages, early mid to late adolescence.
• Normal versus abnormal behaviours: adolescent adjustment reactions versus behavioural problems; oppositional defiant disorders, conduct issues.
• Learning issues: dyslexia, attention deficit hyperactivity disorders, autistic spectrum disorders.
• Eating disorders: normal versus abnormal dieting. 
• The pregnant teen.
• Contraception, sexually transmitted infections (STI)screening and management. 
• Skin problems such as acne, and related psychosocial effects.
Functional pains (psychosomatisation), identifying red flags for organic conditions, including heavy menstruation bleeding.

Communication Skills
• Youth friendly consultation: confidentiality, ethics, medico-legal.
• Motivational interviewing: strategies to facilitate adolescent behaviour change.
• Anticipatory guidance for adolescents (for parents/ caregivers)."

Course Title: Biomaterials
Course Description:
Biomaterials is a topic that cuts across many disciplines and gains more importance, especially in the era of pandemic. The growth of healthcare industries in recent years will bring about higher demand for talents and professionals with related skills in biomedical field, including biomaterials. Therefore, it is timely to offer this course to learners of any age who wish to pick up new knowledge and explore the exciting world of biomaterials.

This course will introduce students to the interdisciplinary field of biomaterials and their use in healthcare. In particular, students will learn about how host responds to biomaterials when they are implanted in the body and the possible complications that may arise. Various in vitro and in vivo compatibility tests will be introduced too. Finally, students will delve into some key applications of metallic and ceramic biomaterials as hard tissue replacements / supports.

Course Title: Implementing Interventions in Health and Social Care (Synchronous e-learning)
Course Description:
Singapore’s healthcare system is constantly adapting to changing external environment, which is compounded by our aging population, increasing prevalence of co-morbidities, increasing demand of hospital services and limitations in resources. Our healthcare expenditure is set to continue to increase, largely due to the demand for additional facilities and the increasing operating cost. There is a need to integrate care across the health care system and between the healthcare and social care systems.

However, workers in the field of care integration are hampered by a lack of recognition, training and the resources needed to support their area of work. Current training systems are still dominated by pre-employment training (PET) principles and healthcare workers tend to work in their respective silos. The competency needed for the tasks of integrating health and social care is not well defined. The link between training and work is not well established in care integration.

This course aims to specially address the need to enhance care integration competency in the following areas:
• Knowledge of how our healthcare system works 
• Understanding health and social needs of patients
• Systemic approach to co-develop health and social care plans 
• Communication skills
• Collaboration skills with partners

This course equips learners with competencies to work in an emerging area of integrating health and social care to work in acute & community hospitals, nursing homes, senior activity centres, family service centres, adult disability homes, day activity centres, etc. The graduate profile of this program will be a client-centred care coordinator who is able to assess clients’ needs, plan interventions and support clients towards improving their wellbeing.

Course Title: Classic TCM prescription for clinical application series: Exogenous types [For registered TCM practitioners only]
Course Description:
[TCM practitioners should confirm with the training provider to ensure that the course is accredited CPE points by TCMPB before signing up.]
Learn how to properly use classic TCM prescription in clinical practice such as Xiao Qing Long Tang, San Ren Tang, Ding Chuan Tang, Fang Feng Tong Shen San, Cang Er Zi San, Gan Lu Xiao Du Dan.

Course Title: Mood Disorders and Acupuncture Treatment [For registered TCM practitioners only]
Course Description:
[TCM practitioners should confirm with the training provider to ensure that the course is accredited CPE points by TCMPB before signing up.]
Mood disorders in Traditional Chinese Medicine are mainly parallel to mental and psychological diseases in Western Medicine which includes depression, anxiety, obsessive-compulsive disorder, bipolar disorder, insomnia, and irritable bowel syndrome etc. Acupuncture can be useful as an adjuvant treatment in managing such disorders. With the development of theories in brain diseases in Traditional Chinese Medicine, many have started to use scalp acupuncture and the meridians (e.g. the governor vessel) to manage mood disorder conditions.

This course will focus on discussing and introducing the combination of the five regions of scalp acupuncture on the head and the thirteen 'ghost' acupoints from both aspects of Chinese and Western medicine to manage common mood disorders, such as depression, anxiety, obsessive-compulsive disorder, insomnia, bipolar disorder, alcohol addiction, irritable bowel syndrome etc. The course will cover scalp acupuncture stimulation points, anatomical characteristics of scalp acupuncture points, scalp acupuncture positioning techniques, acupoints combination characteristics, precautions, and combination with other acupuncture methods. Together with the analysis of real life clinical cases,  this course will take you in depth in the management of common mood disorders.

Course Title: Nervous System Disorders and Scalp Acupuncture [For registered TCM practitioners only]
Course Description:
[TCM practitioners should confirm with the training provider to ensure that the course is accredited CPE points by TCMPB before signing up.]
Nervous system disorders are one of the more common disorders managed using acupuncture, especially central nervous system disorders, which can be difficult to manage and may affect the quality of life of patients and their families. Scalp acupuncture and Du-mai acupuncture are acupuncture methods that have proven to be effective in treating nervous system disorders, with credible research results.

This course will provide a detailed explanation of the common nervous system disorders such as stroke, Parkinson's disease, Alzheimer's disease, multiple sclerosis, pediatric cerebral palsy etc, from both Chinese and Western medicine perspective. The course will also deep dive into the Scalp acupuncture and Dumai acupuncture methods, the specific positioning of the acu-points, scalp acupuncture techniques and precautions.

Course Title: Classic TCM prescription for clinical application series: Chaihu types [For registered TCM practitioners only]
Course Description:
[TCM practitioners should confirm with the training provider to ensure that the course is accredited CPE points by TCMPB before signing up.]
Learn how to properly use classic TCM prescription in clinical practice such as Xiao Chai Hu Tang, Yue Ju Wan and Ban Xia Xie Xin Tang.

Course Title: Classic TCM prescription for clinical application series: Gastrointestinal types [For registered TCM practitioners only]
Course Description:
[TCM practitioners should confirm with the training provider to ensure that the course is accredited CPE points by TCMPB before signing up.]
Learn how to properly use classic TCM prescription in clinical practice such as Li Zhong Wan, Bao He Wan, Si Jun Zi Tang.

Course Title: Diagnosis and Management of Thyroid Diseases in Traditional Chinese Medicine [For registered TCM practitioners only]
Course Description:
[TCM practitioners should confirm with the training provider to ensure that the course is accredited CPE points by TCMPB before signing up.]
This five-part course offers an in-depth exploration of Traditional Chinese Medicine (TCM) approaches to diagnosing and managing common thyroid conditions. Delivered by renowned expert Professor Wu Min, the series is designed for TCM practitioners and healthcare professionals interested in integrating TCM perspectives into endocrine health management.

Course Title: Dong Yuan Theory System and its TCM Clinical Application Series [For registered TCM practitioners only]
Course Description:
[TCM practitioners should confirm with the training provider to ensure that the course is accredited CPE points by TCMPB before signing up.]
Dong Yuan Theory System is an independent and complete system of Traditional Chinese Medicine, which has the same depth and integrity as the Six Classics. Dongyuan research professor Gao Jianzhong to teach a series of Dongyuan Theory System and Clinical Application, and guide you to understand how the two TCM masters, Zhang Zhong Jing and Li Dong Yuan, comprehend the core of diagnosis and prescription method of the Six Classics of Treatise Febrile and Internal Injury Theory.

Course Title: TCM Andrology Series Courses [For registered TCM practitioners only]
Course Description:
[TCM practitioners should confirm with the training provider to ensure that the course is accredited CPE points by TCMPB before signing up.]
In this course, trainees will learn the overview, etiology and pathogenesis of common Andrology conditions such as male infertility, prostate issues such as chronic prostatitis and benign prostatic hyperplasia. Learn about the diagnosis and differentiation of such common Andrology conditions and understand the management with TCM and Western medicine.

Course Title: Meridian Palpation Differentiation: Beginner Course [For registered TCM practitioners only]
Course Description:
[TCM practitioners should confirm with the training provider to ensure that the course is accredited CPE points by TCMPB before signing up.]
Meridian Palpation Differentiation is a comprehensive method created by Professor Ye Liuzhong with the combination of years of clinical experience and based on the records in the Yellow Emperor's Internal Classic, Treatise on Febrile Diseases and other classic medical books. It uses modern classification assessment, sensitization point and other theories and techniques of diagnosis and treatment norms.

Meridian Palpation Differentiation covers the selection of meridians, evaluation of acupoints, the three-dimensional integrated diagnostic system of inquiry, tongue, and meridian diagnosis. Treatment techniques includes regulating yin-yang, qi and shen, and guidelines for medication prescription selection.

Meridian Palpation Differentiation is precise, quick and a simple method to apply into your clinical practice and it is very popular method used in European countries such as Germany and Switzerland.

Course Title: Meridian Palpation Differentiation: Intermediate Course [For registered TCM practitioners only]
Course Description:
[TCM practitioners should confirm with the training provider to ensure that the course is accredited CPE points by TCMPB before signing up.]
This Meridian Palpation Differentiation Intermediate course by Lecturer Ye Liuzhong is a direct continuation from Meridian Palpation Differentiation Basic Course, and will focus more on the differentiation diagnosis of different diseases. 

This course will be steered by real life case studies to explain eight common clinical diseases such as pain, skin diseases, auto-immune diseases, gastrointestinal diseases, and endocrine diseases. At the same time, it will guide students to further understand and master meridian palpation differentiation diagnosis in the management of diseases.

Course Title: TCM Shen Shi Nu Ke Theory in 100 parts [For registered TCM practitioners only]
Course Description:
[TCM practitioners should confirm with the training provider to ensure that the course is accredited CPE points by TCMPB before signing up.]

Course Title: Professor Wang San Hu Classic TCM clinical application on TCM Oncology Series [For registered TCM practitioners only]
Course Description:
[TCM practitioners should confirm with the training provider to ensure that the course is accredited CPE points by TCMPB before signing up.]
In this course, participants will learn how Professor Wang San Hu utilises classical TCM prescriptions on cancer management. Learn about the etiology and pathogenesis of various cancers and the characteristics of syndrome differentiation. Learn how to flexibly manage various cancer patients with the combination of addition and subtraction of classical TCM prescription using the basis of syndrome differentiation.

Course Title: Six Meridian Diagnosis Differentiation and its Clinical Application [For registered TCM practitioners only]
Course Description:
[TCM practitioners should confirm with the training provider to ensure that the course is accredited CPE points by TCMPB before signing up.]
Learn how to properly diagnose and apply the Six Meridian Diagnostic tool in your clinical work. Lecturer Bao Yan Ju will teach about the Zhong Yi Kan Bing San Ban Fu, an exclusive methodology to upgrade your clinical skills.

Course Title: 10x10International Course on Health Informatics
