Course Title: (eCornell) Addressing Healthcare Economics
Course Description: In this course, Dr. Robert Karpman, a seasoned medical professional, will guide you through must-know information when it comes to surveying the financial and economic opportunities within your organization. Much of the course will focus on becoming more profitable by reducing costs, evaluating contracts, improving processes, and maximizing reimbursements. In the course project, you will apply best practices and personal experience to real-world scenarios. You will walk away from this course equipped to make economically sound decisions in your organization while also keeping your patients' safety and care standards at the forefront. It is recommended to only take this course if you have completed Managing People in a Healthcare Setting, Assessing Your Organization's Finances, and Planning Healthcare Investments and Marketing or have equivalent experience. The course is provided by eCornell in partnership with Genashtim.

Course Title: (eCornell) Design Consideration for Senior Facilities
Course Description: In this course, you will examine how psychological factors affect our relationship to the environment in senior living facilities and reflect on how well those factors are being applied in a real-life setting. You'll explore how to apply environmental psychology principles to the design of senior living facilities. You'll conduct an online literature review to help inform your design of senior living facilities. To help you better understand the challenges seniors face so that you can design to address those challenges, you'll participate in a hands-on exercise where you simulate the physical limitations that occur naturally in the aging process and then reflect on your experience. The course is provided by eCornell in partnership with Genashtim.

Course Title: (eCornell) Dining Innovation in Senior Living
Course Description: In this course, you'll examine a framework to help you align your food service operation with your organizational needs. You'll also examine trends and how they might have a place in your food service operation, and you'll access resources to help you keep on top of those trends. You'll review a healthcare facility menu and reflect on what's good and where there's room for improvement. You'll design, or re-design, your own menu and then build a plan to collect and analyze feedback on that menu. You'll also interact with a tool to help you make sure your concept is viable. The course is provided by eCornell in partnership with Genashtim.

Course Title: (eCornell) Effective Process Management in Senior Living
Course Description: In this course, you'll explore the concept of process thinking and access several reusable tools to help you evaluate, develop, and improve processes for an organization. You'll examine how to spot what's wrong in a process and explore solutions to fix those problems. You will also monitor how long a process takes at your own organization and reflect on the results of your observations. You'll examine a process flow at your organization and explore ideas for how to improve the process. You'll participate in a hands-on activity to examine how to improve the wait time of a process at your organization. Finally, you'll write a plan for how process flow can help improve decision-making at your organization. The course is provided by eCornell in partnership with Genashtim.

Course Title: (eCornell) Eliciting New Behaviors
Course Description: In this course, you will use a variety of tools and techniques to help clients set achievable goals and stay motivated. You will identify the top techniques and tips that are specifically applicable to your style of counseling or that can best help you grow as a communicator in your field, and use them to compile a resource for future practice. You will practice applying empathy in order to find out what truly matters to your clients and apply that valuable information to goal setting. You will conduct a motivational interviewing session with a colleague, or friend to practice your skills in a real-life setting, reflect on what you did well and what you would change, and receive helpful feedback from the course facilitator. Finally, you will use five best practices to create your own script for a conversation in which you help a client set a realistic and actionable goal. The course is provided by eCornell in partnership with Genashtim.

Course Title: (eCornell) Navigating the Healthcare Regulatory Environment
Course Description: With a practical focus, this course outlines an approach designed to help you understand and comply with the essential requirements of regulatory agencies. The course begins with a look at regulations and the government bodies that issue them. It moves on to discuss the role of compliance officers within an organization and the steps an organization can take to ensure regulatory compliance. The course concludes with a discussion of healthcare liability and how organizations can reduce their exposure, routinely as well as in the case of medical error. The four-part course project helps you apply what you learn to your own situation, and a set of templates and downloadable tools provides resources designed to assist you as you implement key concepts in the course. It is recommended to only take this course if you have completed Managing People in a Healthcare Setting, Assessing Your Organization's Finances, Planning Healthcare Investments and Marketing, Addressing Healthcare Economics, Guiding Your Organization's Costs and Budgets, and Improving Quality and Performance of Healthcare Services or have equivalent experience. The course is provided by eCornell in partnership with Genashtim.

Course Title: (eCornell) Planning Healthcare Investments and Marketing
Course Description: In this course, Dr. Robert Karpman, a surgeon and entrepreneur, leads you through the processes used in cost-benefit and cost-effectiveness analyses. You will learn about return on investment (ROI), risk, the role of inflation, and how to compare investment opportunities against the returns expected from a simple cash investment. You will also become well-versed in healthcare marketing, another important form of investment. Included in this course is a five-part course project which will guide you in applying what you learned to your own career. You will also gain a set of tools and resources you can download for future use. It is recommended to only take this course if you have completed Managing People in a Healthcare Setting and Assessing Your Organization's Finances or have equivalent experience.The course is provided by eCornell in partnership with Genashtim

Course Title: (eCornell) Promoting Organizational Wellness
Course Description: This course allows you to further develop your counseling skills, explore wellness for groups and organizations, and solidify a plan for your future. You will create a plan to expand your wellness counseling skills and integrate them with your current area of expertise. You will then expand your counseling skills to support group facilitation. You will practice these skills by engaging with a colleague, or friend outside of the course. Finally, you will create a wellness vision and plan how you will communicate your vision to organizations and employees. By the end of this course, you will be prepared to use your counseling skills to work within organizations focused on improving the health and wellness of their employees. The course is provided by eCornell in partnership with Genashtim.

Course Title: (eCornell) Quality and Process Improvement for Senior Living
Course Description: Throughout the course, you will identify a process in need of improvement at your organization and reflect on how it could be improved. You'll access several tools to help you understand the source of a problem with a process and use those tools to assess an issue in your organization. As your course concludes, you will explore strategies for addressing quality issues and access a reusable tool to help remind you of best practices for improving a process. The course is provided by eCornell in partnership with Genashtim.

Course Title: (eCornell) Senior Living Fundamentals
Course Description: In this course, you will assess the senior living landscape and identify senior living workplace challenges as well as the solutions to those challenges. You will also explore best practices for facilitating service excellence. Finally, you will prepare for future trends and emerging complexities in senior living. In your course project, you will select a senior living option you work at or one that you believe is best aligned with your personal career interests. The course is provided by eCornell in partnership with Genashtim.

Course Title: (eCornell) Understanding the Deeper Need
Course Description: In this course, you will practice techniques to elicit information from clients, uncover their deeper needs and in turn help them set better goals. You will conduct a session with a colleague, or friend to practice your client-directed counseling skills in a real-life setting, gather feedback from your partner, and then reflect on what you did well and what you would change. You will describe how the use (or lack) of continuing responses in a conversation you've had in the past has affected trust within that conversation, and you will make a plan to better integrate continuing responses into future conversations. You will observe and evaluate another counselor's use of client-centered techniques to elicit information. While identifying and overcoming challenges in implementing client-centered counseling techniques, you will create an action plan to help clients set goals that align with their true needs. At the end of this course, you will be poised to apply new techniques to better identify the client's ?need behind the need. The course is provided by eCornell in partnership with Genashtim.

Course Title: (eCornell) Understanding the Person
Course Description: In this course, you will learn how client-directed counseling can be effectively blended into a variety of fields where behavioral change is needed. You will reflect on a listening experience and articulate the benefits of client-directed counseling. With a colleague, or friend, you will practice the effective use of silence in a counseling setting. You will also practice interpreting clients' nonverbal cues. You will create a plan to integrate self-management into your sessions, and finally, you will outline research-based principles and techniques to use in your practice. At the end of this course, you will be positioned to use research-based techniques to create rapport, build trust, elicit useful information from clients, and enhance their effectiveness, along with the success of the groups you serve. The course is provided by eCornell in partnership with Genashtim.

Course Title: (eCornell)How to Build a Strategic Plan for your Healthcare Organization
Course Description: This course will equip you with the tools to be able to develop a comprehensive strategic plan that involves the right stakeholders and that aligns with your organization's core mission and values. The course is provided by eCornell in partnership with Genashtim.

Post-Diploma Certificate in Advanced Concepts in Perianaesthesia Nursing (PDC3 of Advanced Diploma in Nursing (Perianaesthesia)) aims to prepare the learners on the following areas:
1. knowledge and skills for effective management of chronic diseases for adults in the primary, acute and intermediate long term care (ILTC) settings; relate the relevant care management concepts to the children population.
2. knowledge and skills to adopt systematic approaches to critical appraisal and synthesis of research evidence; to apply the knowledge and skills acquired to complete a critical appraisal of a selected clinical question or topic of interest and identify clear recommendations for practice with supporting level of evidences.
3. knowledge and skills in post anaesthesia care of all categories of clients in Post Anaesthesia Care Unit (PACU)
4. knowledge and skills in crisis management in anaesthesia.

Course Title: Post-Diploma Certificate in Advanced Concepts in Nephro-Urology Nursing (part of Advanced Diploma in Nursing (Nephro-Urology))
Course Description:
Post-Diploma Certificate in Advanced Concepts in Nephro-Urology Nursing (PDC3 of Advanced Diploma in Nursing (Nephro-Urology)) aims to prepare the learners in the following:

1.  acquire knowledge and skills for effective multidisciplinary management of chronic diseases for adults in the primary, acute and intermediate long-term care (ILTC) settings.
2.  examine the importance of evidence-based practice (EBP) and the key influencing factors on the adoption of EBP in healthcare and nursing. 
3.  in-depth study of the current trends in management of clients with acute kidney injury (AKI), chronic kidney disease (CKD) and its related co-morbidities. 
4.  in-depth study of cancers of the urinary tract and their current management. Management of clients requiring renal transplantation.

Course Title: Post-Diploma Certificate in Advanced Concepts in Medical-Surgical Nursing (part of Advanced Diploma in Nursing (Medical-Surgical))
Course Description:
Post-Diploma Certificate in Advanced Concepts in Medical-Surgical Nursing (PDC3 of Advanced Diploma in Nursing (Medical-Surgical) aims to prepare the learners on the following:

1.  acquire knowledge and skills for effective multidisciplinary management of chronic diseases for adults in the primary, acute and intermediate long-term care (ILTC) settings. 
2.  equip the learners with knowledge and skills through critical appraisal and synthesis of research evidence in a systematic approach. 
3.  recognize, manage and evaluate complex medical-surgical problems associated with dysfunction of gastrointestinal, hepatobiliary, renal and urology systems in the adult population.
4.  recognize, manage and evaluate complex medical-surgical problems associated with dysfunction of endocrine, autoimmune and integumentary systems in adult population.

Course Title: Post-Diploma Certificate in Practice of Orthopaedic Nursing 2 (part of Advanced Diploma in Nursing (Orthopaedics))
Course Description:
Post-Diploma Certificate in Practice of Orthopaedic Nursing 2 (PDC 4 of Advanced Diploma in Nursing (Orthopaedics)) aims to prepare the learners on the following:

1. Provide the learners with a better understanding of orthopaedic practices in different settings. 

2. Utilize the problem-solving approach in the assessment, planning and delivery of nursing care, as well as develop analytical and critical thinking through reflective practice.

Course Title: Post-Diploma Certificate in Practice of Paediatric Nursing 2 (part of Advanced Diploma in Nursing (Paediatrics))
Course Description:
Post-Diploma Certificate in Practice of Paediatric Nursing 2 (PDC 4 of Advanced Diploma in Nursing (Paediatrics)) aims to prepare the learners on the following:

1. Provide the learners the opportunity to consolidate their knowledge and skills as well as gain the experience of delivering care to the paediatric client. 

2. The learners will utilize the problem-solving approach in the assessment, planning and delivery of nursing care, as well as develop analytical and critical thinking through reflective practice.

Course Title: Post-Diploma Certificate in Practice of Perioperative Nursing 2 (part of Advanced Diploma in Nursing (Perioperative))
Course Description:
Post-Diploma Certificate in Practice of Perioperative Nursing 2 (PDC 4 of Advanced Diploma in Nursing (Perioperative)) aims to prepare the following modules with a total of 320 hours:
1. Utilize the problem-solving approach in the assessment, planning and delivery of nursing care, as well as develop analytical and critical thinking through reflective practice.
2. Further develop competence and analytical and critical thinking in the assessment, planning and delivery of nursing care so as to transit to their roles as nurse specialists.

Course Title: Post-Diploma Certificate in Practice of Medical-Surgical Nursing 2 (part of Advanced Diploma in Nursing (Medical-Surgical))
Course Description:
Post-Diploma Certificate in Practice of Medical-Surgical Nursing 2 (PDC4 of Advanced Diploma in Nursing (Medical-Surgical)) aims to prepare the learners in the following:

1.  utilize problem-solving or evidence-based approach in the assessment, planning and delivery of nursing care to with different medical conditions. 
2.  equip learners with better understanding of care delivery and needs of clients who have undergone different medical-surgical procedures in different healthcare settings.

Course Title: Pharmacy Technician Entry to Practice Framework (PT ETP) Train-the-trainer Workshop
Course Description:
At the end of this module, participants will be able to:
1.	Articulate the need to have national competency standards for entry-level pharmacy technicians
2.	Familiarise with the skill elements in each of the 7 domains within the PT ETP framework
3.	Contextualise skill elements to institutional settings
4.	Administer assessments using stipulated assessment tools
5.	Identify trainees’ learning gap based on training records, and assessments

Course Title: Post-Diploma Certificate in Practice of Oncology Nursing 1 (part of Advanced Diploma in Nursing (Oncology))
Course Description:
On completion of this module, learners will be able to:
1.  Demonstrate knowledge, critical analytic process and psycho-socio-motor skills in the care of clients. 
2.  Apply nursing process and evidenced-based practice in the delivery of nursing care. 
3.  Plan and implement health teaching for clients and their significant others.
4.  Collaborate with the multi-disciplinary team. 
5.  Develop professional knowledge, oncology nursing expertise and clinical leadership qualities through reflective practice.

Course Title: Post-Diploma Certificate in Practice of Orthopaedic Nursing 1 (part of Advanced Diploma in Nursing (Orthopaedics))
Course Description:
On completion of this module, learners will be able to:

1. Apply knowledge of orthopaedic nursing in a variety of orthopaedic settings.

2. Perform the psychomotor skills related to orthopaedic nursing.

3. Collaborate with members of the health care team in the provision of comprehensive care to the orthopaedic clients and their significant others.

4. Plan and implement educational and rehabilitative programmes for clients and their significant others.

5. Explore the resources available and the role of the multidisciplinary team.

6. Reflect on own practice to evaluate and provide quality care in the orthopaedic settings.

Course Title: Post-Diploma Certificate in Practice of Mental Health Nursing 1 (part of Advanced Diploma in Nursing (Mental Health))
Course Description:
On completion of this module, learners will be able to:
1.  apply knowledge of advanced mental health nursing in a variety mental health setting.
2.  understand the scope of mental health nursing and the responsibilities of the extended role of the mental health nurse.
3.  assess, plan, implement and evaluate care for the client with mental health problems and their significant others.
4.  participate in a comprehensive range of treatments and therapeutic approaches for different problems encountered by the clients.
5.  collaborate with members of the multidisciplinary team in the provision of comprehensive care to the clients with mental health problems and their significant others. 
6.  reflect on own practice with the aim to provide quality care and service to the clients with mental health problems and their significant others.

Course Title: Post-Diploma Certificate in Practice of Medical-Surgical Nursing 1 (part of Advanced Diploma in Nursing (Medical-Surgical))
Course Description:
On completion of this module, learners will be able to:

1.  Apply knowledge of advanced medical-surgical nursing in high dependency units in healthcare settings.
2.  Understand the scope of medical-surgical nursing and the responsibilities of the extended role of the medical surgical nurse high dependency units in healthcare settings.
3.  Assess, plan, implement and evaluate care for the clients with complex medical and surgical health problems and their significant others
4.  Participate in a comprehensive range of treatments and therapeutic approaches for different problems encountered by the clients.
5.  Collaborate with members of the multidisciplinary team in the provision of comprehensive care to the clients and their significant others.
6.  Apply appropriate research evidence to inform clinical judgement in relevant setting and with a range of clients with different medical-surgical conditions.
7.  Reflect on own practice with the ultimate aim to provide quality care and service to the clients and their significant others.

Course Title: Post-Diploma Certificate in Principles and Science of Nephro-Urology Nursing (part of Advanced Diploma in Nursing (Nephro-Urology))
Course Description:
Post-Diploma Certificate in Principles and Science of Nephro-Urology Nursing (PDC1 of Advanced Diploma in Nursing (Nephro-Urology)) aims to prepare the learners on the following:

1.  in-depth study of general and systemic pathophysiology, clinical presentations and complications of disease states. 
2.  develop learners’ skills and knowledge in history taking and physical examination by integrating assessment findings to make appropriate clinical decisions
3.  explore various modalities of renal replacement therapies and current clinical practice guidelines. 
4.  in-depth study of the causes, risk factors, pathophysiology, complications and current trends of management of individuals with acute and chronic urological conditions.

Course Title: Post-Diploma Certificate in Advanced Concepts in Mental Health Nursing (part of Advanced Diploma in Nursing (Mental Health))
Course Description:
Post-Diploma Certificate in Advanced Concepts in Mental Health Nursing (PDC3 of Advanced Diploma in Nursing (Mental Health)) aims to prepare the learners on the following:

1.  acquire knowledge and skills for effective multidisciplinary management of chronic diseases for adults in the primary, acute and intermediate long-term care (ILTC) settings. 
2.  examine the importance of evidence-based practice (EBP) and the key influencing factors on the adoption of EBP in healthcare and nursing. 
3.  provide the learners with advanced knowledge and fundamental skills
in counseling and in dealing with group processes.
4.  examine the contemporary issues in mental health nursing in addition to providing learners with the knowledge in community mental health nursing.

Course Title: Post-Diploma Certificate in Practice of Diabetes Management and Education (part of Specialist Diploma in Nursing (Diabetes Management & Education))
Course Description:
Post-Diploma Certificate in in Practice of Diabetes Management and Education (PDC2 of Specialist Diploma in Nursing (Diabetes Management & Education)) has a total of 155 training hours and cover the following modules:

1.  Diabetes Education (30 hours)                 
2.  Evidence-Based Practice (45 hours)
3.  Clinical Nursing Practice in Diabetes Management (80 hours)

Course Title: Post-Diploma Certificate in Practice of Oncology Nursing 2 (part of Advanced Diploma in Nursing (Oncology))
Course Description:
Post-Diploma Certificate in Practice of Oncology Nursing 2 (PDC4 of Advanced Diploma in Nursing (Oncology)) aims to prepare the learners in the following:

1.  apply relevant theoretical concepts in hematology into practice. In addition, the cross-training settings enable the learners to consolidate the concepts of cancer rehabilitations and community services in the care of their clients with cancer.
2.  apply principles of care for clients requiring ambulatory cancer treatment, including safe administration of cytotoxic agents. This module also enables the learners to apply the concept of palliative care and symptoms management towards end-of-life.

Course Title: Post-Diploma Certificate in Practice of Mental Health Nursing 2 (part of Advanced Diploma in Nursing (Mental Health))
Course Description:
Post-Diploma Certificate in Practice of Mental Health Nursing 2 (PDC4 of Advanced Diploma in Nursing (Mental Health) aims to prepare the learners on the following:

1.  develop competence and analytical and critical thinking in the assessment, planning and delivery of nursing care. It will enable them to transit to their roles as nurse specialists.
2.  develop competence and analytical and critical thinking in the assessment, planning and delivery of nursing care.

Course Title: Post-Diploma Certificate in Practice of Perianaesthesia Nursing 1 (part of Advanced Diploma in Nursing (Perianaesthesia))
Course Description:
On completion of this module, learners will be able to:
1.  Apply knowledge of advanced perianaesthesia nursing to various surgical settings.
2.  Understand the scope of perianaesthesia nursing and the responsibilities of the extended role of the perianaesthesia nurse.
3.  Assess, plan, implement and evaluate care for clients undergoing anaesthesia for surgery.
4. Collaborate with members of the multidisciplinary team in planning, implementing and evaluation of anaesthesia and PACU care.
5. Reflect on own practice with the ultimate aim to provide quality care and service to the clients and their significant others.

Course Title: Post-Diploma Certificate in Practice of Perianaesthesia Nursing 2 (part of Advanced Diploma in Nursing (Perianaesthesia))
Course Description:
Post-Diploma Certificate in Practice of Perianaesthesia  Nursing 2 (PDC4 of Advanced Diploma in Nursing (Perianaesthesia)) aims to prepare the learners on the following:
1.  develop learners' competence and analytical and critical thinking in the assessment, planning and delivery of nursing care for clients
in the clinical placement.
2.  develop learners' understanding, competence and knowledge in the care of geriatric clients requiring continuous nursing care in the
community.

Course Title: LIVING WELL - DESIGNING AN ELDERLY- AND DEMENTIA-INCLUSIVE, SMART ENVIRONMENT
Course Description: The course aims to comprehensively address issues of environmental fit, for seniors and/or persons with cognitive impairment (associated with dementia) and their families, by introducing applicable elderly- and dementia-inclusive technology/IOT and environmental design in the living space.

Course Title: Post-Diploma Certificate in Principles of ENT Nursing (part of Specialist Diploma in Nursing (Ear, Nose and Throat))
Course Description:
Post-Diploma Certificate in Principles of ENT Nursing (PDC1 of Specialist Diploma in Nursing (Ear, Nose and Throat) aims to prepare the learners in the following:

1.  provide an understanding of the contemporary principles and processes of cellular injury and inflammation which are the fundamental causes of most diseases, followed by an in-depth study of pathophysiology processes related to ear, nose and throat nursing. 
2.  examine the importance of evidence-based practice (EBP) and the key influencing factors on the adoption of EBP in healthcare and nursing. It will equip the learners with knowledge and skills through critical appraisal and synthesis of research evidence in a systematic approach.
3.  focus on the assessment and identification of problems associated with the ear and nose.

Course Title: Post-Diploma Certificate in Principles of Radiology Nursing (part of Specialist Diploma in Nursing for Radiology)
Course Description:
Post-Diploma Certificate in Principles of Radiology Nursing (PDC1 of Specialist Diploma in Nursing for Radiology) has a total of 135 training hours and covers the following modules:

1.  Fundamentals of Radiology Nursing (30 hours)
2.  Diagnostic Radiology Nursing (30 hours)
3.  Radiation Biology & Radiation Protection (30 hours) 
4.  Evidence-Based Practice (45 hours)

Course Title: Post-Diploma Certificate in Practice of ENT Nursing (part of Specialist Diploma in Nursing (Ear, Nose and Throat))
Course Description:
Post-Diploma Certificate in Practice of ENT Nursing (PDC2 of Specialist Diploma in Nursing (Ear, Nose and Throat) aims to prepare the learners in the following:

1.   provide learners with the knowledge and skills to assess and identify problems associated with oral cavity and throat structures and the management and care of clients, including children.
2.  provide the knowledge and skills to assess and identify significant problems associated with head and neck non-neoplastic and neoplastic diseases. It also enables for the learner to apply the knowledge and skills in management, care and rehabilitation of ENT clients with complex needs.
3.  provide the learner with the opportunity to integrate theory into practice in a variety of ENT settings. The learners will utilize the problem-solving approach in the assessment, planning and delivery of nursing care to the ENT clients as well as develop analytical and critical thinking through reflective practice.

Course Title: Post-Diploma Certificate in Practice of Radiology Nursing (Part of Specialist Dipolma in Nursing for Radiology )
Course Description:
Post-Diploma Certificate in Practice of Radiology Nursing
(PDC2 of Specialist Diploma in Nursing for Radiology) has a total of 155 training hours and covers the following modules:

1.  Interventional Radiology Nursing (45 hours)
2.  Anesthetic and Surgical Asepsis (30 hours)
3.  Radiology Nursing Clinical Practice (80 hours)

Course Title: Post-Diploma Certificate in Principles of Perianaesthesia Nursing (part of Advanced Diploma in Nursing (Perianaesthesia))
Course Description:
Post-Diploma Certificate in Principles of Perianaesthesia Nursing (PDC1 of Advanced Diploma in Nursing (Perianaesthesia) aims to prepare the learners in the following areas:
1.  key concepts in applied pharmacology which will form the foundation to understanding the rationale for pharmacological management of clients with complex health problems
2. an in-depth knowledge and skills of health assessment to develop their abilities to perform systematic assessment for clinical decisions and interventions.
3.  fundamentals, knowledge and skills of anaesthesia to enable learners to function as a perianaesthesia nurse. Learners will be equipped with knowledge in the management of these clients during the perianaesthesia phase. 
4.  complex problems and complications associated with all types of anaesthesia, a wide variety of surgeries / procedures and all categories of clients.

Course Title: Specialist Diploma in Nutrition Science
Course Description:
Upon completion of Specialist Diploma in Nutrition Science, the participants will be able to:
•	Demonstrate nutrition knowledge and skills in the food and nutrition industry, and able to integrate and apply them effectively.
•	Communicate effectively on nutrition matters in the community.

Course Title: Specialist Diploma in Nursing (Diabetes Management & Education)
Course Description:
The Specialist Diploma in Nursing (Diabetes Management and Education) will be offered part-time over 8 months, covering the following PDCs:

1.Post-Diploma Certificate in Principles of Diabetes Management and Education (135 hours)                                                                                       
2. Post-Diploma Certificate in in Practice of Diabetes Management and Education (155 hours)    

Total training hours: 290

Course Title: Specialist Diploma in Nursing (Ear, Nose and Throat)
Course Description:
The Specialist Diploma in Nursing (Ear, Nose and Throat) is offered to to equip registered nurses (RNs) with specialist knowledge and skills to ensure competence in ENT nursing. The course runs over 9 months covering the following PDCs:

1.Post-Diploma Certificate in Principles of ENT Nursing (135 hours)
2.Post-Diploma Certificate in Practice of ENT Nursing (155 hours)

Course Title: Specialist Diploma in Nursing (Endoscopy)
Course Description:
The Specialist Diploma in Nursing (Endoscopy) is targeted at registered nurses who are working in the endoscopy centres and require in-depth knowledge and skills required to provide quality care for clients undergoing a range of endoscopic procedures.  The course is offered part-time over 39 weeks, covering the following PDCs:

1.Post-Diploma Certificate in Principles of Endoscopy Nursing (135 hours)  
2.Post-Diploma Certificate in in Practice of Endoscopy Nursing (155 hours)

Course Title: Specialist Diploma in Radiology Nursing
Course Description:
The Specialist Diploma in Radiology Nursing will be offered part-time over 10 months, covering the following PDCs:

1.Post-Diploma Certificate in Principles of Radiology Nursing (135 hours)
2.Post-Diploma Certificate in Practice of Radiology Nursing (155 hours)

Course Title: Advanced Diploma in Nursing (Management)
Course Description:
The Advanced Diploma in Nursing (Management) will be offered part-time in 3 PDCs over 12 months (52 weeks) with academic lessons held three times a week (9 hours/week) with the following Post Diploma Certificates.

(1) Post-Diploma Certificate in Business Management (135 hours)
(2) Post-Diploma Certificate in Healthcare Management (165 hours)
(3) Post-Diploma Certificate in Quality and Information Management (180 hours)

Course Title: Advanced Diploma in Nursing (Medical-Surgical)
Course Description: The Advanced Diploma in Nursing (Medical-Surgical) aims to develop specialty nurses with a well-developed knowledge base and specialist skills in both the technological and the caring dimensions of their respective specialty to make sound and rapid clinical judgements within a complex care environment, and to provide optimal holistic care to clients requiring specialty nursing.

Course Title: Advanced Diploma in Nursing (Mental Health)
Course Description: The Advanced Diploma in Nursing (Mental Health) aims to develop specialty nurses with a well-developed knowledge base and specialist skills in both the technological and the caring dimensions of their respective specialty to make sound and rapid clinical judgements within a complex care environment, and to provide optimal holistic care to clients requiring specialty nursing.

Course Title: Advanced Diploma in Nursing (Nephro-Urology)
Course Description: The Advanced Diploma in Nursing (Nephro-Urology) aims to develop specialty nurses with a well-developed knowledge base and specialist skills in both the technological and the caring dimensions of their respective specialty to make sound and rapid clinical judgements within a complex care environment, and to provide optimal holistic care to clients requiring specialty nursing.

Course Title: Advanced Diploma in Nursing (Oncology)
Course Description: The Advanced Diploma in Nursing (Oncology) aims to develop specialty nurses with a well-developed knowledge base and specialist skills in both the technological and the caring dimensions of their respective specialty to make sound and rapid clinical judgements within a complex care environment, and to provide optimal holistic care to clients requiring specialty nursing.

Course Title: Advanced Diploma in Nursing (Orthopaedics)
Course Description: The Advanced Diploma in Nursing (Orthopaedics) aims to develop specialty nurses with a well-developed knowledge base and specialist skills in both the technological and the caring dimensions of their respective specialty to make sound and rapid clinical judgements within a complex care environment, and to provide optimal holistic care to clients requiring specialty nursing.

Course Title: Advanced Diploma in Nursing (Paediatrics)
Course Description: The Advanced Diploma in Nursing (Paediatrics) aims to develop specialty nurses with a well-developed knowledge base and specialist skills in both the technological and the caring dimensions of their respective specialty to make sound and rapid clinical judgements within a complex care environment, and to provide optimal holistic care to clients requiring specialty nursing.

Course Title: Advanced Diploma in Nursing (Perianaesthesia)
Course Description: The Advanced Diploma in Nursing (Perianaesthesia) aims to develop specialty nurses with a well-developed knowledge base and specialist skills in both the technological and the caring dimensions of their respective specialty to make sound and rapid clinical judgements within a complex care environment, and to provide optimal holistic care to clients requiring specialty nursing.

Course Title: Advanced Diploma in Nursing (Perioperative)
Course Description: The Advanced Diploma in Nursing (Perioperative) aims to develop specialty nurses with a well-developed knowledge base and specialist skills in both the technological and the caring dimensions of their respective specialty to make sound and rapid clinical judgements within a complex care environment, and to provide optimal holistic care to clients requiring specialty nursing.

Course Title: Sports Medicine Theory Course (Asynchronous E-learning)
Course Description: The Sports Medicine Theory Course (SMTC) comprises six online lecture modules and aims to provide clinicians with fundamental knowledge of Sports Medicine practice.

Course Title: Foundation of Palliative Medicine (Synchronous & Asynchronous e-learning)
Course Description:
On completion of the module, the learners should be able:
1.  To understand and identify the symptoms patients suffering from cancer and end-stage organ disease experience

2. To understand the pharmacological and non-pharmacological management for symptoms relief in patients suffering from life-limiting diseases

3. To understand and to describe the multi-modal approach and clinical therapeutics of cancer pain management

4. To recognize conditions presenting as palliative emergencies and understand the management strategies

5. To recognize the issues related to patients suffering from life-limiting diseases, taking into account the physical, physiological, social and spiritual dimensions of care

Course Title: Foundation of Psychosocial Care and Thanatology (Synchronous & Asynchronous e-learning)
Course Description:
On completion of the module, the learners should be able:

1.	Identify the foundational knowledge on death, dying and bereavement as well as issues concerning spirituality and meaning of life. 

2.	Articulate the overarching psychological, social, and cultural impact of death and loss with a particular focused on the Asian experience.

3.	Identify the impact of grief and bereavement through the lens of thanatology, psychology, sociology, social work and counselling science. 

4.	Apply basic practical skills for working with individuals and families facing the challenges of death, dying, grief and bereavement.

5.	Develop essential reflective practices on personal beliefs, attitudes and experiences with death and loss through the principles of dignity and compassion

Course Title: Living Well with Hypertension and Hypercholesterolemia
Course Description:
At the end of the course, for the diseases of hypertension and hypercholesterolemia , learners will be able to: 
•	Coach and counsel patients (mostly senior citizens) on the prevention and management of hypertension and hypercholesterolemia through behavioural and lifestyle strategies such as behavioural change, overcoming substance abuse e.g., smoking and alcohol, and in exercise and diet management.
•	Manage community and healthcare treatments in specialist and community clinics, and in Senior Activity Centres.
•	Design treatment plans for patients and senior citizens on lifestyle and behavourial interventions.

Course Title: Care and Prevention of Common Sport Injuries
Course Description:
At the end of the course, learners will be able to: 
(1) Identify and mitigate risk factors in physical movements 
(2) Classify the various types of sport injuries 
(3) Apply appropriate first-aid treatment for trauma injuries 
(4) Advocate on the prevention of acute sport injuries 
(5) Advocate for basic sports care and prevention of sport injuries resulting from overuse 
(6) Counsel athletes on sports-related health and well-being matters 
(7) Coach sports-persons on the planning of their fluid intake, prevention and execution of first-aid for persons suffering from heat injury 
(8) Acquire skills to perform joint taping for common sport injuries 
(9) Coach sports-persons on the care and prevention of acute and chronic sports injuries

Course Title: Advanced Palliative Care in Practice (Synchronous & Asynchronous e-learning)
Course Description:
On completion of the module, the learners should be able:

1.	Apply the principles and philosophies of palliative care
2.	Discuss the concept of quality of life across the spectrum of living with life limiting disease, including at the terminal phase
3.	Integrate disease modifying and palliative care
4.	Analyze the implications of ethical issues in palliative care 
5.	Discuss palliative care in various healthcare settings, its’ applications and limitations
6.	Reflect on the techniques and effect of therapeutic communications affecting the well-being and patients and practitioners
7.	Discuss the benefits of rehabilitation in palliative care
8.	Discuss and appreciate the benefits of interdisciplinary care for patients
9.	Synthesize and plan management of care of patients

Course Title: Advanced Palliative Medicine (Synchronous & Asynchronous e-learning)
Course Description:
On completion of the module, the learners should be able:

1.  To understand the holistic assessment and management of symptoms which patients suffering from advanced cancer and end-stage organ diseases experienced, taking into account the physical, physiological, social and spiritual dimensions of care

2. To understand the multi-modal approach in the pharmacological and non-pharmacological management for symptoms relief in patients suffering from advanced life-limiting diseases

3. To understand the multi-modal assessment, management and clinical therapeutics of complex cancer pain management

4. To recognize conditions presenting as palliative emergencies and understand the management strategies

5. To appraise critically palliative care literature in research, ethics and appreciate the impact of evidence-based practice, systems-based policies and practice guidelines which shaped clinical practice

6. To develop self-evaluation and critical reflective skills and to incorporate the knowledge into your own professional practice

7. To formulate a plan in the management of patients suffering with life-limiting diseases, incorporating suggestions fro

Course Title: Living Well with Hypertension and Hypercholesterolemia with Diabetes Mellitus
Course Description:
At the end of the course, for the diseases of hypertension and hypercholesterolemia with diabetes mellitus , learners will be able to: 

•	Acquire and retain accurate basic knowledge of diabetes mellitus, hypertension and hypercholesterolemia.
•	Coach and counsel patients (mostly senior citizens) on the management of living with hypertension, hypercholesterolemia and diabetes mellitus through compliance with medication, and behavioural and lifestyle (such as exercise and diet) strategies. 
•	Manage community and healthcare treatments in specialist and community clinics, and in Senior Activity Centres. 
•	Acquire the necessary skills to design treatment plans for patients and senior citizens on lifestyle and behavorial interventions.

Course Title: Advanced Psychosocial Care and Thanatology (Synchronous & Asynchronous e-learning)
Course Description:
On completion of the module, the learners should be able:

1.	Discuss the most advanced knowledge on death, dying and bereavement as well as issues concerning dignity, compassion, and spirituality. 

2.	Analyse the overarching psychological, social, and cultural impact of death and loss with a particular focused on the Singaporean experience.

3.	Explain the impact of grief and bereavement through the lens of thanatology, psychology, sociology, social work and counselling science. 

4.	Apply advanced practical-skills for working with individuals, families and communities facing the challenges of death, dying, grief and bereavement.

5.	Develop deep reflective practices on personal beliefs, attitudes and experiences with death and loss through the principles of self-care, self-compassion, and sustainable resilience

Course Title: Foundation of Palliative Care in Practice (Synchronous & Asynchronous e-learning)
Course Description:
On completion of the module, the learners should be able:
 
1.	Apply the principles and philosophies of palliative care
2.	Describe the models of palliative care delivery 
3.	Discuss the concept of quality of life across the spectrum of living with life-limiting disease, including in the terminal phase
4.	Integrate disease modifying and palliative care
5.	Analyze ethical issues arising in palliative care 
6.	Initiate and facilitate advance care planning conversations with patient and family
7.	Discuss palliative care application in various healthcare settings
8.	Apply the basic principles of therapeutic communications in palliative care
9.	Relate palliative care to special patient groups – pediatric and young adults
10.	Discuss the focus of quality nursing care in advanced illness
11.	Discuss the benefits of rehabilitation in palliative care

Course Title: Living Well with Arthritis
Course Description:
At the end of the course, for living with arthritis, learners will be able to:
•	Acquire and retain accurate basic knowledge of osteoarthritis (OA) and rheumatoid arthritis (RA) 
•	State the cause(s) of OA and RA
•	Describe the effect of lifestyle, posture and supplements on OA and RA
•	Coach and counsel, especially elderly, patients on the management of osteoarthritis at the different stages, working towards improving their self competency and self sufficiency in living with OA/RA
•	modify and optimise exercise variables to reduce risk of, and progression of OA/RA
•	Acquire the skill sets in managing community and healthcare treatments in specialist and community clinics, and in Senior Activity Centres. 
•	Enable learners to acquire the necessary skills to design treatment plans for patients and senior citizens on lifestyle and behavioural interventions.

Course Title: Living Well with Common Joint Pain
Course Description:
At the end of the course, Living Well with Common Joint Pain,  learners will be able to: 
•	acquire and retain accurate basic knowledge of joint pain (osteoarthritis)
•	acquire evidence-based management of joint pain/ osteoarthritis
•	acquire basic application skills of physiotherapy and adjunct modalities for treating osteoarthritis/ joint pain
•	Acquire the necessary skills sets in managing community and healthcare treatments in specialist and community clinics, and in Senior Activity Centres. 
•	Enable participants to acquire the necessary skills to come up with treatment plans for patients and senior citizens on lifestyle and behavioural interventions.

Course Title: Living Well with Diabetes Mellitus
Course Description:
At the end of the course, allied healthcare professionals, nurses and other health-related workers will be able to:
•	Acquire and retain accurate basic knowledge of exercising with diabetes
•	Identify the safety aspects of each exercise
•	Explain how to carry out each exercise safely
•	Develop a personalised plan/ regime to allow the diabetic patient to incorporate regular exercise and live a healthier lifestyle 
•	Acquire the necessary skills sets in managing community and healthcare treatments in specialist and community clinics, and in Senior Activity Centres.
•	Enable participants to acquire the necessary skills to come up with treatment plans for patients and senior citizens on lifestyle and behavioural interventions.

Course Title: Workplace Learning and Performance Assessment in Healthcare (Classroom & Synchronous e-Learning)
Course Description:
The course learning objectives are:
(a)  Able to collaborate with learners to develop an individualised learning intervention plan based on WPLF's constructive feedback.
(b) Able to apply IHLs tools to perform effective workplace learning assessments of learners with different levels of learning.

Course Title: Fundamentals of Population Health
Course Description:
At the end of the programme, the student will be able to:
1.     Articulate the foundational concepts and paradigms of Population Health.
2.     Analyse and establish sound objectives for Population Health initiatives.
3.     Analyse and construct sound dynamics for Population Health systems.
4.     Apply systems thinking to manage complexity and constraints in Population Health.
5.     Integrate and synthesise evidence for design, implementation, monitoring and evaluation of policy and programmes.
6.     Apply appropriate levers to create the biggest impact to the health of the population.
7.     Appreciate the principles in designing an appropriate monitoring and evaluation framework for Population Health.
8.     Architect a sound systems approach for improving the Health of the Population.

Course Title: Beginning Artificial Intelligence Through Neuroscience (Synchronous e-learning)
Course Description:
Neuroscience helped inspire deep learning, which is the basis of Artificial Intelligence (AI) technologies like speech recognition and face recognition, and is being developed for applications such as assisted interpretation of medical images

This course introduces the relevant neuroscience, including the function and connectivity of the cerebral cortex, which is used to motivate the architecture of deep learning artificial neural networks. Participants will learn the mathematical and statistical concepts needed to fit models to data beginning with simple examples such as linear regression and binary classification, followed by application of these principles to train deep learning models. There will be opportunities for participants to experience hands-on coding with common AI tools. Potential dangers of AI will also be examined and discussed. 

At the end of the course, participants will be able to: 

 - Understand fundamental AI concepts to effectively manage AI technology
 - Acquire basic coding skills to construct simple AI systems
 - Build a simple deep learning model and deploy it as required

Course Title: Understanding Orofacial Pain
Course Description:
Orofacial pain is frequently challenging to manage because the subject is poorly understood. At the same time, the changes that have occurred in the related fields over the previous 20 to 30 years were siloed and not well researched, making it a confusing topic. 

With the current understanding about Orofacial Pain, a comprehensive presentation will equip the course attendees with the working knowledge of the basic and clinical science of general and orofacial pain. This allows them to develop competency in performing a thorough assessment of patients, including history taking and physical examination, recognising the related co-morbidities, and the formulation of a management plan.

Course Title: Facilitate Effective Workplace Learning for Healthcare Professionals (Classroom and Synchronous e-Learning)
Course Description:
The course learning objectives are:
(a) Able to demonstrate coaching and mentoring skills to facilitate workshop learning and formulated action plans to develop coaching culture at the workplace.
(b) Able to serve as a role model for lifelong learning.

Course Title: Lead Workplace Learning and Development for Healthcare Professionals
Course Description:

4. Professional development of staff

Learners will apply these skills in the following areas:
1. Designing a clinical education programme for students/new hires/juniors

3. Managing interdisciplinary teams
4. Mentoring of staff

Course Title: Towards Ageing Well with Healthier SG: Community Mobilization and Asset-building Approaches
Course Description:

Course Description:

• Understand how healthcare policy is made to address the key contemporary issues and challenges that Singapore healthcare faces

Course Title: Graduate Diploma in Palliative Medicine
Course Description:
The Graduate Diploma in Palliative Medicine (GDPM) Programme is offered by the Division of Graduate Medical Studies (DGMS), NUS Yong Loo Lin School of Medicine, in collaboration with the Chapter of Palliative Medicine Physicians (CPMP). 

The Programme aims to train practicing doctors, especially family physicians, to be competent and confident in managing patients who require palliative care in the clinic and in the home setting. 

The Programme is also relevant to family physicians working in hospices, nursing homes, community hospitals, and home medical services, and to non-specialist doctors and specialists in other disciplines who may be interested in palliative care.

Course Title: Graduate Diploma in Mental Health
Course Description: The Graduate Diploma in Mental Health [GDip (Mental Hlth)], offered by the Division of Graduate Medical Studies (DGMS), National University of Singapore, in conjunction with Institute of Mental Health (IMH), aims to increase the capability of general practitioners (GPs) in mental health by training them appropriately for the role to detect and treat minor mental health disorders in the community. Students who successfully complete the six Courses and final examination will be awarded the GDip (Mental Hlth).

Course Title: Graduate Diploma in Family Practice Dermatology
Course Description: The Graduate Diploma in Family Practice Dermatology [GDip (FP Derm)], a joint programme offered by the Division of Graduate Medical Studies (DGMS) and the National Skin Centre (NSC) aims to equip Medical Officers or General Practitioners (both locals and overseas) with the knowledge and skills to deal with skin diseases at a primary care level.

Course Title: Informed Consent for Healthcare Practitioners (Classroom & Asynchronous e-learning)
Course Description:
This course will cover the following learning objectives:
1. Understand the definition of consent vs informed consent.
2. Understand the legal principles in Informed Consent from Bolam to Montgomery.
3. Understand how changes in legislation have affected the Informed Consent process.
4. Index cases of legal cases involving Informed consent in Singapore.
5. Understand the ethical basis for negotiating Informed Consent.
6. Understand the basic requirements of taking an Informed Consent.
7. Understand the principles of Informed Consent in line with the current legal standards.
8. Appreciate possible challenges, limitations and pitfalls in Informed Consent.
9. Apply the consent process in certain selected patient groups (paediatrics, loss of mental capacity, emergencies, national servicemen) that may pose special challenges

Through faculty presentations, facilitations, supported by case studies, scenario plays, and peer discussions, learners will review details of consent in medical practice.

Course Title: Graduate Diploma in Occupational Medicine
Course Description:
The Graduate Diploma in Occupational Medicine [GDip (Occ Med)], offered by the Division of Graduate Medical Studies (DGMS), National University of Singapore, aims to provide comprehensive skills to doctors in general practice and specialists who wish to have a better understanding of the occupational aspects of their specialty and to be able to implement programmes to protect and promote a healthy workplace in line with the Total Workplace Safety and Health (Total WSH) Initiatives.

Emphasis is placed on the importance of offering a total and integrated workplace safety, health and wellbeing programme. The Programme will also include “Fitness-to-return-to-work” assessment, management of workers with common chronic diseases, and a project to start a Total WSH programme at a workplace.

Course Title: Foundations in Health Professions Education (Classroom and Asynchronous e-learning)
Course Description:
This foundation course designed especially for teaching staff and administrative staff involved in teaching, developing and managing educational programmes in medical, dental and other healthcare professions.  It consists of 2 parts that learners will have to complete: 5 online modules and 1 face-to-face workshop. The 5 online modules comprised of all the crucial elements in education: curriculum organisation, delivery of instruction and evaluation, assessment and feedback, supporting learners and scholarship of teaching and learning. And, face-to-face workshop will enhance their skills and knowledge in education. Learner may choose one of these workshops: i)teaching and learning; ii) leading and managing in education; iii) quality assurance & accreditation

The trainers in this course consist of experienced medical educationalist, qualified clinical, allied health and nursing educators, who will share their knowledge and experiences through engaging mini-presentations and case discussions. 

The aim of this course is to develop confidence and equip health professions educators with knowledge and skills in education so that they are competent in educating health professions students as well as proficient making educational related decision (such as curriculum development, assessment, quality assurance and leadership).

Learning objectives:

4.	interpret clinical immunological tests and outcomes.
5.	evaluate the potential of emerging strategies of immunotherapy for target diseases.

Course Title: WSQ Respiratory Care in Nursing (L3)
Course Description:
The healthcare industry is one of the fastest-growing service industries around the world. In Singapore, the healthcare market grew to $29.8 billion in 2020, a 9% rise from the previous year and is expected to double by 2029 (The Straits Times, 2020). The factors that lead to the growth are an ageing population, increasing cases of chronic health conditions and increasing consumerism. By 2030, ageing population will rise to one in four or 25 per cent of Singaporeans, just like Japan and some European countries (The Straits Times, 2022). As a result, nurses are in high demand across the board in the healthcare industry.
Despite the growth and demand, across nations, nurses are quitting in large numbers. In Singapore, nurses are resigning in record numbers, causing a severe shortage at the hospitals. In year 2021 alone, 14.8% of the foreign nursing cohort left to other countries that offer better remuneration (The Straits Times, 2022). Hospitals are actively seeking nurses to recruit and also hiring healthcare assistants (HAs) to sort out the manpower issues. Healthcare assistants (HAs) work alongside with nurses and they assist nurses with any additional duties where nurses need a helping hand. Their duties can range from common caretaking such as changing bedsheets or giving a patient a bath, to more healthcare-specific tasks such as recording a patient's health concerns and symptoms or checking vital signs (i.e., blood pressure, heart rate and etc.,).
