Course Description:
Upon completion of the programme, graduates will be able to:
- Demonstrate an understanding in the concepts and fundamentals of human anatomy and physiology, cell and molecular biology, and the disorders and diseases affecting the human body
- Apply practical knowledge for working proficiently in diagnostic laboratories, including preparation of chemical reagents, collection, safe handling, processing, and analysis of biological specimens
- Apply the test principles and methodologies used in laboratory investigations to detect diseases and disorders, and assess and troubleshoot common errors in laboratory investigations
- Ensure and maintain excellence in quality and standards in the medical laboratory science industry

Course Title: Population Health Advocacy
Course Description: Understanding human biology is key to health coaching. Learners will be introduced to the 3 Health Pillars (Nutrition, Physical Activity, Mental Wellness) of Health Promotion. With the knowledge, learners can now apply relevant health advocacy techniques used in the field.

Course Title: Fundamentals of Health Coaching
Course Description: This module introduces learners to the Health Coaching framework. Learners will understand the modes and roles of health coaching in Singapore. Learners will have an understanding of the purpose of being a health coach in the community with skills of health assessment and monitoring. Learners will pick up tips on the upcoming digitalisation of health coaching and ethics.

Course Title: Post Diploma Certificate in Community Health Management across Lifespan
Course Description: This Post Diploma Certificate focuses on the effective delivery of relevant health prescriptions within the community for specific groups of people. There will be engagement with industry partners for different scenarios using case documentation as part of building up their personal portfolios.

Course Title: Post Diploma Certificate in Population Health Management across Lifespan
Course Description: This Post Diploma Certificate focuses on understanding the role of health coaching within the framework of population health within Singapore. Key domains expected of health advocacy will be applied to different scenarios for a sustainable behaviour change.

Course Title: Modular Certificate in Communication in Healthcare
Course Description:
This Modular Certificate consists of the following modules: 
- Counselling and Communication
- Health Coaching 
- Communication with Community Partners
- Project on Communication in Healthcare

Course Title: Modular Certificate in Histotechnology and Cytotechnology (HC)
Course Description: This MC is part of the Diploma in Applied Science (Medical Laboratory Science)(MLS)

Course Title: Modular Certificate in Molecular Diagnostics
Course Description:
This MC consists of the following modules:
Molecular Bioscience
Molecular Techniques
Clinical Applications of Molecular Testing

Course Title: Modular Certificate in Promoting Health Behaviours
Course Description:
This Modular Certificate consists of the following modules: 
- Health Promotion 
- Health and Wellness 
- Behaviour Change 
- Nutrition Care Process 
- Project on Promoting Health Behaviours / OJT (

Course Title: Community Health Prescription
Course Description: Knowledge of a well planned and executed health programme is vital for being a Health Coach. This module explores the planning and execution of health programmes in the community. It touches on the operationalisation of health screening, nutrition, physical and mental wellness programmes.

Course Title: Optimum Nutrition for All Ages
Course Description:
This course aims to equip participants with the basic knowledge of physiological changes and nutritional needs for each stage of life, covering pregnancy and older age. The course will specifically focus on the dietary practice and nutirtional intake to support the nervous and endocrine systems, for optimal brain health and hormonal balance.

Through this course, professionals in nutrition-related industries such as dietary attendants, nutrition advisors, meal planners and caregivers will be able to gain knowledge of the role of nutrients in promoting healthy dietary intakes and preventing diseases. This knowledge facilitates better advisory on dietary and nutritional approaches.  

Food technologists/technicians and food retailers will also be able to utilize insights gained through this course to provide useful information to consumers when developing/marketing their products.

Course Title: Counselling and Communication
Course Description: Topics covered include the theoretical approaches, the counselling process, ethics and confidentiality, and the interpersonal skills required to conduct a session in individual or group settings.

Course Title: Health Coach Portfolio
Course Description: This is a practicum module that encompasses all the concepts of health coaching. Learners will need to build up a portfolio for purpose of health coaching, population health advocacy, communication in behaviour change, community health prescription and case management. At the end of the module, learners will be competent in the knowledge and skills of health coaching.

Course Title: Health Promotion
Course Description: By the end of the module, students will appreciate the different dimensions of health promotion and systematically design and evaluate health promotion programmes.

Course Title: Health Psychology
Course Description: Topics include theories of healthy behaviour, health enhancing behaviour, health risk behaviour, and rehabilitation. This course will also challenge students to critically evaluate the role that cognition, society and the environment play in the health of individuals.

Course Title: Inclusive Physical Activity
Course Description:
Students will appreciate varying recreational and sporting needs of different inclusive profiles through physical activities. They will also understand the challenges of these inclusive profiles from the human developmental viewpoint. This will help students to modify physical activities to help the different inclusive profiles to be included in physical play, games and even sports. 

Students will realise the need to screen and assess the health, physical and fitness conditions before implementing and adapting activities to suit the needs of different inclusive profiles. They will also learn the importance and benefits of inclusive physical activity programming, and be aware of sensitivities associated with programme management for different inclusive profiles.

Course Title: Health Coaching
Course Description: The module also prepares students to educate clients on chronic disease management, mental health issues, polypharmacy and coping strategies in a more engaging and compassionate manner.

Course Title: Machine Learning for Biomedical and Healthcare Applications
Course Description: This module is part of the Post-Diploma Certificate in Biomedical Analytics.

Course Title: Advanced Integrated Care Planning
Course Description: This module aims to equip students with the knowledge and skills to develop comprehensive care plans to effectively utilise community resources for care coordination and co-manage patients with chronic diseases in the community. It also introduces students to the principles of palliative care for advance care planning

Course Title: Leadership and Outcome Management
Course Description: Students will appreciate the activities that contribute towards the improvement of the quality of patient care and support hospital activities/organisational goals. They will gain knowledge in the optimal utilisation of resources, efficiency in delivery of high-quality care, patient flow, capacity management and other clinical cost reduction initiatives.

Course Title: Specialist Diploma in Health and Wellness Coaching
Course Description:
1.	Synthesise the health coaching framework with existing practices in Singapore.
2.	Apply health promotion techniques using the biopsychosocial aspect of health.
3.	Communicate effectively on self-discovery, autonomy and give back decision-making skills to the client.
4.	Plan, execute and operationalise health programmes for the community.
5.	Apply a holistic framework on case management and documentation.
6.	Build a portfolio of health coaching competencies and experiences.

Course Title: Part-time Diploma in Health Sciences (Health Management)
Communicating for Behaviour Change
Course Description: This module aims to equip learners with advanced communication and health coaching skills. It introduces learners to theoretical framework and evidence-based approaches such as motivational interviewing and active listening so that they can assist individuals to change their health behavior to improve their own health and wellbeing.

Course Title: Communication with Community Partners
Course Description: Students will use these community resources to help the community grow and improve the resident’s quality of life. As a result, students will be able to effectively coordinate health and social care in the community with community partners.

Course Title: Community Case Management
Course Description: In this module, learners will have an overview of case management within the community. Learners will understand the importance of case documentation. Using this knowledge, learners can expand to community and workplace outreach.

Course Title: Clinical Microbiology
Course Description: This subject covers the host-microbe interactions with emphasis on infectious diseases in humans. It includes various modes of transmission, diagnosis, prevention and control of infectious diseases caused by bacteria, viruses, fungi and parasites.

Course Title: Analytical Biochemistry for Clinical Sciences
Course Description: This module presents the basic principles, knowledge and skills for analytical methods used routinely in the analysis of biomolecules with examples of clinical application. The topics include spectrometry, separation and imaging/detection techniques as well as discussion on selected current approaches in the analysis of clinically important biomolecules.

Course Title: Clinical Applications of Molecular Testing
Course Description: This module is an advanced module that focuses on applications of bioinformatics, molecular testing assays and techniques, which enable accurate and precise detection of biomarkers as well as infectious pathogens found in clinical samples.

Course Title: Clinical Chemistry and Instrumentation
Course Description: This module will cover topics on the physiological changes in the disease states and discuss the assessment of underlying biochemical disturbances in diseases based on altered analytes levels. These include the principles of analytical techniques and instrumentation that are routinely use to measure/analyse different analytes in body fluids as well as the importance of maintaining a quality management system particularly in a clinical chemistry laboratory for accurate results reporting beginning from the point of sample collection.

Course Title: Medical Imaging
Course Description: This course covers various aspects of medical imaging, image processing and visualisation. Topics include X-Ray, Magnetic Resonance, Functional Infrared Thermal imaging and Ultrasonic Imaging, as well as more specialized techniques for dynamic studies of specific body functions. The importance of electronics medical images transmission through cloud and cybersecurity will also be covered in this module.

Course Title: Project on Health Through Exercise
Course Description: A project designed for students to demonstrate their ability to assess and prescribe appropriate exercises will be developed with the employer and it is expected to be executed at the workplace.

Course Title: Project on Health Across Lifespan
Course Description: The module will allow students to understand how psychology and sociology of health, and biopsychosocial aspects of ageing impact health and well-being, thus promoting active ageing.

Course Title: Project on Promoting Health Behaviours
Course Description: Students will display their knowledge and skills through the development of a health promoting management plan, after assessing and evaluating client’s health condition(s).

Course Title: Microbiology Technology
Course Description: This module covers the basic techniques used in microbiology laboratories.  It includes identifying types of microorganisms, cultivation and growth of microorganisms.  Students also need to preform various laboratory techniques and procedures for conducting microbiological work/test.

Course Title: Basic Microbiology
Course Description: This module investigates the important fundamentals of microbiology and its relevance to the medical laboratory science.  It covers the types of microorganisms and their relationships to human diseases.

Course Title: Biopsychosocial Aspects of Ageing
Course Description: Students will learn to view ageing from a holistic perspective and at the same time differentiate and manage biological, physical, psychological and physiological ageing. Students will also acquire knowledge and skills related to mental wellness and resilience for seniors in order to promote positive ageing.

Course Title: Immunology
Course Description: This module aims to provide students with an overview on the fundamental concepts of immunology and its importance in clinical setting especially in blood transfusion science. Pathophysiology of various immune-mediated diseases will also be emphasized in this module so that students can understand the mechanisms and processes behind various immune-mediated responses in the human body.

Course Title: Managing Diabetes and Cardiovascular Health
Course Description:
This course aims to equip participants with the basic knowledge to manage diabetes and maintain cariovascular health through dietary and nutritional approaches. The course will cover the nutrients required and their mechanism of action, for gut health, blood glucose control and cardiovascular health.

Through this course, professionals in nutrition-related industries such as dietary attendants, nutrition advisors, meal planners and caregivers will be able to gain knowledge of the role of nutrients in promoting healthy dietary intakes and preventing diseases. This knowledge facilitates better advisory on dietary and nutritional approaches.  

Food technologists/technicians and food retailers will also be able to utilize insights gained through this course to provide useful information to consumers when developing/marketing their products.

Course Title: Assessment and Management of Chronic Diseases
Course Description: Students will also gain a deeper understanding on how these chronic conditions impact the lives of the elderly living in the community. Importantly, students will be taught on identification of “red flags” and how to prevent and manage them.

Course Title: Advanced Care Coordination and Navigation
Course Description: Students will gain an appreciation of various financial schemes and community resources available for the patients, families and caregivers. Students will develop plans to coordinate with the multidisciplinary team of health professionals such as physical therapist and occupational therapist in the promotion of home rehabilitation.

Course Title: Case Management
Course Description: Students will gain an appreciation of the complexities in the coordination of care amongst various stakeholders such as the service providers, clients and their caregivers, while maintaining the desired health outcome and cost.

Course Title: Exercise Programming and Assessment
Course Description: Topics covered include exercise safety and first aid, screening of clients; with focus areas on anthropometrical assessments, as well as fitness assessments for the development and maintenance of cardio-respiratory fitness, muscular strength, power and endurance, flexibility, rehabilitation, and weight control. Students would also equip themselves with the evolving trends in the sports, health and fitness industry and the ethical issues in the delivery of sports and fitness activities.

Course Title: Sociology of Health and Social Care
Course Description: Students will obtain basic knowledge on the influence of social, economic, cultural and environmental factors on health-related behaviour and attitudes as well as their impact on chronic illnesses among seniors.  Socio-economic, and cultural dimensions of individual and health-related behaviour, attitude and beliefs including the structure, dynamics and roles of social support services will be explored. Students will also learn to manage and care for the elderly by applying relevant social theories

Course Title: Biomedical Instrumentation and Systems
Course Description: The aim of this course is to teach students the principles, design and applications of the medical instruments most commonly used in the healthcare industry. Quantitative methods of analysis are developed including simplifying assumptions to make the students understand and design an instrumentation system for diagnostic and therapeutic medical applications. Integration of systems through Internet of Things (IoT) and the concept of networking will be introduced.

Course Title: Biosafety
Course Description: This subject covers the fundamental knowledge and applications of risk assessment and management of biological and other hazards, risks and threats. Biosafety and biosecurity principles and practices as well as interpretation of national and international biosafety standards, guidelines and legislations will also be covered. This subject will also cover administrative controls, incidents and accidents reporting, as well as implementation of measures to maintain a safe working environment.

Course Title: Biochemistry
Course Description: The students will acquire the knowledge of the core biochemical components and metabolic processes of living things and skills for performing biochemical assays. The names, structures and functions of biomolecules are discussed in relation to one another.  The role of enzyme catalysis in regulating biochemical reactions will be covered.  Major metabolic pathways such as glycolysis and citric acid cycle will be examined in the role of generating biological energy.  Practical skills, such as measurement of solutions and chemicals, performing colorimetric assays, and analysis of experimental data will be taught.

Course Title: Blood Banking
Course Description: This module provides students with the fundamentals of routine procedures performed in immuno-haematology laboratories.

Course Title: Haematology
Course Description: This module will equip students with the theoretical knowledge and technical skills to perform routine laboratory investigations in haematology. They will also learn to interpret results and relate findings to various types of haematological disorders such as anaemia, haematological malignancies and coagulopathies for diagnosis and treatment. The importance of quality assurance and quality control in the laboratory will be emphasised in this module.

Course Title: Molecular Bioscience
Course Description: This module introduces the fundamentals of cell biology, molecular biology and genetics. Topics covered include cellular organelles, membrane trafficking, cell cycle and division, chromosome structure, study of genes, eukaryotic gene expression, epigenetics as well as molecular events of replication, transcription and translation.

Course Title: Molecular Techniques
Course Description: This module is designed to equip students with knowledge and molecular techniques that are used to analyse samples. Students will learn about the isolation of nucleic acids and gel electrophoresis, applications of polymerase chain reaction (PCR) and real-time PCR (RT-PCR) in molecular testing, restriction fragment length polymorphism (RFLP) analysis, single nucleotide polymorphism (SNP) genotyping, and enzyme linked immunosorbent assay (ELISA).

Course Title: Project on Communication in Healthcare
Course Description: Emphasis will be placed on utilisation of health promotion and health coaching strategies and engaging community partners to promote health and wellness among seniors.

Course Title: Project on Community Care
Course Description: Students will work with a client and propose a care plan that will include the programmes to help the client live independently.

Course Title: Biomedical and Healthcare Data Sciences
Course Description: This module is part of the Post-Diploma Certificate in Biomedical Analytics.

Course Title: Basic patient care for frontline ambassadors
Course Description:
This 1-day course aims to equip participants with the basic understanding of basic patient care for frontline ambassadors to perform their job.  At the end of this course, participants will be able to: 

1. Understand the chain of infection cycle to better prepare them in the course of work and for risk management
2. Apply proper infection control techniques to minimise spread of infectious diseases in carrying out their duties
3. Apply appropriate communication skills in the execution of their job roles and identify non-verbal cues

Course Title: Introduction to Pharmaceutical Technology
Course Description: This course equips process technicians with the essential skills and knowledge to perform the daily operations in food, pharmaceutical or complementary health products manufacturing industries. The participants will learn about use of automation in manufacturing industries and deepen their hands-on skills to perform manufacturing techniques such as granulation, tabletting and coating.

Course Title: Health and Wellness
Course Description: Topics covered include hygiene and skin care, alternative medicine, wellness therapy, sexually transmitted infections, addictions, nutritional needs, posture and care, genes and health, workplace health and current global health threats.

Course Title: Behaviour Change
Course Description: At the end of the module, students should possess relevant knowledge and applicable techniques to help specific groups of clients in addressing their health needs.

Course Title: Falls and Frailty Prevention
Course Description: This module will also provide tools to plan and implement the different types of exercises and/or functional fitness programmes safely and effectively in order for the senior or elderly population to live independent lives.

Course Title: Integrated Care
Course Description: This module will also equip students with the necessary knowledge and skills that would enable them to perform as Care Coordinators in the healthcare industry.

Course Title: Nutrition Care Process
Course Description: Students will also develop ability to apply medical nutrition care on common chronic medical conditions. Nutritional management give students opportunity to deepen their learning through investigation of client’s health condition(s), plan and execute intervention and education, and re-evaluate client’s progress with follow-up.

Course Title: Psychosocial Aspects of Exercise
Course Description: Students will learn about factors influencing participation in physical activity, the effects of exercise on psychological and mental well-being, and cultural aspects to physical activity participation.

Course Title: Sports and Exercise Prescription
Course Description: Through this module, students will also develop an appreciation of assessing and analyzing traditional and popular exercise programs used presently in the sporting and fitness industry.

Course Title: Integrated Care and Singapore Health System
Course Description: This module focuses on the application of the principles of integrated transitional care in the health system. It aims to equip students with the knowledge on patient assessment for biopsychosocial needs, nursing and financial needs in both inpatient and outpatient settings

Course Title: OJT training cum project
Course Description: Students on the WSP track will be involved in an On-the-Job training (OJT) at their place of employment. A project on designing/regulating biomedical devices will be structured with the employer. Students are expected to execute the project at the workplace. Through hands-on experience in designing and improving biomedical devices, students will acquire a greater understanding of progress and advancement of tools used in healthcare. Non-WSP track students will be assigned in-house projects in place of the OJT component.

Course Title: Project on Communication and Health Coaching
Course Description:
The student-led project equips students with advanced communication and health coaching skills. 
Students will be required to apply theoretical frameworks and evidence-based approaches such as motivational interviewing. The focus will be on the health behavior change strategies used for clients to manage chronic diseases and mental health.

Course Title: Project on Social and Family Dynamics
Course Description: The module will allow students to understand interdisciplinary collaboration, innovative action and the well-being of individuals, families and social institutions through active community involvement.

Course Title: Post-Diploma Certificate in Integrated Care Coordination (ICC)
Course Description: To better meet the evolving healthcare needs of Singapore's greying population, the Specialist Diploma in Integrated Care Management prepares participants for the role of a Care Coordinator Associate (CCA) as an alternative career progression pathway within the healthcare sector.

Course Title: Post-Diploma Certificate in Integrated Care Systems (ICS)
Course Description: To better meet the evolving healthcare needs of Singapore's greying population, the Specialist Diploma in Integrated Care Management prepares participants for the role of a Care Coordinator Associate (CCA) as an alternative career progression pathway within the healthcare sector.

Course Title: Modular Certificate in Diagnostic Clinical Chemistry (DCC)
Course Description:
"The Diploma in Applied Science (Medical Laboratory Science) is a multi-campus experience where it combines the strengths of five polytechnics’ curricula and aims to equip participants with the knowledge and technical skills to support the needs of the biomedical science industry, particularly in medical laboratory science.  This course is relevant for individuals who are keen to pursue a career in diagnostic clinical laboratories or other related fields in biomedical research and technology.

Info: This programme is offered in collaboration with other institutions. Please refer to the respective institution's webpages for updated PDC fees:
Modular Certificate in Diagnostic Clinical Chemistry (Nanyang Polytechnic)
Modular Certificate in Medical Microbiology (Temasek Polytechnic)
Modular Certificate in Molecular Diagnostics (Ngee Ann Polytechnic)
Modular Certificate in Haematology and Immuno-haematology (Singapore Polytechnic)"

Course Title: Modular Certificate in Medical Microbiology
Course Description:
This MC consists of the following modules:
Basic Microbiology
Microbiology Technology
Clinical Microbiology
Biosafety

Course Title: Modular Certificate in Medical Microbiology (MM)
Course Description: This MC is part of the Diploma in Applied Science (Medical Laboratory Science)(MLS)

Course Title: Modular Certificate in Molecular Diagnostics (MD)
Course Description: This MC is part of the Diploma in Applied Science (Medical Laboratory Science)(MLS)

Course Title: Specialist Diploma in Integrated Care Management (ICM)
Course Description: Transitional care in various healthcare institutions

Course Title: Specialist Diploma in Integrated Care Management
Basic Cardiac Life Support + Automated External Defibrillator
Course Description:
Understand Common Cardiovascular Issues and Emergency Response Plans
Perform Cardio-Pulmonary Resuscitation (CPR) on Adult, Children and Infant 
Safely use an Automated External Defibrillator on Adult Children and Infant 
Perform Conscious Foreign Body Airway Obstruction (FBAO) techniques on Adult, Children and Infant 
Perform Unconscious Foreign Body Airway Obstruction (FBAO) techniques Adult, Children and Infant

Course Title: Nervous System Series (Peripheral Nervous System) [For registered TCM practitioners only]
Course Description:
[TCM practitioners should confirm with the training provider to ensure that the course is accredited CPE points by TCMPB before signing up.]
This course will describe the anatomy, physiology and pathology of the peripheral nervous system so as to equip TCMPs with the necessary knowledge to complement TCM diagnosis and treatment.

Course Title: Lecture Series on TCM management for Geriatric Conditions [For registered TCM practitioners only]
Course Description:
[TCM practitioners should confirm with the training provider to ensure that the course is accredited CPE points by TCMPB before signing up.]

This course aims to equip TCMPs with the necessary knowledge to improve diagnosis and management of geriatric diseases such as dementia, type II diabetes and fractures.

Course Title: Muscular Function and Testing [For registered TCM practitioners only]
Course Description:
[TCM practitioners should confirm with the training provider to ensure that the course is accredited CPE points by TCMPB before signing up.]
This course will introduce the contents of 'The Muscle Book' by Klaus-Peter Valerius. This renowned literature describes in detail the various muscle testing techniques in relation to muscular anatomy and fascia. This is to equip TCMPs with the necessary knowledge to complement TCM diagnosis and treatment, especially in the field of musculoskeletal conditions and Tuina.

Course Title: WSQ - Inter-professional Collaborative Practice (IPCP)
Interprofessional Skills Based Behavioural Health for Health Professionals
Course Description:
At the end of this course, the participant will be able to: 
1. Discuss the principles of chronic diseases self-management, using behavioral change and lifestyle modification approaches to support clients to manage the medical, role behavioral and emotional consequences of living common chronic diseases. 
2. Assess clients’/patients’ level of functioning, environmental factors, health literacy and needs for holistic occupational therapy intervention. 
3. Apply principles of behaviour change and self-management support to allow clients to set and meet goals. 
4. Apply behavioural change techniques to facilitate self-management and increase activity participation in clients and their families.

Course Title: Mulligan Concept Part A and B (Classroom & Asynchronous E-Learning)
Course Description:
Part A - Upper Quarter – Peripheral Joints, Cervical and Thoracic Spines.
This two-day course presents the Mulligan approach to manual therapy for the Cervical and Thoracic spine as well as for the upper extremity. It includes lecture, patient demonstrations, and lab time to give the participants a thorough and in-depth coverage of the Mobilization With Movement approach for the upper quarter. This unique approach to manual therapy was discovered and developed by Brian Mulligan F.N.Z.S.P. (Hon), Dip. M.T. from Wellington, New Zealand. This simple yet effective manual approach addresses musculoskeletal disorders with pain free manual joint repositioning techniques for restoration of function and abolition of pain.

Part B - Lower Quarter – Peripheral Joints, Lumbar Spine and Sacroiliac joints.
This two-day course presents the Mulligan approach to manual therapy for the lumbar spine and sacroiliac joint as well as for the lower extremity. It includes lecture, patient demonstrations, and lab time to give the participants a thorough and in-depth coverage of the Mobilization With Movement approach for the lower quarter.

Course Title: Management of Stuttering in School-Aged Children
Course Description: This 2-day workshop is designed for practicing Speech Therapists with basic knowledge about stuttering. Participants will receive an update of the evidence-based treatment approaches for children 7-12 years. Hands-on activities are included to help participants practice and trouble-shoot common challenges when working with this clinical population.

Course Title: Workshop: Ultrasound Physics, Instrumentation and Quality Assurance
Course Description: #NAME?

Course Title: Pain Management Through Food and Diet
Course Description:
At the end of the 2-day module, participants will be able to:
• Understand the anatomy and physiology of pain
• Evaluate the impacts of pain on individuals and societies
• Assess dietary factors associated with pain
• Create dietary care plan for alleviating pain

Course Title: Diet and Nutrition for Mental Wellness
Course Description:
At the end of the 2-day module, participants will be able to:
• Explain mental wellness and its relationship with nervous system
• Assess the roles of diet and nutrition on mental wellness
• Create dietary care plan for specific groups of people

Course Title: Train Your Brain: How to Improve Brain Health and Thrive After 45
Course Description:
At the end of the 2-day course, participants will be able to:
•Learn the science and brain training techniques to strengthen cognitive capabilities which peak during our 40s,  50s, 60s, and even 70s.
•Improve brain health – increase the mind’s clarity and sharpness, reduce brain fog, build up brain energy, and stay sharp – effectively, making the brain smarter.
•Apply the peak cognitive abilities at work to execute strategy, better serve customers, and create value.
•Reduce the risk / delay the onset of cognitive decline or neurogenerative disease.

Course Title: Personal Wellbeing and Resilience in the Workplace Module 1: The Foundations
Course Description:
At the end of the 2-day module, participants will be able to 
•	Understand and recognise the effects of distress and burnout 
•	Identify cognitive traps and unhelpful beliefs 
•	Integrate resilience practices into their self-care routine 
•	Manage life challenges with greater mental flexibility
•	Improve personal efficacy and job satisfaction at work

Course Title: Certificate in Nutrition and Wellness: Mindfulness for Health: The Breathworks approach
Course Description:
This module aims to develop the knowledge and skills necessary for participants to: 
- Recognising different types of mindsets and emotions on their impact  
- Incorporate mindfulness living into daily life to manage our mindset and emotions in different situations 
- Learn different mindfulness tools to enhance mindfulness living. 
 Incorporate tools into daily life.

Course Title: Personal Grooming and Wellness
Course Description: This course is designed for those interested in acquiring knowledge and skills in personal grooming to enhance one’s outward appearance, personal style, self-confidence and personal branding in socio-business context. The ultimate aim of this workshop is to have the participants understand and apply the knowledge they have acquired in professional and social settings.

Course Title: Certificate in Nutrition and Wellness: Diet and Disease
Course Description: Student will learn to develop sound recommendations in the nutritional prevention of these diseases.

Course Title: Certificate in Nutrition and Wellness: Nutrition for Weight Management
Course Description:
To describe the roles of various essential nutrients on weight 
To apply Dietary Reference Intakes (DRI) in evaluating nutrition status 
To apply DRI and specific nutritional tools for analysing and planning meals 
To examine various FAD diets and their efficacy for weight management

Course Title: Advanced Certificate in Healthcare Management and Leadership Module 4: Future of Healthcare and Action Learning
Course Description: The Future of Healthcare and Action Learning Module aims to prepare participants for the future of healthcare, by introducing them to the disruptive technologies that are/ will be employed in healthcare, equip them with effective communication skills, as well as provide them with a  platform to network with healthcare leaders and to translate their learning into actionable areas that will bring real value to healthcare organisations.

Course Title: Certificate in Nutrition and Wellness: Contemporary Food
Course Description: Nutritional knowledge, with education of both the general public, and particularly health professionals is critical if we are to succeed in significantly reducing the excessive premature morbidity and mortality from our leading killer diseases - heart disease, cancer, and stroke.

Course Title: Certificate in Exercise Science: Practical Nutrition Strategies
Course Description: This module aims to provide students with a basic understanding of the science of nutrition and the importance of nutritional adequacy and balance for optimal growth and health. The Dietary sources of the major nutrients, their digestion, absorption, and metabolism are covered in the module.

Course Title: Professional Certificate in Fitness Training Module 1: Advanced Programme Design for Weight Management
Course Description: This module aims to educate participants on how to make training programmes specifically designed for weight management. This will include modifications on how to tailor programmes to help clients with common lifestyle related disease like high blood pressure and high blood sugar

Course Title: Train Your Brain: How to Improve Brain Health and Thrive After 45 (Synchronous e-Learning)
Course Description:
At the end of the 2-day course, participants will be able to:
•Learn the science and brain training techniques to strengthen cognitive capabilities which peak during our 40s,  50s, 60s, and even 70s.
•Improve brain health – increase the mind’s clarity and sharpness, reduce brain fog, build up brain energy, and stay sharp – effectively, making the brain smarter.
•Apply the peak cognitive abilities at work to execute strategy, better serve customers, and create value.
•Reduce the risk / delay the onset of cognitive decline or neurogenerative disease.

Course Title: Pain Management Through Food and Diet (Synchronous E-learning)
Course Description:
At the end of the 2-day module, participants will be able to:
• Understand the anatomy and physiology of pain
• Evaluate the impacts of pain on individuals and societies
• Assess dietary factors associated with pain
• Create dietary care plan for alleviating pain

Course Title: Graduate Certificate in Digital Health Module 4: Legal Issues & Risk Management (Synchronous E-Learning)
Course Description:
At the end of this module, participants will be able to:
• Understand their duties, obligations and liabilities based on both law and ethics.
• Examine/question the kind of data their institutions are collecting, why they need it and if they have a right to collect and use it the way they are using.
• Assess the risks involved in the deployment of digital health solutions.
• Develop a risk management framework to anticipate/transfer/mitigate the risks involved.

Course Title: Graduate Certificate in Digital Health Module 6: Healthcare Leadership for a Digital Era
Course Description:
At the end of this module, participants will be able to:
• Recognize that organization excellence is a concerted effort. 
• Understand who owns leadership and why traditional leadership practices won’t work. 
• Develop and build informal means of influence through technology and networks at all levels to facilitate purposeful collaboration across domains and areas. 
• Encourage and harness distributed leadership for wider employee engagement to build a leaner and more agile organization culture for exceptional organizational performance.

Course Title: Advanced Certificate in Healthcare Management and Leadership Module 3: Key Healthcare Operational Competencies
Course Description: The Key Healthcare Operational Competencies Module aims to equip participants with the key administration/ operational skills that would be helpful to them in most healthcare settings.

Course Title: Advanced Certificate in Healthcare Management and Leadership Module 3: Key Healthcare Operational Competencies (Synchronous E-Learning)
Course Description: The Key Healthcare Operational Competencies Module aims to equip participants with the key administration/ operational skills that would be helpful to them in most healthcare settings.

Course Title: Advanced Certificate in Healthcare Management and Leadership Module 4: Future of Healthcare and Action Learning (Synchronous E-Learning)
Course Description: The Future of Healthcare and Action Learning Module aims to prepare participants for the future of healthcare, by introducing them to the disruptive technologies that are/ will be employed in healthcare, equip them with effective communication skills, as well as provide them with a  platform to network with healthcare leaders and to translate their learning into actionable areas that will bring real value to healthcare organisations.

Course Title: Advanced Certificate in Healthcare Management and Leadership Module 1: The Healthcare Operating Environment
Course Description: The Healthcare Operating Environment Module aims to provide participants with a useful window into the world of healthcare at the local and regional level.

Course Title: Advanced Certificate in Healthcare Management and Leadership Module 1: The Healthcare Operating Environment (Synchronous E-Learning)
Course Description: The Healthcare Operating Environment Module aims to provide participants with a useful window into the world of healthcare at the local and regional level.

Course Title: Advanced Certificate in Healthcare Management and Leadership Module 2: Healthcare People and Culture
Course Description: The Healthcare People and Culture Module aims to provide participants with an appreciation of the healthcare industry work norms, environment and culture, which may differ from organisations with distinct command and control structure/processes.

Course Description: The Healthcare People and Culture Module aims to provide participants with an appreciation of the healthcare industry work norms, environment and culture, which may differ from organisations with distinct command and control structure/processes.

Course Title: Diet and Nutrition for Mental Wellness (Synchronous E-learning)
Course Description:
At the end of the 2-day module, participants will be able to:
• Explain mental wellness and its relationship with nervous system
• Assess the roles of diet and nutrition on mental wellness
• Create dietary care plan for specific groups of people

Course Title: Professional Certificate in Fitness Training Module 2: Advanced Fitness Training for Sporting Activity
Course Description: This module aims to educate participants on the development of sports specific training programmes. This will include the thought process and key considerations for designing  programme from speed sports like rugby, to endurance sports like triathlons, as well as sports with a mix of speed and endurance like martial arts. There will also be an emphasis on the trainign needed to lower the risk of common injuries in these sports

Course Title: Personal Wellbeing and Resilience in the Workplace Module 1: The Foundations (Synchronous e-Learning)
Course Description:
At the end of the 2-day module, participants will be able to 
•	Understand and recognise the effects of distress and burnout 
•	Identify cognitive traps and unhelpful beliefs 
•	Integrate resilience practices into their self-care routine 
•	Manage life challenges with greater mental flexibility
•	Improve personal efficacy and job satisfaction at work

Course Title: Graduate Certificate in Digital Health Module 6: Healthcare Leadership for a Digital Era (Synchronous E-Learning)
Course Description:
At the end of this module, participants will be able to:
• Recognize that organization excellence is a concerted effort.
• Understand who owns leadership and why traditional leadership practices won’t work.
• Develop and build informal means of influence through technology and networks at all levels to facilitate purposeful collaboration across domains and areas.
• Encourage and harness distributed leadership for wider employee engagement to build a leaner and more agile organization culture for exceptional organizational performance.

Course Title: Food Safety and Nutrition Course for Food and Beverage Industry
Course Description:
By the end of the module, learners will be able to:
Appreciate the roles of nutrition and food safety in promoting and maintaining human health•Plan and create safe and nutritious meals for consumers with different dietary needs•Identify and analyze food safety risks•Propose methods to prevent, reduce, or eliminate food safety risks

Course Title: Food Safety and Nutrition Course for Food and Beverage Industry (Synchronous e-Learning)
Course Description:
By the end of the module, learners will be able to:
Appreciate the roles of nutrition and food safety in promoting and maintaining human health•Plan and create safe and nutritious meals for consumers with different dietary needs•Identify and analyze food safety risks•Propose methods to prevent, reduce, or eliminate food safety risks

Course Title: Certificate in Nutrition and Wellness: Active Aging
Course Description: Students will study nutrition for the elderly, functional lifestyles, chronic disease care, osteoporosis and falls safety, diabetes, preventative health screening, and exercise prescription. They will also learn about the methods of increasing health literacy in the ageing population.

Course Title: Personal Wellbeing and Resilience in the Workplace Module 2: Psychological First Aid Training for Peer Support (Synchronous e-Learning)
Course Description:
At the end of the 2-day module, participants will be able to:
• Recognise and approach persons in distress with confidence 
• Apply Psychological First Aid (PFA) concepts and communication skills to offer practical help 
• Set mindful boundaries and access support early 
• Strengthen employee relations and engagement

Course Title: Professional Certificate in Fitness Training Module 3: Advanced Programme Design for Injury Prevention and Management
Course Description: This module aims to educate participants on the application of various injury prevention and management strategies. The goal is to enable them to help their future clients stay injury free so they can enjoy as many sporting and exercise activities with minimal pain or injury risk. They will be able to help a wide range of people from athetes to active aging seniors

Course Title: Professional Certificate in Fitness Training Module 4: Advanced Lifestyle Habit Development
Course Description: This module aims to educate participants on how to develop a multi-faceted lifestyle plan for their clients. The goal is to learn the underlying biocehincal and physiological principles behind these lifestyle changes.

Course Title: Professional Certificate in Fitness Training Module 5: Fitness Training for Special Populations (seniors, children, pregnancy, chronic illness/ joint pain)
Course Description: This module aims to educate participants on the considerations needed to safely and successfully train different types of clients. They will be taught the principles of what to adjust when a client is a senior, a child, pregnant, or has some kind of chronic illness.

Course Title: Professional Certificate in Fitness Training Module 6: Fitness and Wellness Coaching Methodology
Course Description: This module aims to educate participants on the ability to coach others effecitively. They will be taught how to assess clients current health status, and determine which areas need improvement first. They will also be taught different coaching styles which can be applied to improve compliance of clients. There will also be practical application of coaching in multiple settings

Course Description: This course aims to train new entry opticians in the technical skills & professional knowledge required in the field of Opticianry, which includes dispensing and refraction.

Course Description: This is a module under the Specialist Diploma in Nutrition & Exercise Science.

Course Title: Specialist Diploma in Nutrition & Exercise Science
Course Description:
1.1	The Specialist Diploma in Nutrition & Exercise Science aims to provide the participants with the understanding of the science of nutrition and the importance of nutritional adequacy and balance for optimal growth and health as well as the role of nutrition in disease prevention and management.

The course will also provide an overview of the evolving field of exercise and sports nutrition, highlighting key principles and practices. Additionally, participants will gain insight into the physiological mechanisms that govern the human body's functioning during exercise. 

The objective of the course is to provide participants with an integrated education focusing on nutrition and exercise science so that participants can acquire a holistic education in health and fitness. It also enhances occupational and personal competencies for better career enhancement in the health and fitness industry.

1.2	The Specialist Diploma in Nutrition & Exercise Science focuses on theoretical knowledge as well as practical applications nutrition and exercise science concepts relevant to the health and fitness industry. Participants will also gain hands-on experience in laboratory work and practical sessions. Individuals who are working in the health and fitness industry and who are looking to deepen their nutrition and exercise science knowledge will benefit from the course. It equips the participants with equivalent roles with the knowledge and skills required to undertake new roles that include Assistant Food Technologist, Healthcare Assistant, Therapy Assistant and Patient Service Executive in Healthcare and Food Manufacturing sector of the Skills Framework.

Course Title: (SCTP) Certificate in Healthcare Administration
Course Description:
As Singapore edges towards our status as a super-aged society, the healthcare industry would need to grow manpower exponentially, and at the same time, experiment new operating models to leverage ancillary workers. Amid the competition with other industries for talent, the programme intends to draw aspiring healthcare workers (such as mid-career entrants) by familiarising them with the Healthcare landscape to increasing their preparedness and resilience levels as they transit into a new undertaking. This will increase a ready pool of workers for the healthcare sector. 

The certification programme consists of 10-day workshop:
• SCH101 Overview of Singapore Healthcare Landscape
• SCH102 Healthcare Capacity and Healthcare Professionals
• SCH103 SG Vision and Healthcare Financing
• SCH104 Emerging Healthcare Trends
• SCH105 Care Givers and Mental Health
• SCH106 Career Transition into Healthcare Sector
• SCH107 Population Health
• SCH108 Theory and Behaviour Norms in Healthcare
• SCH109 Project Management in Healthcare
• SCH201 Healthcare System Applied Project

Course Title: Introduction to Psychology 1
Course Description: This Introductory Psychology module introduces the basic principles, concepts and theories of the following key areas; Research Methods, Biological Psychology, and Cognitive Psychology. Psychology is fundamentally the science of behaviour and mental processes, and psychologists use scientific methods to test their ideas empirically. A better understanding of human behaviour can be achieved by studying the biological basis of behaviour and the various cognitive functions involved. Such core concepts will be covered by means of pre-recorded lectures, interactive and instructive online resources that supplement the textbook and study guide, multimedia tutorials, videos, simulations, animations, tests and quizzes that make learning accessible and stimulating.

Course Title: Introduction to Psychology 2
Course Description: This Introductory Psychology module introduces the basic principles, concepts and theories of Developmental Psychology, Learning, Personality, Social Psychology, Abnormal Psychology and Health Psychology. The human life-span will be explored with reference to theories of physical, perceptual, cognitive, intellectual, language, moral, social, and emotional development and how they might be influenced by nature versus nurture. The learning journey will include pre-recorded lectures, interactive and instructive online resources that supplement the textbook and study guide, multimedia tutorials, videos, simulations, animations, tests and quizzes that will enhance one's knowledge and mastery of the key concepts covered in this course.

Course Title: Enhancing the Health Environment at Workplaces
Course Description: Aligning to WSH 2028 Strategies, the inter-relationship between safety and health of employees has to reinforce as an integrated approach in addressing work, safety and health. With focus on the health status of the workforce, this allows the identification of health issues that may impact work and safety such that a customised health intervention programmes can be implemented appropriately at the workplace.

Course Title: Skills in Psychology
Course Description: This is a unique course that focuses on bridging the gap between theory to practice to equip psychology undergraduate students with both foundational and functional competencies required to practice in the field of psychology. This course focuses on the science-practitioner model, emphasizing the links between science and practice of psychology.

Course Title: Stroke and Stroke Sequel Management - TCM Perspective for Health and Wellness Practitioners
Course Description:
Course objectives: 
 This course will let the participants know the etiology and pathogenesis of Stroke, Understand the different TCM management approaches in the area of prevention, rehabilitation, and recovery management. Learn approaches such as the use of TCM Acupressure Massage, Medicated Diets and the practice of dynamic breathing techniques suitable for Stroke Rehabilitation and Sequel.

Course Title: Sub-Health Management - TCM Perspective for Health and Wellness Practitioners
Course Description:
Course objectives: 
Sub-health condition as defined by the WHO (World Health Organization) as a state between health and disease or a gray state of health, when all necessary physical and chemical indexes are tested negative by medical equipment, things seem normal but the person experiences all kind of discomfiture and even pain. The course will is to share with the Health and Wellness practitioners, the Chinese Medicine approaches in addressing conditions such as Insomnia, Chronic Fatigue, Perspiration or hyperdydrohesis, and GERD.

Course Title: Women's Health - The TCM Perspective for Health and Wellness Practitioners
Course Description:
Course Objective:
Every woman's life is a complete beautiful cycle, makes up of sequence of intertwining life stages, which was carefully and well documented in the Chinese medical literatures. The objective of the course is to share with the Health and Wellness practitioners, the Chinese Medicine approaches in addressing Women's Health issues, during different life's stages, the Menstrual Cycle, Pregnancy, Menopause and corresponding syndromes face by Women such as Emotional Management.

Course Title: Pain Management - TCM Perspective for Health and Wellness Practitioners
Course Description:
Course Objective:
Pain, while not comfortable, is a protective reaction and warning signals to let us know that something needs correction. This course will let the participants know the etiology and pathogenesis of different kind of Pain from the TCM Perspective. Management of Rheumatic diseases are characterized by inflammation affecting supporting structures of the body will be discussed and learners will learn approaches such as the use of TCM Acupressure Massage, Medicated Diets in the management of Pain.

Course Title: Essential Wellness Skills for Ageing Population
Course Description:
Course Objective:
After working with 15,000 clients from 30 countries over three decades, the trainer has identified most common health challenges due to work-life environment and this program he will share with health and wellness practitioner what these common challenges are and outlines strategies to deal with these issues. This course is designed for those who are involve in providing health and wellness care activities, and looking for in-depth understanding of functional wellness, movement, nutrition and in their activities. To help manage issues arise from the aging population and to raise their standard and quality of service to their respective clients.

Course Title: Gamification in Healthcare driven by AI, IOT and VR
Course Description:
This comprehensive course is designed to furnish participants with essential skills in leveraging gamification, artificial intelligence (AI), and the Internet of Things (IoT) technology in healthcare. Participants will gain a profound understanding of these emergent technologies and develop the expertise to construct an interactive rehabilitation game employing smart technologies.
 
Through intensive hands-on sessions, participants will learn the process of data acquisition using IoT sensors. The course will guide them through the utilization of cloud platforms for secure data storage and instruct on the integration of Virtual Reality (VR) for remote visualization and monitoring of patient progress, fostering a disciplined and serious approach to healthcare applications.
 
A key emphasis will be placed on cultivating the ability to discern trends through data analytics and AI, with the ultimate goal of enhancing patient care. This course is for serious practitioners who are keen to understand and implement new technologies in healthcare.

Course Title: (SCTP) Advanced Certificate in Basic Community Care Management
Course Description:
The learning objective of the (SCTP) Non-WSQ Advance Certificate in Basic Community Care Management is to train individuals who have no prior experience in the sector but are interested to join the Social Service sector, particularly but not limited to, the Community Care spaces like Senior Activity Centres / Active Aging Centres / Active Aging Care Hubs, to work with volunteers & clients with professionalism.

This course is administered by Hua Mei Training Academy, an initiative of Tsao Foundation.

Course Title: Foundation Knowledge for Effective Care Delivery (ACBCCM)
Course Description:
The required skills and knowledge for this course are as follows:
? Education:
? be able to listen and speak English at a proficiency level equivalent to the Employability Skills System (ESS) Literacy Level 4
? be able to read and write English at a proficiency level equivalent to the Employability Skills System (ESS) Literacy Level 4
? be able to manipulate numbers at a proficiency level equivalent to Employability Skills System (ESS) Numeracy Level 4
? have basic languages / dialects relevant to the target client group. ? Have prior working experience with older adults will be an added advantage
? Be motivated to learn and have positive attitude towards work and commitments.
? Have interest in community and social work.

Required years of experience in relevant Domain
- No prior experience required.

Course Title: Identifying the Bio-Psycho-Social (BPS) Risk and Red flags in elders
Course Description:
The objective of the course is to equip learners with the skills and knowledge to Identify Bio-Psycho-Social (BPS) Risk and Red Flags in the older person, while at the same time know and familiarise with the types of Elder Abuse screening tools and reporting procedures for elder abuse.

This course is administered by Hua Mei Training Academy, an initiative of Tsao Foundation

Course Title: Practical Application to Work with High-Risk Elders with Mental Health Conditions
Course Description: The objective of the course is to equip learners with the skills and knowledge to understand the process of care for older adults with mental health issues and recognize the various community resources available to support these older adults and enable them to age well in the community.

Course Title: Caring for Persons with Dementia
Course Description:
The objective of this course is to equip learners with the skills and knowledge about the fundamentals of both the Person-Centred Care approach and Palliative care/end-of-life approach in caring for persons with Dementia. Also, with the use of effective communication, to recognise the behavioural change, factors that can affect interactions, and the importance of social activities or engagements for Persons with dementia. Not to be missed, learners will also pick up skills to identify the various ways to prevent and cope with personal stress and burnout.

This course is administered by Hua Mei Training Academy, an initiative of Tsao Foundation.

Course Title: Working with Families
Course Description: The objective of the course is to equip learners with the skills and knowledge to understand the purpose of working with family, relationship patterns, its history and impact on current relationships; and further make use of Genogram as a graphical representation of family relationships to understand family structure to better support the older person in need.

Course Title: Working with Teams
Course Description:
The objective of the course is to equip learners with the skills and knowledge to understand the purpose of working with Teams, its principles, and the key characteristics of an effective team. In addition, learn how to relate effective communication with the participation of clients and families to maximize process and outcome. Not to be missed to understand how interprofessional education, health informatics, and technology can contribute towards a positive meaningful work culture.

This course is administered by Hua Mei Training Academy, an initiative of Tsao Foundation

Course Title: Life Review Skills
Course Description: The objective of the course is to equip learners with the skills and knowledge of how people recount and re-evaluate their personal life history and in so doing reframe their present difficulties in a way that enables them to make therapeutic changes to their thoughts, feelings or behavior, so as to raise client’s self-esteem to adapt to life changes

Course Title: (SCTP) Higher Certificate in Community Healthcare
Course Description:
The learning objective of the (SCTP) Non-WSQ Higher Certificate in Community Healthcare is to train individuals who have no prior experience in the sector but are interested to join the Social Service sector, particularly but not limited to, the Community Care spaces like Senior Activity Centres / Active Aging Centres / Active Aging Care Hubs, to work with volunteers & clients with professionalism.

With the practical phase, it consolidates the learning and translates to practice in targeted and specific areas which will be relevant and current to the learners’ job tasks and responsibilities.

Full Qualification consists of four core modules, three elective modules and attachment.

This course is administered by Hua Mei Training Academy, an initiative of Tsao Foundation

Course Title: CoC in Conducting Health Education for the Incontinent Client
Course Description: This course aims to equip nurses with the knowledge and skills to conduct a health teaching for clients with incontinence.

Course Title: CoC in Performing Diabetic Foot Screening and Education on Diabetic Foot Care
Course Description: This course aims to equip nurses with the knowledge and skills to perform diabetic foot screening and conduct health teaching on diabetic foot care.

Course Title: Peripheral Intra-Venous Cannulation Course
Course Description: Equips learners with the proper skillsets to be competent cannulation practitioners.

Course Title: Post-Diploma Certificate in Practice of Nephro-Urology Nursing 2 (part of Advanced Diploma in Nursing (Nephro-Urology))
Course Description:
Post-Diploma Certificate in Practice of Nephro-Urology Nursing 2 (PDC4 of Advanced Diploma in Nursing (Nephro-Urology)) aims to prepare the learners on the following:

1.  utilise the skills in assessment, planning and delivery of nursing care, problem-solving approach, develop critical thinking in the management of client who require acute dialysis treatment and community health care.   
2.  develop deeper understanding to the various types of nephro-urological disorders.

Course Title: Management of Temporomandibular Disorders
Course Description:
Oral Healthcare Professionals play an essential role in the prevention, diagnosis, and management of Temporomandibular Disorders (TMD). The comprehensive management of the patient, utilizing the best evidence-based assessment and procedures will be presented. The course will allow them to provide an accurate diagnosis, recognize the more complex Temporomandibular Disorders (TMD) pain conditions, and initiate referrals to appropriate experts in managing such patients.

Hands-on practical assessment of the stomatognathic system will further their clinical skills in the management of patients in their daily clinical practice. The different treatment modalities, including interdisciplinary modalities will be discussed. The program adopts a biopsychosocial model for the diagnosis and management of Temporomandibular Disorders (TMD).

Course Title: Health and MedTech Project Management Plus (Classroom and Asynchronous e-Learning)
Course Description:
By the end of this module, participants should be able to:

•	Formulate medical device development Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) and schedule 
•	Analyze, pre-empt and manage project risks 
•	Develop a detailed and effective resource allocation plan for all health & medtech project activities and implement within agreed cost, timescale and resources 
•	Communicate effectively amongst multi-disciplinary internal and external stakeholders on a regular basis

Course Title: Modular Certificate in Haematology and Immuno-haematology (HIH)
Course Description:
"The Diploma in Applied Science (Medical Laboratory Science) is a multi-campus experience where it combines the strengths of five polytechnics’ curricula and aims to equip participants with the knowledge and technical skills to support the needs of the biomedical science industry, particularly in medical laboratory science.  This course is relevant for individuals who are keen to pursue a career in diagnostic clinical laboratories or other related fields in biomedical research and technology.

Info: This programme is offered in collaboration with other institutions. Please refer to the respective institution's webpages for updated PDC fees:
Modular Certificate in Diagnostic Clinical Chemistry (Nanyang Polytechnic)
Modular Certificate in Medical Microbiology (Temasek Polytechnic)
Modular Certificate in Molecular Diagnostics (Ngee Ann Polytechnic)
Modular Certificate in Haematology and Immuno-haematology (Singapore Polytechnic)"

Course Title: Modular Certificate in Haematology and Immuno-haematology
Course Description:
This MC consists of the following modules:
Immunology
Blood Banking
Haematology (renamed)

Course Title: Nervous System Series (Central Nervous System) [For registered TCM practitioners only]
Course Description:
[TCM practitioners should confirm with the training provider to ensure that the course is accredited CPE points by TCMPB before signing up.]
This course will describe the anatomy, physiology and pathology of the central nervous system so as to equip TCMPs with the necessary knowledge to complement TCM diagnosis and treatment.

Course Title: Upper Limb Plain Film Radiography for Occupational Therapists
Course Description:
At the end of the course, learners will be able to:
1.	List the indications and contraindications for plain film radiography for upper limb musculoskeletal system 
2.	Recognise normal and some common pathological/degenerative changes in plain film radiography for upper limb musculoskeletal system 
3.	List the differences in plain film radiography findings between the paediatric and adult population
4.	Systematically evaluate plain film radiography and reports for upper limb musculoskeletal system 
5.	Differentiate and interpret normal and pathological findings in plain film radiography and correlate the findings with clinical examination/observation
6.	Integrate plain film radiography findings into safe and effective clinical practice.

Course Title: Personal Wellbeing and Resilience in the Workplace Module 2: Psychological First Aid Training for Peer Support
Course Description:
At the end of the 2-day module, participants will be able to:

- Health Psychology
- Sociology of Health and Social Care 
- Biopsychosocial Aspects of Ageing 
- Active Ageing 
- Project on Health Across Lifespan / OJT

Course Description:
This MC consists of the following modules:

Course Title: Care Assistance (Basic Care Attendant)
Course Description: This WSQ Care Assistance course is tailored to equip family members, domestic helpers and those who intend to become professional Basic Care Attendants, Caregivers and Healthcare Assistance. Through classroom interactive teaching, practice and assessment, participants can gain fundamental caregiving knowledge and skillsets to provide care and comfort to the care recipient without compromising safety.
Training Company: Singapore Rescue Training Centre Pte. Ltd.

Course Title: Wound Management in Nursing (Classroom and Synchronous)
Course Description:
1.	Relate the basic anatomy and physiology of integumentary system to process of wound healing.
2.	Discuss the types and classification of wounds and the factors which delay/complicate wound healing.
3.	Select appropriate wound products for simple wound dressing by adhering to the principles of aseptic technique and assist in stoma care drainage of pouch.
4.	Assist in the preparation, performance of wound dressing and post procedure process according to standard operating procedures and infection control practice and prevention guidelines
Training Company: Hmi Institute Of Health Sciences Pte. Ltd.

Course Title: Post-Diploma Certificate in Principles and Science of Medical-Surgical Nursing (part of Advanced Diploma in Nursing (Medical-Surgical))
Course Description:
Post-Diploma Certificate in Principles and Science of Medical-Surgical Nursing (PDC1 of Advanced Diploma in Nursing (Medical-Surgical)) aims to prepare the learners in the following:

1.  study key concepts in pharmacology which will form the foundation to understanding the rationale for pharmacological management of clients with complex health problems.
2.  develop skills and knowledge in history taking and physical examination by integrating assessment findings to make appropriate clinical decisions.
3.  apply appropriate evidence-based therapeutic regimes and individualized nursing interventions to clients who are experiencing potential/actual life-threatening conditions, or undergoing surgery for different types of cardiovascular disorders. 
4.  apply appropriate evidence-based therapeutic regimes and individualized nursing interventions to clients who are experiencing potential/actual life-threatening conditions, or undergoing surgery for different types of respiratory and neurological disorders.

Course Description: Upon completion of the course, learners will understand how to build mental resilience capabilities within workplaces via peer-to-peer mental resilience support. Learners will have the knowledge and skills to apply comprehensive mental health awareness, self-care techniques, peer support, and well-being facilitation at their workplace.

Course Title: Developing a Mental Resilience Strategy for Workplaces
Course Description: Upon completion of the course, learners will build up the foundation identifying various of stress and practice appropriate, root causes of the stress and signs of burnout and learn how to formulate and strategist well-being plan for team or organisation

Course Title: Enhancing Wellness and Resilience: Strategies for Personal Empowerment
Course Description: Upon completion of the course, learners will build up the foundation identifying various of stress and the causes of it. Learners will go exercise emotional and practical support with the organisation, identifying one’s strength and weakness to develop coping strategies and handle stress situation, further re-frame negative experience as a good learning lesson for self-improvement.

Course Title: Mastering Your Mental Compass: A guide to Self-Awareness and Growth
Course Description: Upon completion of the course, learners will understand how to recognise personal strengths and weaknesses for growth mindset and basic mindfulness and self-awareness. Learners will have the knowledge and skills to do self-assessment and self-monitoring, they will also recognise one’s limitations and boundaries so as to seek professional help when needed.

Course Title: Standard First Aid with CPR AED
Course Description: The Standard First Aid with CPR and AED Certification Course is a comprehensive training programme that covers critical aspects of first aid. It is designed to equip learners with in-depth knowledge and skills in rendering appropriate first aid treatment.
Training Company: Academy Of Sports And Exercise Science Pte. Ltd.

Course Title: STANDARD FIRST AID PROVIDER CERTIFICATION (BLENDED LEARNING)
Course Description: Upon completing this course, learners will have the knowledge and application skills to make Identify, assess and manage immediate hazards to the health and safety of self and others and provide first aid assistance, Cardiopulmonary Resuscitation (CPR) and Automated External Defibrillator (AED) in accordance with established first aid principles and procedures. Increase awareness of possible accidents, identify accidents with casualties and optimize health and performance at the workplace

Course Title: BCLS+AED PROVIDER CERTIFICATION (Asynchronous E-learning and Classroom)
Course Description: Upon completing this course, learners will have the knowledge and application skills to recognise critical signs of emergencies, activate emergency medical services, manage patients requiring emergency care, and provide immediate basic cardiac life support. Abilities in performing Cardiopulmonary Resuscitation (CPR) and skills in using Automated External Defibrillator (AED) in accordance with established first aid principles and procedures. Increase awareness of identifying casualties with cardiac arrest and optimize health and performance at the workplace.

Course Title: Dementia Care at Home
Course Description:
•	To gain an introductory awareness to dementia, learn the signs and symptoms of dementia and basic concepts of how to care for a person with dementia. 
•	To gain basic understanding and general knowledge of the emotional and behavioural changes of a person with dementia at different stages of dementia and how to manage these behaviours.

Course Title: ABCs of Caregiving Set C

Course Title: Dementia Care
Course Description:
1.	To equip caregiver with skills and knowledge for care recipient with dementia
2.	To be able to understand different stages of dementia
3.	To be able to communicate and manage care recipient suffering from dementia
4.	To be able to assess risks at home
