This course will benefit the family members of the elderly in taking care of their seniors at home, as well as supervising and guiding the migrant domestic workers in their home setting. The course also aims to enable caregivers to take care of their own emotional and psychological needs, as well as that of the elderly.

Course Title: Caregiver Training Course for Bed-bound and Wheelchair-bound Elderly
Course Description: This caregiver training course for bed-bound and wheelchair-bound elderly is designed to equip participants with basic knowledge and fundamental concepts to provide home-based and personalised care to bed-bound and wheelchair-bound elderly in their home. It combined both knowledge and skills to assist and provide them with basic healthcare and first aid, as well as to manage home-based healthcare emergencies. The course aims to enable caregivers to take care of their own needs, as well as that of the elderly.

Course Title: Caregiver Training Course for Ambulating and Wheelchair-bound Elderly
Course Description: The caregiver training course for ambulating and wheelchair-bound elderly is designed to equip participants with basic knowledge and fundamental concepts to provide safe home-based and personalised care to ambulating, and wheelchair-bound elderly in their home. This Course combined both knowledge and skills to enable caregivers to assist and provide them with basic healthcare and first aid, as well as to manage home-based healthcare emergencies. The course also focused in enabling caregivers to take care of their own needs, as well as that of the elderly.

Course Title: Diabetes Mellitus & Blood Glucose Monitoring Course
Course Description: This course aims to prepare participants with basic knowledge and skills to provide personalised care to home-based clients who requires care for diabetes mellitus. This course would equip participants with skills to monitor blood glucose level and to administer subcutaneous insulin injection for client when needed.

Course Title: Effective Communication with the Elderly
Course Description: The course aims to provide participants with essential knowledge and fundamental concepts necessary to communicate effectively with the elderly to deliver good care in a home setting, addressing their safety and healthcare needs. This course integrates theoretical understanding with practical skills, empowering caregivers to deliver the correct communication skills in addressing emotional needs of the elderly and build relationships to provide daily physical and emotional support. This course will also benefit migrant domestic helpers in self-grooming and personal hygiene to enable them to perform their caregiving duties more effectively.

Course Title: Nasogastric Tube (NGT) & Percutaneous Endoscopic Gastrostomy (PEG) Care Course
Course Description: This course aims to prepare participants with basic knowledge and fundamental skills to provide home-based and personalised care to clients who requires nasogastric tube (NGT) and/or percutaneous endoscopic gastrostomy (PEG) tube care.  This course includes introduction to the different types of gastrostomy tube, recognition of complication of having these tubes and how to feed enteral feeds via NGT and PEG.

Course Title: WSQ HIGHER CERTIFICATE IN HEALTHCARE (NURSING)
Course Description:
The Health Care Assistant Programme is designed to equip students with the essential skills and knowledge required to provide high-quality care and support to patients in various healthcare settings. This comprehensive programme blends theoretical knowledge with practical hands-on experience, preparing students for a rewarding career in the healthcare sector. 

Applicable to all healthcare workers.

Course Title: Phonics for Reading (Online)
Course Description: This workshop will allow participants to understand the importance of phonics instructions and phonological awareness, especially for learners with specific learning difficulties such as dyslexia. Participants will be more aware of the various letter-sounds in the English language and the components of good phonics and phonological awareness instruction through a multi-sensory approach. This will equip participants in carrying out these activities in both home and school settings.

Course Title: Supporting Your Child in Mathematics (Online)
Course Description: This workshop allows participants to understand the nature of Mathematics and why learners with dyslexia struggle with Mathematics. Participants will be more aware of the theoretical and practical implications of Mathematics and gain practical strategies that can be applied in both home and school settings to support learners with dyslexia in their learning of Mathematics.

Course Title: Understanding children with attentional and hyperactivity issues (Online)
Course Description: Learners with learning difficulties often have one or more comorbidities. Attention-related challenges are one of the more commonly seen symptoms among children with dyslexia. Attention is primary to all learning and communication. In this workshop, we hope to be able to provide a broader understanding of attentional challenges, impulse control and other executive function difficulties, alongside ADHD.

Course Title: Clever but Cluttered: Developing Executive Function Skills (Online)
Course Description: This workshop is targeted at parents and teachers who are interested in helping learners with Specific Learning Difficulties (SpLD) to become more effective in managing their studies. SpLD learners tend to struggle more with school work due to the nature of their learning needs. They can also have co-occurring issues such as Attention Deficit which leads to poor focus and disorganisation. This workshop thus aims to help these parents and teachers understand the role of a key set of mental skills called executive function and how applying executive function strategies can help SpLD learners be more effective as students.

Course Title: Coping with Spelling (Online)
Course Description: This workshop will give participants a better understanding of why learners with dyslexia find spelling difficult. Participants will be more aware of the skills and knowledge needed to spell English words, and the underlying principles of spelling instruction. Participants will gain skills in supporting learners with dyslexia to spell phonetic and non-phonetic words through a multi-sensory approach.

Course Title: Developing Your Child’s Comprehension Skills (Online)
Course Description: The workshop aims to enable participants to explore the process of comprehension, as well as develop strategies and instructions that will aid the learner in deciphering a long text. Participants will gain a deeper understanding of the difficulties that learners with dyslexia face incomprehension and be exposed to various strategies that are effective in building comprehension skills such as vocabulary building and recognising different types of questions.

Course Title: Mindful Moments - Mindfulness for Children with Attentional and Impulsivity Issues (Online)
Course Description: Participants will be proficient in implementing tailored mindfulness practices that enhance focus, promote calmness, and foster a supportive environment conducive to managing the unique challenges of life. Participants will also gain insight into integrating these practices into daily routines, ensuring sustainable benefits and improved behavioral outcomes for children with attentional and impulsivity issues.

Course Title: Supporting Your Child in Chinese (Online)
Course Description: This workshop aims to help attendees understand the difficulties dyslexic children face when learning the Chinese Language as a second language, as well as the nature and structure of Chinese characters. Strategies for parents and caregivers to support their children with dyslexia in learning Chinese will be shared in this workshop.

Course Title: Supporting Your Child in Math Problem-Solving Using Heuristics (Online)
Course Description: This workshop will cover both the theoretical and practical aspects of supporting learners with learning difficulties in solving Maths word problems using heuristics and other problem-solving approaches. Participants will become aware of how the Mathematical Problem Solving Framework shapes the Maths curriculum and the struggles that learners with learning difficulties have with Maths word problems. Beyond theoretical content, the workshop will also introduce participants to the various heuristics and problem-solving approaches needed to solve common Maths word problems at the upper primary level. Participants will also be given some hands-on practice on how to apply these strategies to solve similar word problems.

Course Title: Be Your Child’s Social Skills Coach (Online)
Course Description: The participants will learn practical strategies to help children learn specific social skills useful for daily interactions with peers and adults in familiar settings. Participants will also learn ways to talk that will support the development of self-awareness and perspective-taking in children by developing their vocabulary for describing their own and other people’s actions, thoughts, emotions and intentions.

(AED)

Course Title: (eCornell) Building Health Care Compliance Programs
Course Description: Throughout this course, you will explore various laws and regulations that health care organizations are required to follow in order to meet compliance obligations. Since there are many ways you could unintentionally fall out of compliance, you will discover strategies for averting risk by anticipating key areas of concern. By examining the consequences of noncompliance, you will recognize how such violations can be costly. As you uncover seven myths about health care compliance, it will be clear that laws and regulations apply to all organizations.

Course Title: (eCornell) Changing Organizational Culture in Healthcare
Course Description: In this course, professionals will reset their thinking around how best to understand, measure, implement, and lead successful change initiatives

Course Title: (eCornell) Design Considerations for Healthcare Facilities
Course Description: In this course, you will explore how to access and analyze design research to evaluate the world around you in order to create environments that support health and wellness

Course Title: (eCornell) Effective Process Management in Healthcare
Course Description: In this course, you'll explore the concept of process thinking and access several reusable tools to help you develop and improve processes at your organization

Course Title: (eCornell) Medical Billing and Contracting Liabilities
Course Description: While healthcare involves a noble mission, it is nonetheless a business, and just like any other business operation, there is a potential for fraud. In this course, you will focus on what are commonly called fraud and abuse laws. You will discover the legal protections for the doctor-patient relationships in state law, such as doctor-patient confidentiality, as well as the doctor's fiduciary duties to the patient. At the federal level, you will examine fraud and abuse statutes that carve out particular types of transactions with profit motives to penalize. These are transactions where providers submit false claims to the government for payment and where providers receive kickbacks in exchange for ordering care or making referrals.

Course Title: (eCornell) Obligations to Patients
Course Description: Healthcare organizations and their compliance departments spend a significant amount of time focusing on interactions with individuals. These diverse groups include everyone from patients to human research participants, along with many others, and these interactions necessitate different types of consent to do things like successfully provide service, obtain and use information, and engage in research activities. Throughout this course, you will explore some of the obligations that apply to healthcare organizations regarding these interactions with individual people.

Course Title: (eCornell) Quality and Process Improvement Metrics and Strategies for Healthcare
Course Description: In this course, you'll explore how to measure quality and diagnose what's causing issues with quality in your organization. You'll also explore methods for improving processes while maintaining quality at your organization

Course Title: (eCornell) Public Health Foundations
Course Description: Public health professionals work to prevent or reduce disability and disease in communities. Throughout history, public health interventions have allowed societies to improve the length and quality of life. This course offers an overview of what public health is and fosters your understanding of the various factors that impact health as well as the role you can play in improving health outcomes. You will gain the opportunity to build your knowledge, skills, and confidence as you take a more active role in community health efforts as a member of the public health workforce.

Course Title: (eCornell) Public Health Preparedness
Course Description: You will start by understanding the risks then, using a case-study approach, examine some of the main environmental issues that affect our health. Looking back at history as well as into the future, you will share ideas for your role in addressing these problems

Course Title: (eCornell) Supporting Public Health Behaviors
Course Description: Practicing public health involves working directly with people to advocate for and support the behaviors that will prevent disease, promote health, and prolong life among individuals and the population as a whole. Through this course, you have the opportunity to enhance your ability to facilitate behavior change by studying behavior principles. You begin by examining two important behavior models and considering several different factors that influence behaviors. You will then review key components of effective public health communication, including audience analysis and accessible messaging. Finally, you will explore and practice critical strategies for developing trust and meaningful connections with people of other cultures and social identities.

Course Title: (eCornell) Using Public Health Data for Action
Course Description: Public health actions should be informed by data, and opportunities to use data that can inform public health interventions are everywhere. In this course, you will build your data- and evidence-based public health skills and confidence. You will explore trusted and reliable sources of public health data and look for themes and disparities that can answer your questions about a community's health and wellbeing.

This helps healthcare organisations reduce costs and retain revenue, and also improving patient health outcomes and experience. “Caring to Go Beyond” is a service excellence training for healthcare service professional.

Organizations are also re-examining business strategies, workforce models, values, and culture. With this, there is an urgent challenge to future-proof the workplace with employees who have relevant and up-to-date competencies. Amid this era of massive transformation, it is imperative that employees take on self-directed learning and facilitate learning at the workplace. By capitalizing collective learning, it is the organisations that can learn the fastest that will lead the world.

By building resilience, individuals are better able to cope with the stress and uncertainty that often accompanies transition and are more likely to successfully navigate the changes.

Course Title: Introduction to Oxygen Concentrator Use at Home
Course Description:
At the end of the course, participants should be able to: 
1.	Understand Oxygen Therapy: basics of oxygen therapy, including its purpose, benefits, and the role it plays in patient care.
2.	List the Types of Oxygen Concentrators: Offering an overview of different types of oxygen concentrators available, their features, and how to select the appropriate device for individual needs.
3.	Possess Operational Knowledge: Providing detailed instructions on how to operate and adjust oxygen concentrators, including understanding flow rates, reading gauges, and using controls.
4.	Condcuct Maintenance and Troubleshooting: Teaching participants routine maintenance practices for keeping the device in optimal condition, and basic troubleshooting for common issues.
Training Company: Hmi Institute Of Health Sciences Pte. Ltd.

Course Title: PATIENT CARE DELIVERY IN NURSING (COMMUNITY CARE)(AOP)
Course Description:
It is essential to sequence the first topic on anatomy and physiology to understand how the structures and functions of a body that relates to the physical being of a person.
Next is the psychological aspect which involves the mental awareness and cultural factors which influence patient care. Cultural and mental awareness provides understanding of the patient as a person in order to provide patient centred care in the clinical procedures performed on them.
The following learning units are clinical procedures which includes the main core skill of performing vital signs monitoring which reflects a person’s physical wellbeing. The other added skills are reporting and recording the normal and abnormal findings
Vital sign taking and recording is a pre-requisite to the procedures for admission and discharge of patients in learning unit 4.
Other learning topics for patients are admission and discharge procedures, follow with their continued care, which is supported with elimination activities, a basic need in providing patient centred care in the clinical setting.
The last learning unit is an integration of knowledge and skills from previous learning unit to manage the patient hygiene activities, a complex procedure which involves more competencies in providing holistic care.
Training Company: Hmi Institute Of Health Sciences Pte. Ltd.

Course Title: CPR and AED training in Home Environment
Course Description:
At the end of the course, participants should be able to: 
1.	Recognize the signs and symptoms of a Cardiac Arrest
2.	Gain knowledge on prevention and mitigation of risk factors contributing to heart disease.
3.	Skilled in safely moving elderly individuals from a bed to a hard surface to ensure effective CPR administration
4.	Gain knowledge on where and how to retrieve the nearest AED
5.	Application of CPR+AED
6.	Application of CPR+AED on chair and wheelchair
Training Company: Hmi Institute Of Health Sciences Pte. Ltd.

2.	Understand the selection of appropriate diapering products based on individual needs.
3.	Provide Effective Perineal Care
4.	Recognize signs of potential issues like irritation or infection and know the appropriate steps to take.
5.	Implement strategies for skin care, particularly for individuals prone to bedsores or skin breakdown.
6.	Use suitable skin care products and techniques to maintain skin health and prevent complications.
Training Company: Hmi Institute Of Health Sciences Pte. Ltd.

Course Title: VTCT (ITEC) Level 1 Award in Mental Health Awareness
Course Description: This purpose of the qualification is to provide learners with an understanding of mental health and wellbeing. Learners will develop an awareness of a range of mental health conditions, the factors affecting mental health and the impact of mental health from a global, individual and workplace perspective. Learners will develop an understanding of what constitutes good mental health and mental ill-health and how to recognise the signs and symptoms of a number of mental health conditions. Learners will also develop an awareness of the types of support available for mental health conditions and how to access them.
Training Company: International College Of Holistic Health Pte. Ltd.

Course Title: VTCT (ITEC) Level 2 Certificate in Mental Health Awareness
Course Description: Learners will develop an understanding of what is meant by mental health, mental ill-health and mental well-being. Learners will explore the historical context of mental health care within the community and the impact of those changes. Furthermore, learners will develop an understanding of the social and cultural aspects of mental health as well as looking at the legal frameworks that relate to the provision of mental health care.
Training Company: International College Of Holistic Health Pte. Ltd.

Course Title: VTCT (ITEC) LEVEL 3 CERTIFICATE IN DIET AND NUTRITION FOR COMPLEMENTARY THERAPISTS (BLENDED LEARNING)
Course Description:
This course enable learners to provide healthy eating advice for their clients who they already treats for another therapy for the purpose of improving health and lifestyle. This qualification is for learners who want to work within the complementary therapy industry. The qualification consists of 1 mandatory unit with a total of 80 hours of Guided Learning (GL) to achieve the full qualification. The main skills the learner will perform are:

1. Diet and nutrition – to include dietary requirements such as; roles of carbohydrates, protein, fibre, vitamins, nutritional value of foods, effects of antioxidant and common aliments.

The learner will also learn about related health and safety and client care and communication which is essential in order to work and be successful in the industry.
Training Company: International College Of Holistic Health Pte. Ltd.

Course Title: ISA Obesity and Weight Management Course
Course Description: Obesity and weight management are significant health concerns worldwide. It is a major risk factor for various chronic conditions such as diabetes, cardiovascular disease and certain cancers. This course aims to provide a comprehensive understanding of the causes, consequences, and management strategies for obesity and weight-related issues. Students will develop knowledge and skills in assessing, preventing, and managing obesity, including understanding the impact of diet, physical activity, behaviour, and other lifestyle factors on body weight. This course will also discuss various societal, cultural, and environmental factors that contribute to obesity and the role of public health policies in addressing this complex issue.

Course Title: ISA Wellness and Behaviour Modification Course
Course Description: The fundamental wellness and behaviour change course is designed to provide a comprehensive understanding of the principles and practices of promoting wellness and behavior change. This course covers the basic concepts of health and wellness, as well as the practical application of behavior change strategies to improve personal and public health. The course explores the principles of motivational interviewing and the Transtheoretical Model. In addition, the course covers the importance of nutrition, physical activity, stress management, and sleep in promoting wellness and preventing chronic diseases.

Course Title: Home-based Eldercare – Basic 6 ADLs with Fall-Prevention
Course Description:
This program aims to provide learners with an awareness of how aging impacts the elderly’s 6 Activities of Daily Livings, and aims to equip learners with skills to assist the elderly to carry out their basic 6 ADLs effectively in the comfort of the elderly’s home environment.

 Learners will also understand the possible factors contributing to a fall, be equipped with the knowledge to anticipate and prevent falls at home, to support elderly who are fall-risk, and manage any emergency situations that might arise from a fall.

Course Title: Managing Elderly with Dementia - Effective Communication Strategies
Course Description: This program aims to provide learners with an awareness of dementia and an understanding of its impact on elderly and communication. This program aims to equip learners with effective communication strategies to interact and communicate effectively with elderly living with dementia, while managing challenging behaviour.

Course Title: Managing Eldercare (6 ADLs) and Caregiver Self Care Effectively
Course Description:
This program provides learners with an understanding of the importance and expectation of the caregiving (delivery of 6 ADLs) role, and educates learners to appreciate other roles that might be involved in the caregiving of the elderly. Amidst the ongoing expectations of being a caregiver, Learners are also taught how to take care of oneself by engaging in positive practices of  caregiver selfcare. 

The main aim of this foundational program is to equip learners with skills to assist the elderly to carry out their basic 6 ADLs effectively. 

As movement and mobility is an important ADL, Learners are also equipped with the knowledge of how to anticipate and prevent falls at home, to support elderly who are fall-risk and manage any emergency situations that might arise from a fall and affect the elderly’s mobility.

Course Title: Home-based Nasogastric (NG)/Percutaneous Endoscopic Gastrostomy (PEG) Tube Feeding Training for Caregivers

Course Title: WSQ Higher Certificate in Healthcare (Community Care) - (Classroom & Asynchronous)
Course Description:
The proposed qualification equips learners with practical skills and underpinning knowledge of the required task to function as a new entrant community care associate in supporting the healthcare workforce in the community care sector. 

The qualification entails modules which are essential to safely care for clients daily in the community care setting. Infection Prevention and Control ensures that CCA know the importance of hand hygiene and wearing of personal protective equipment (PPE) and can do it correctly. 

Mobility and Ambulation Assistance create a secure environment for both the CCA and the patients and ensuring that CCA can safely lift, transfer and position clients.

Feeding Management ensure that CCA can manage clients on dysphagia and therapeutic diet and safely perform oral and tube feeding. 

Medication Management enables CCA to learn the administration techniques, dosage schedules, enhancing medication adherence for oral and topical medications. 

Patient Care Delivery enables CCA to assist clients with Activities of Daily Living (ADLs) and support clinical procedures. 

Patient Empowerment on Self-care ensure the CCA can build trust and rapport with patients and caregivers and acquire skills in empowering patients. 

Intervention Implementation enables CCAs to provide comprehensive, personalized and effective care, ensuring better health outcomes and overall satisfaction.

Course Title: Building Mental & Emotional Resilience for Professional Excellence
Course Description:
Building Mental and Emotional Resilience for Professional Excellence addresses the challenge of how individuals’ well-being impacts their performance at work. While most people today recognize the importance of well-being, many still struggle to practice self-care in an organizational setting.

This course offers two key developments:
1.	The ability to recognize one’s work styles, mindset, and physiological changes that impact mental and emotional well-being.

2.	Techniques to consistently manage mental and emotional states and effectively dissociate from negative experiences.

This is a hands-on, experiential learning program that provides application-based practice, NLP tools, and brain-based learning.

The knowledge and skills you gain from this course will empower you to maintain composure, optimize performance, and develop self-management plans that are sustainable.

Course Title: Phlebotomy Course

Objective

Facilitating individuals who seek upskilling or re-skilling to become competent Phlebotomist with sound knowledge and consolidated skills & support the enhancement of healthcare Standards of Care & Practice. Thus, reducing complications and failure rate as Phlebotomist will be readily trained and can value-add to the professional healthcare team. 

To assist in the uniform stratification of the required competency for essential skill in Phlebotomy with formalized training and certification 

It will also assist to complement the healthcare fraternity in Training & Development effort and render the remaining healthcare team focused resources on productive healthcare delivery and outcome.

Content 
The training has 3 components - Diactics , Practicum & On-the-Job Training

Caregivers will understand diabetes mellitus disease, able to handle diabetic emergencies (severe hyperglycemia and hypoglycemia), advice on well-balanced diet, sick day management and travelling tips, knowing the importance of foot care and keeping active, as well as performing home blood glucose monitoring and insulin injection for person with diabetes.

Handle and manage day-to-day care, techniques, and strategies to prevent falls, apply proper and safe transferring techniques, basic communication skills and safe practice of medication administration to elderly.

Course Title: The Art of Self-Compassion for Self-Care
Course Description: Feeling like you are not good enough? You are not alone. We often judge ourselves harshly when we slip up or doubt our abilities. This course is designed to help learners develop self-compassion, a powerful skill that equips you to be your own best friend during challenging times. Through this resource-building program, you will learn to treat yourself with the same kindness and understanding you offer others. This will not only enhance your well-being and resilience but also reduce the risk of burnout. Remember, self-compassion is the foundation for extending compassion to others daily. Enroll today and learn to be kinder to yourself!

Course Title: The Art of Breathing with Yoga Principles
Course Description:
At the end of this workshop, learners will be able to:
Demonstrate an understanding of the basic principles of yoga and their relationship to breathing.
Acquire proficiency in various Pranayama (breath regulation) techniques and their distinctive benefits.
Utilise breathing as a tool for mitigating stress, inducing relaxation, and regulating emotions.
Develop a personal breathing practice that aligns with their objectives.

Course Title: Yogic Arts for Self-Care
Course Description: Discover the transformative power of yogic arts in this course designed to boost self-compassion and promote physical, mental, and emotional well-being. Explore the harmonious practice of gentle breathwork, sun salutations and a soothing flow to ease tension and improve mobility. Learners will be guided with meditations for self-inquiry, body awareness, and stress management. Perfect for both beginners and seasoned practitioners.

Total curriculum is 790 hours comprising 420 hours of academic studies and 440 of clinical practice. The following PDCs are covered under this programme:
1. Post-Diploma Certificate in Principles and Science of Palliative Care Nursing-ADCC1 (225 hours)
2. Post-Diploma Certificate in Advanced Concepts in Palliative Care Nursing-ADCC1 (165 hours)
3. Post-Diploma Certificate in Practice of Palliative Care Nursing-ADCC1 (400 hours)

Course Title: Advanced Diploma in Nursing (Perianaesthesia)-ADPA1
Course Description:
The Advanced Diploma in Nursing (Perianaesthesia) course will be offered full-time in 3 Post-diploma Certificates (PDCs) over 7 months (30 weeks). Total curriculum is 790 hours comprising 390 hours of academic studies and 400 of clinical practice. The following PDCs are covered under this programme:
1. Post-Diploma Certificate in Principles of Perianaesthesia Nursing-ADPA1 (225 hours)
2. Post-Diploma Certificate in Advanced Concepts in Perianaesthesia Nursing-ADPA1 (165 hours)
3. Post-Diploma Certificate in Practice of Perianaesthesia Nursing-ADPA1 (400 hours)

Course Title: Advanced Diploma in Nursing (Perioperative)-ADPO1
Course Description:
The Advanced Diploma in Nursing (Perioperative) course will be offered full-time in 3 Post-diploma Certificates (PDCs) over 7 months (30 weeks). Total curriculum is 790 hours comprising 390 hours of academic studies and 400 of clinical practice. The following PDCs are covered under this programme:
1. Post-Diploma Certificate in Principles and Science of Perioperative Nursing-ADPO1 (225 hours)
2. Post-Diploma Certificate in Advanced Concepts in Perioperative Nursing-ADPO1 (165 hours)
3. Post-Diploma Certificate in Practice of Perioperative Nursing-ADPO1 (400 hours)

Course Title: Mental Health and Ageing (Synchronous e-learning)
Course Description: This course provides students with an overview of the principles, current knowledge and practice of mental health care for older adults. The course will cover the mental health spectrum from normal to pathological ageing, selected mental disorders of late life and the challenges across the disease trajectory.

Course Title: Ageing and the End of Life
Course Description: This course provides an interdisciplinary overview of the major psycho-socio-cultural perspectives, theories and clinical practices on ageing and end-of-life care from a global perspective with a critical focus on the Asian experience. Through interactive lecture and experiential learning, students will examine the ethical, political, financial, environmental, and public health issues of death and loss. Such exploration will facilitate insights, reflections and personal growth for enhancing students’ capacity in dealing with the inevitability of mortality, while equipping them with the core intellectual knowledge and practical skills for supporting older dying patients, bereaved families, grieving communities, as well as their own selves who will eventually be faced with the challenges of death, dying, and bereavement.

Course Title: Traditional Chinese Medicine for Older Adults
Course Description: Chinese medicine has long been used as a complementary treatment for long term diseases and general health maintenance. This course explores the fundamentals of Chinese Medicine (TCM) and its application to health and wellness of older people. Emphasis will be on age-related issues and illnesses such as hypertension, diabetes, cerebrovascular diseases and Alzheimer's disease.

Course Title: Perspectives on Ageing
Course Description: This course provides an overview of ageing from multidisciplinary perspectives covering major theoretical and substantive areas in the fields of sociology, psychology, economics, communication, and health. Through the course, students will gain comprehensive understanding and knowledge of ageing issues across Asia and the world.

Course Title: Health Coaching Techniques
Course Description: Building up from the Health Coaching for Older Adults course, this course covers topics of advocacy as part of patient-centred practice, communication skills, and techniques on the use of medical devices The curriculum considers the emotional progression of learning, beginning with ethics and professional boundaries before addressing mental health and self-care. Moving onto crisis intervention and equipping learners for challenging situations, it also focuses on providing positive reinforcement for self-care management, emphasizing the importance of motivating both patients and caregivers with coaching techniques for optimal health outcomes. This overall flow aims to create a comprehensive and impactful learning experience for future health coaches.

Course Title: Research Methods for Ageing
Course Description: This course will provide an overview of the different qualitative and quantitative methods and tools available for ageing research. Particularly, learners will be trained to understand and apply evidence-based research and practice in aged care service provision, development, and evaluation, all of which are essential skills in the twenty-first century. Moreover, learners will gain a broad conceptual understanding of various research methods and obtain practical experiences on using such methods for assessing and appraising the quality and merit of empirical studies on a broad range of topics related to older persons including but not limited to examining lived experiences of unique population cohorts, public policy formulation, intervention development, and programme evaluation.

Course Title: Physical Activity, Nutrition & Health (Classroom & Asynchronous e-learning)
Course Description:
Non-communicable lifestyle diseases are a major cause of death worldwide. Regular physical activity and good nutrition are crucial factors that can significantly lower the risk of many of these diseases.
This course is designed for physical educators, sports coaches, exercise specialists, and healthcare professionals working in healthcare, sports, and fitness.
These professionals are key in fighting these diseases. Therefore, they need a solid understanding of the background of each disease and how physical activity and nutrition can prevent and treat them. This course introduces to learners how physical activity can be measured as well as the link between energy intake and expenditure. This knowledge will enable learners to recommend the right amount of physical activity and the appropriate diet to reduce the occurrence of these diseases in society.

Course Title: Master of Science in Holistic Palliative Care (HoPE)
Course Description: The HoPE programme is targetted primarily at healthcare professionals and is aimed at equipping them in the primary care environment with the knowledge and skillsets relevant to the local culture and context to meet the growing needs for palliative care professionals.

Course Title: Communicating Complexity Part 2: Data Visualization
Course Description:
Communicating Complexity is a series of three linked courses in which 
students will have the opportunity to practice a number of skills and techniques aimed at helping them to communicate complex ideas in ways that are easier to understand, and can be tailored to different audiences.

Course Title: Communicating Complexity Part 1: Tackling communication barriers
Course Description: Communicating Complexity is a series of three linked courses in which students will have the opportunity to practice a number of skills and techniques aimed at helping them to communicate complex ideas in ways that are easier to understand, and can be tailored to different audiences.

Course Title: Communicating Complexity Part 3: Communicating for different audiences
Course Description:
Communicating Complexity is a series of three linked courses in which students will have the opportunity to practice a number of skills and techniques aimed at helping them to communicate complex ideas in ways that are easier to understand, and can be tailored to different audiences. 

Students may benefit from completing Communicating Complexity Part 1: Tackling communication barriers and Communicating Complexity Part 2: Data visualization before starting Part 3.

Course Title: Counselling Older Persons (Synchronous e-learning)
Course Description: Counselling older people require specific knowledge and skills. This course will introduce students to the basic counselling skills, approaches and interventions pertinent to working with older people. The course provides opportunities for students to develop key competencies and be equipped with tools to effectively assess, intervene and employ basic counselling techniques when they work with older people.

Course Title: A Scientific Look at Thoughts and Behaviors in the Social World
Course Description:
This course is a broad overview of social psychology, the scientific study of thought and behavior in response to the social world. Using the Outcome-Based Teaching and Learning (OBTL) approach as the backbone, this course will benefit you by highlighting the basic psychological principles that shape the ways you make sense of yourselves, other people, and the world. You will learn the major theories and empirical findings on topics that are central to everyday life, such as obedience, prosocial behaviors, and romantic attraction. Through the scope of social psychology, you will learn that numerous social phenomena can be traced back to a subset of socialcognitive principles that guide the human mind. You will learn how human thinking and behaviors are influenced by various social contexts, from group membership and interpersonal relationships to society and culture. Finally, you will receive progressive training on theoretical thinking, critical reasoning, and methodological designs. 

More broadly, this course is in deep connections with other key courses of the program such as Introduction to Psychology, Cognitive Psychology, Social Cognition, Developmental Psychology, Human Motivation, Laboratory in Social Psychology, Fundamentals of Social Science Research, and Research Design and Data Analysis in Psychology. The course forms part of the foundational training on psychological knowledge, research capability, theoretical reasoning, and critical thinking.

Course Title: BACHELOR OF CHINESE MEDICINE
Course Description: Please refer to NTU website.

Course Title: Theories in Medical Sociology
Course Description: The course will cover some of the most dominant theories in the field of medical sociology. Theories covered in the course include, but not limited to fundamental cause theory, gender theory, cumulative disadvantages theory, stress process theory, labeling theory, social capital theory. Course materials will deal with theoretical views about social patterns, processes, and outcomes, as well as the relevance of social contexts for contributing to health outcomes. The course will also examine how these theories are used and applied in research on medical sociology.  Thus, this course may equip students with theoretical tools to understand the dynamics of health and society, and at the same time, enhance the ability of students to conduct their own research on medical sociology.

Course Title: Musculoskeletal (MSK) Ultrasound Course for Nurses and Allied Health Professionals (AHPs)
Course Description:
This program is designed as an introduction for healthcare providers who are exploring point-of-care ultrasound to enhance the evaluation and management of the patients with upper and lower limb pathologies.

The format will be live scanning of a subject to familiarize participants with a standardized regional scanning protocol, followed by practical hands-on scanning sessions (with a ratio of 1 trainer to 5 trainees). Additional didactic teaching is also done to highlight specific key pathologies and their sonographic appearance.

A formative assessment on the theoretical and practical aspects of
musculoskeletal ultrasound will also be completed at the end of the course.

Course Title: Graduate Diploma in Cardiovascular Medicine (GDCVM)
Course Description:
The GDCVM programme is targeted primarily at MOH-registered GP/FMP and is aimed at equipping them in the primary care environment with the knowledge and skillsets to upskill family doctors in cardiovascular medicine is also aligned with MOH policy to transform outpatient care for chronic diseases. The core paradigm of this
transformation is to shift from hospital-based to community care, and to promote prevention over treatment of chronic diseases.

Course Title: BACHELOR OF SCIENCE IN BIOMEDICAL SCIENCES AND BACHELOR OF MEDICINE (CHINESE MEDICINE)
Course Description: Please refer to NTU website.

Course Title: Empowering Caregivers (Domestic Helpers & Families) in the Nutritional Management for people requiring Therapeutic Diet and Modified Textured Diet
Course Description: This course aims to provide caregivers with the knowledge on how to manage expectations and skills to manage the day-to day care of the elderly. Caregivers will also learn ways to take care of themselves as well as get an overview of the various resources available to support them in their journey as a caregiver.

Course Title: Empowering Caregivers (Domestic Helpers & Families) in the Nutritional Management for people requiring Therapeutic Diet and Modified Textured Diet (Online)
Course Description: This course aims to provide caregivers with the knowledge on how to manage expectations and skills to manage the day-to day care of the elderly. Caregivers will also learn ways to take care of themselves as well as get an overview of the various resources available to support them in their journey as a caregiver.

Course Title: Foundations of Critical Care Nursing
Course Description:
Learners will be able to:  
Knowledge
•	Apply the pathophysiology knowledge to the management of common critical care conditions.
•	Apply critical care pharmacology to manage critical care conditions.
Skills
•	Perform comprehensive assessments and hemodynamic monitoring for critically ill patients.
•	Demonstrate effective communication and collaboration within the critical care team.
•	Develop hands-on clinical skills required for providing care in the ICU, including airway management, mechanical ventilation, and medication administration.
•	Foster interdisciplinary care and recognize the contributions of various healthcare professionals in the ICU.
•	Apply critical thinking skills to analyze complex clinical situations and make evidence-based decisions.
Attitude
•	Advocate for the rights and preferences of critically ill patients and their families.

Course Title: Multisystem Nursing Practice I
Course Description:
Learners will be able to:  
Knowledge
1. Apply knowledge of the pathophysiology, pharmacology, and principles of management of common multisystem (cardio, respiratory, metabolic and multiorgan) disorders to the care of patients.
2. Apply knowledge of the various treatment modalities related to multisystem (cardio, respiratory, metabolic and multiorgan) disorders.
3.  Apply knowledge of the complications and management related to multisystem (cardio, respiratory, metabolic and multiorgan) disorders.

Skill
4.Perform the health assessment and apply clinical reasoning skills in the conduct of focused health assessment on patients with multisystem (cardio, respiratory, metabolic and multiorgan) disorders.
5.Detect early signs of clinical deterioration related to multisystem (cardio, respiratory, metabolic and multiorgan) disorders. 
6.Perform the essential skills and management related to multisystem (respiratory, endocrine, oncologic and  multiorgans) nursing.

Attitude
7. Adopt patient safety principles to the care of patients with multisystem (respiratory, endocrine, oncologic and multiorgans) disorders.

Course Title: Regulation and Clinical Evaluation of Medical Devices (Classroom and Asynchronous e-learning)
Course Description: On successful completion of this course, you will be able to describe the regulatory concepts and major frameworks governing the development and regulatory management of medical devices and technology, list the key regulatory guidance and requirements, and articulate the important considerations for assessing the clinical performance of medical devices and technology.

Course Title: Foundations of Integrated Care
Course Description:
Learners will be able to:  
1.	Apply the knowledge and core concepts of integrated care to the role of case management in the clinical practice.
2.	Demonstrate the knowledge and principles of acute disease management in clinical patient care.
3.	Demonstrate the knowledge and principles of chronic disease management in clinical patient care.
4.	Apply the knowledge and principles of symptom management in clinical patient care within an integrated care framework.

Course Title: Continuing Education & Training in Regulatory Toxicology (Synchronous e-learning)
Course Description: Gain an in-depth understanding of the role of regulatory toxicology in safeguarding public health. Comprehend the principles of risk assessment, including hazard identification, dose-response assessment, exposure assessment, and risk characterization. Explore toxicological mechanisms leading to local (e.g., skin, eye, respiratory irritation) and systemic toxicities (e.g., genotoxicity, organ toxicity, reproductive and developmental toxicity, carcinogenicity). Gain insights into safety testing methodologies for local and systemic toxicities and their relevant regulations for chemical and product registrations. Investigate emerging trends in regulatory toxicology, including the regulatory adoption of New Approach Methodologies (NAMs) for chemical and product registrations. Gain practical knowledge and skills to make an informed safety decision in the field of regulatory toxicology. Explore the principles and practices of risk communication, including how to effectively convey toxicological information to stakeholders such as regulatory authorities, the public, and industry professionals.

Course Title: Child Health (0-6 years old) (Synchronous & Asynchronous)
Course Description:
Learners will be able to: 
1. analyse and apply the principles of primary health care to promote and maintain overall wellbeing of children in the community settings;
2. understand importance of engaging and enabling parents to promote physical and mental health as well as safety of the children; and
3. recognize common health and safety issues experienced by children aged 0-6-year-old. 
4. demonstrate evidence-based and policy informed care 
for children aged 0-6 years’ old;
5. perform growth and developmental screening and initiate appropriate referrals for children with developmental delays. 
6. advocate parental involvement to promote well-being and ensure safety of children aged 0-6 years old and
7.adopt culturally appropriate care for child health.

Course Title: The role and application of Genetic Counselling (Classroom & Asynchronous e-learning)
Course Description: This  course enables participants to apply concepts of basic genetics to assess risk and to identify patients with/or at risk of a genetic condition. They should be able to identify genetic testing appropriate to patient needs as well as to discuss the ethical issues in genetics and genomics. They can also recognize basic counselling skills including active listening, attending, reflecting, clarifying and summarising.

Course Title: Fundamentals of Cognitive Behavioural Therapy (Synchronous e-learning)
Course Description: This course introduces the fundamentals of Cognitive Behavioural Therapy, which can be useful for both healthcare professionals and the general public. It aims to equip learners with the understanding of maladaptive thinking patterns and basic techniques on therapeutic approaches. At the same time, learners will become more aware of the thinking process in their patients or people around them, so that they can respond or provide support in a more effective and efficient manner.

Course Title: Basics in Genetics and Genetic Testing (Classroom and Asynchronous e-learning)
Course Description: This course offers significant advantages for career prospects and skills development. It boosts employability by imparting comprehensive knowledge in genetics, bioinformatics and clinical genomics making participants appealing to the industry. The course equips individuals with expertise in genetic testing and interdisciplinary competencies, enabling them to excel in diverse roles, driving improvement of clinical services development. In a time when genetics plays a critical role in healthcare, this course is highly pertinent and positions participants to make meaningful contributions to these fields while staying adaptable and well informed in a dynamic industry by recognizing the role of genomics in the practice of precision medicine and complex genetic conditions.

Course Title: Learning Needs Analysis
Course Description:
This module aims to identify the learning needs of healthcare professionals using the learning needs analysis framework



Students are expected to display competency in the following after course completion:

1 Discuss the process of learning needs analysis 

2 Conduct learning needs analysis using the learning needs analysis framework

3 Evaluate the results of the learning needs   analysis 

4 Develop recommendations for short-and long-term training plans and budget and

5 Discuss barriers to learning of learners

Course Title: Information Technology in Healthcare
Course Description:
Upon completion of the course, participants will:
1. Know how Information Technology is used in healthcare
2. Understand workflow and standards in healthcare Information Technology
3. Propose an Information Technology solution for a business need
4. Design and evaluate healthcare Information Technology products according to usability standards
5. Conduct requirements gathering
6. Rapid prototype solutions for acceptance
7. Foresee and overcome challenges in deploying Information Technology in healthcare

Course Title: Integrated Care for Special Population
Course Description:
Learners will be able to:  
1.	Apply core concepts of healthy ageing and geriatric care using a person-centred, multidisciplinary in the treatment of elderly patients.
2.	Apply knowledge of palliative care in an integrated setting to support both patient and their families.
3.	Demonstrate knowledge in applying integrated, interdisciplinary approaches to deliver holistic mental health care.
4.	Apply the principles and process of transitional care to ensure continuous patient care across different settings.

Course Title: Advanced Clinical Techniques For Retinal Diseases (Classroom & Asynchronous e-learning)
Course Description:
The module will focus on enhancing optometrists’ knowledge and skills in performing their duty of care as a clinical optometrist practicing in the community. The specialized skills have been stipulated in the Competency Framework of Clinical Optometry and Community Services (COCS), approved by MOH.
•	Relative Afferent Pupillary Defect (RAPD)
•	78 Dioptre Lens (D)
•	Fundus photography

Course Title: Curriculum Design
My Microbes and me, in Sickness and in Health
Course Description:
The workshop aims to educate/equip participants with knowledge and basic skills in the following healthcare topics:
1. Introduction to what microbiomes are
2. Bacterial DNA (metagenomic) sequencing
3. Microbiomes effect on health (development) and disease
4. Probiotics and prebiotics in food
5. Technics to prepare home-made probiotic drinks
6. Influence of skin microbiomes on beauty and home hygiene
7. Appreciation of microbiome research in science/medicine

Training will be conducted in the form of seminars (small group lectures with Question and Answer), laboratory demonstrations and hands-on preparation of probiotic drinks.

At the end of the course, the participants should be able to effectively communicate the following:
1. What comprises a microbiome and where these microbial communities can be found
2. The principles of microbiome sequencing
3. The importance of microbiomes in human development and disease
4. The difference between prebiotics and probiotics and their benefits in diet
5. Recipes for probiotics cultures/drinks and main principles
6. Recognize the role of microbes in beauty and in hygiene
7. Application of microbiome research in the local science and healthcare scene

Course Title: Navigating Medical Law and Ethics in the Current and Future Landscape
Course Description: This course seeks to refresh and update clinicians, patient safety officers, nursing staff, and clinical pharmacists with a current understanding of medical law and statutes involving ethical and legal considerations affecting clinical care practices in an evolving litigious landscape. Primary outcome of this course is to achieve higher-order critical thinking skills (thought leadership) towards enhanced risk management capabilities of healthcare professionals

Course Title: Effective Clinical Supervision Skills - Basic Course
Course Description:
Equip healthcare professionals with:
1 Basic theoretical knowledge underlying clinical education
2 Knowledge of supervision/assessment frameworks
3 Clinical educator skills: building supervisory relationships, understanding supervisory styles
4 Strategies to enhance supervisee's clinical reasoning skills
5 Feedback skills and strategies for improving supervisee's performance
6 Strategies for specific supervisory contexts

Learners will apply these skills in the following areas:
1 Supervision of students
2 Supervision of newly qualified Speech Therapists (STs)
3 Supervision of junior staff

Course Title: Ocular Pharmacology for Clinical Optometrists (Classroom & Asynchronous e-learning)
Course Description:
This module is designed to provide students with a refresher on the pharmacological principles and therapeutic interventions related to the treatment of ocular diseases. The course will cover the basic pharmacology of drugs used in ophthalmology, including mechanisms of action, pharmacokinetics, adverse effects and therapeutic application. Students will gain insight into the different classes of ocular medications and their appropriate use in the management of various ocular conditions. 

Module Outline:
1.	Brief overview of basic pharmacokinetics and pharmacodynamics of ocular drugs and route of administration and drug deliver to the eye
2.	Describe the mechasnism of action, clinical application, side-effects, indication and contraindication of the following classes of ocular drugs:

•	Topical anaesthetics
•	Mydriacyl
•	Cyclogyl
•	Atropine for myopia control
•	Anti-glaucoma
•	Anti-infective agents
•	Anti-inflammatory and Anti-allergy agents
•	Ocular medication for dry eye

Course Title: Skills Training in Health Coaching
Course Description:
At the end of the course, participants should be able to:
1)Explain the benefits of health coaching to patients 
2)Describe the role of health coaches in a hospital 
3)Demonstrate the use of opened ended questions and reflections to build rapport 
4) Use core coaching competencies to manage conversations with patients and enable them to develop their own health goals 

This course enables participants to gain valuable insight into how health coaching is conducted in a hospital setting. It is the first course to allow participants to observe trained health coaches carry out sessions with patients. In addition, participants are given the opportunity to directly apply newly learned skills with professional guidance and feedback, giving them the confidence to put their new skills to use.

Course Title: Regulation of Pharmaceutical Manufacturing (Classroom & Asynchronous e-learning)
Course Description: This module will explain the importance of manufacturing and quality control for pharmaceuticals. Participants will learn about the strict controls and regulatory requirements, particularly on product stability and specifications, in addition to the practical skills obtained through mock reviews of dossier materials. The module closes with an emphasis on the utility of a system approach to quality control, and the new regulatory trends upcoming in this domain.

Course Title: Principles and Practices of Laboratory Biosafety and Biosecurity
Course Description:
This course aims to equip learners with the following knowledge:
• Definition and relationship between Biosafety & Biosecurity
• National & International Biosafety, Guidelines & Legislations to comply with Biosafety & Biosecurity requirements in workplaces
• Laboratory Hazards and Risks
• Biorisk identification and management
• Biosafety & Biosecurity risk mitigation principles and practices
• Emergency Response Management
• Laboratory Biorisk Management System

Course Title: Fundamentals of Health Products Regulation (Classroom & Asynchronous e-learning)
Course Description: This module lays the foundational basis of regulatory management and decision-making for health products, and covers the essential principles  in managing pharmaceuticals and medical devices across their life cycles. Regulatory requirements for the different product development phases will be showcased, and hands-on practical sessions will help participants learn about the real-life settings in regulatory decision-making. In this course, participants will also learn about the major regulatory organisations steering the innovation of regulatory processes and focus.

Course Title: Transforming Adolescent Mental Health (Synchronous e-learning)
Course Description: 1. Understanding Adolescent Mental Health: Gain an in-depth understanding of the mental health challenges and developmental changes in adolescence.                          2. Communication Skills: Develop skills for effective communication and engagement with adolescents. 3. Counseling and Support Strategies: Learn counseling techniques and strategies to support adolescents in distress.        4. Managing Anxiety and Depression: Focus on identifying and managing common mental health issues such as anxiety and depression in adolescents. 5. Promoting Resilience and Coping Skills: Teach adolescents coping mechanisms and resilience-building strategies to navigate life challenges.      6. Social Media and Mental Health: Address the impact of social media and digital technology on adolescent mental health. 7. Cultural and Ethical Considerations: Understand diverse cultural backgrounds and ethical considerations in adolescent mental health care.

Course Title: Debriefing in Simulation-Based Healthcare Education
Course Description: This one-day workshop is meant to provide both Undergraduate and Postgraduate faculty with additional skills to facilitate an effective debriefing session Participants will be exposed to different debriefing techniques and strategies and work in groups to discuss and role-play various debriefing situations using both standardized patients and high-fidelity simulators They will also evaluate debriefing sessions, receive feedback on their debriefing techniques and reflect on areas where their debriefing can be made more effective

Course Title: Postgraduate Course in Palliative Medicine for Doctors (Classroom & Asynchronous)
Course Description:
At the end of the course, all participants will 
- understand and appreciate the principles and approach of supportive and palliative care and its importance in Singapore
- understand the principles of pain and symptom management in end of life care
- understand the principles of effective communication in supportive and palliative care
- understand palliative care in cancer and non-cancer conditions 
- understand the practical aspects of palliative care nursing 
- understand emergency conditions in palliative care
- understand ethical issues surrounding palliative care
- understand the principles of terminal care
- understand the principles of palliative care in frailty

Course Title: Renal Supportive Care Course (Classroom & Asynchronous)
Course Description:
The course will cover the following, 
- symptom control in advanced chronic kidney disease (CKD) in patients
- care of the dying patient with advanced CKD 
- role of supportive care in advanced CKD 
- geriatric syndromes (e.g., frailty, Dementia) in CKD
- serious illness communication in advanced CKD
- shared decision making with advanced CKD patients and their families
- Anxiety and depression in advanced CKD patients
- Ethical considerations in treatment decisions

Course Title: Fundamentals of Simulation-Based Healthcare Education
Course Description: This two-day workshop is designed to be interactive - consisting of short didactic sessions and hands-on practice of the essential skills needed for simulation-based healthcare education Participants will work in groups to run their simulation scenarios, discuss the common pitfalls encountered, and receive feedback on these sessions They will also have an opportunity to share their experiences and exchange ideas on integrating simulation-based learning into their existing curricula This workshop is aimed at faculty who are interested in using simulation methodologies to enhance their curriculum

Course Title: Common Retinal & Symptomatic Posterior Eye Diseases (Classroom & Asynchronous e-learning)
Course Description:
This module provides clinical optometrists a refresher on the etiology, pathophysiology, clinical presentation, diagnostic methods and management strategies in particular DR and AMD. 

The module will allow clinical optometrists to make use of the clinical skills learnt in the previous module to fulfil the following Competency Framework of Clinical Optometry and Community Services (COCS), approved by the Ministry of Health (MOH).

•	mild to moderate to severe Non-Proliferative Diabetic Retinopathy/Proliferative Diabetic Retinopathy (NPDR/PDR)
•	treated Diabetic Retinopathy (DR)
•	Tractional Diabetic Retinopathy (TDR)
•	Diabetic Maculopathy (DM)
•	Clinical Significant Macular Edema (CSME)
•	Epiretinal Membrane (ERM)
•	Central Retinal Vein Occlusion /Branch Retinal Vein Occlusion (CRVO/BRVO)
•	Central Retinal Artery Occlusion /Branch Retinal Artery Occlusion (CRAO/BRAO)
•	Dry Age-related Macular Degeneration (AMD)
•	Exudative Age-related Macular Degeneration (AMD)

Course Title: Laboratory Biosafety and Biosecurity Risk Assessment and Risk Management
Course Description:
This course aims to equip learners with the following insights:
• Interpret and apply national and international biosafety and biosecurity standards, guidelines, and legislations
• Perform biorisk assessment by identifying hazards, determining risk levels and appropriate risk mitigation measures.
• Plan and develop appropriate programmes for addressing biorisk, decontamination and waste management as well as occupational health to minimise incidents and accidents at the workplace
• Manage risks and threats as well as implement appropriate internal controls to maintain a safe and secure workplace
• Develop and integrate appropriate training and communication programmes to enhance staff readiness

Course Title: Occupational Training Periodisation and Heat Stress Management
Course Description:
Upon completion of the course, participants will be able to:

1 Describe and identify risk factors for exertional heat stroke
2 Prescribe heat management and training programs for heat-exposed personnel/workers based on current scientific evidence
3 Design and customize heat mitigation strategies based on individual occupational/sporting needs

Course Title: Certificate in Chronic Disease Management for Healthcare Professionals
Application of Clinical Genetics (Classroom and Asynchronous e-learning)
Course Description: Identify common genetic disorders which present in clinical practice and become familiar with the underlying etiology, genetic mechanisms and phenotypic characteristics. The course prepares individuals to learn and familiarise with the various genetic conditions in patients of all ages. This knowledge is important in the healthcare industry.

Course Title: Advanced Clinical Techniques For Anterior Eye Disease, Cataracts and Glaucoma (Classroom and Asynchronous e-learning)
Course Description:
The module will focus on enhancing optometrists’ knowledge and skills in performing their duty of care as a clinical optometrist practicing in the community. The specialized skills have been stipulated in the Competency Framework of Clinical Optometry and Community Services (COCS), approved by Ministry of Health.
•	Van Herrick Angle Assessment
•	Goldmann Applanation Tonometry (GAT)
•	Red reflex
•	Anterior Chamber (AC) cell assessment

Course Title: The Application and Use of Genetic Counselling - A Practical Approach (Classroom & Asynchronous e-learning)
Course Description: Through this hands-on course, participants would be able to gain a more in-depth understanding of genetic counselling and variant curation. This is an important course to let the participants to incorporate what was learnt in the previous three courses to demonstrate specific expected competencies in genetic counselling as well as to apply the knowledge in variant curation.

Course Title: Learning Analytics and Student Performance (Synchronous & Asynchronous e-learning)
Course Description: In learning analytics, educators use technology to improve learning by capturing course delivery and student learning data to report on the student’s performance. Through learning analytics, we can obtain objective traces of data to understand student learning and do a faster iteration to close the loop of teaching, learning and feedback to improve student’s learning experience. During this module, you will learn to design and identify student learning data collection, handle, clean and curate data , visualise and analyse student performance data, formulate the possibilities of providing real-time feedback and be equipped with skills for decision making.

Course Title: Graduate Diploma in Dental Implantology
Course Description: Understand the science of implant dentistry and to apply the knowledge in clinical practice; Diagnosis and treatment planning in implant dentistry; Apply a multidisciplinary approach to the management of patients requiring implants; Perform the surgical stages of implant dentistry; Perform the restorative or rehabilitative phases in implant dentistry.

Course Title: Health Literacy and Coaching
Course Description:
Learners will be able to:  
 1) Define the concept of health literacy, distinguishing between different levels of health literacy.

 2) Understand what is health coaching, the theoretical framework driving behavioural change, and the role of health coaching in the community for better health outcomes and preventive health.

 3) Structure the health coaching process to increase client’s engagement. 

 4) Develop a patient-centred intervention plan with the client using Biopsychosocial-spiritual (BPSS) model to understand client’s goals, values, beliefs, culture and background. 

 5) Work with the client to evaluate progress, re-assess goals and make modifications to the coaching plan to ensure the sustainability of lifestyle changes made. 

 6) Apply Motivational Interviewing skills to build rapport and relationship in a health coaching conversation and to elicit and strengthen change talk. 

 7) Navigate and address ethical dilemmas that may arise in coaching relationships

 8) Apply self-care to prevent burnout

Course Title: Professional Certificate in Application of Immune Effector Cells in Cell Therapy (Synchronous & Asynchronous)
Course Description:
This course will equip learners with the following competencies:
- Understand the process of Chimeric antigen receptor (CAR) T-cell treatment and the key steps involved
- Gain a better understanding of the regulation of CAR T-cell treatment and implications on the practice of cell therapy 
- Understand the quality control and assurance aspects of manufacturing and application of cell-based therapeutics to patients 
- Gain insights and acquire knowledge in the manufacturing of CAR T-cell for cell therapy treatment
- Know the considerations involved in cell therapy clinical research

Course Title: Health Economics and Financing
Course Description:
Upon completion of the course, the participants will:

1. Be able to discuss the approaches and viewpoints that economists bring to the health field.

2. Recite evidence from seminal articles in health economics.

3. Discuss some of the most important economic issues and problems faced by the health sector, & of the extent to which economic analysis can assist in their solutions.

4. Examine critically how economics can be used to influence policy for critical health issues facing Singapore today.

Course Title: Ultrasound for Renal Tract - Level 1 (Acquisition) (Classroom and Asynchronous)
Course Description:
Objective:
To acquire a complete set of diagnostic quality views to answer the following protocol based clinical question, 'Is there hydronephrosis or urinary retention?'

Expected Learning Outcomes:
1) Demonstrate effective use of an ultrasound machine
2) Optimise an ultrasound image
3) Recognise normal ultrasound anatomy and describe using sonographic terminology
4) Acquire images of ta kidney in 2 planes and a post micturition bladder volume

Learning Strategies:
- Hands-on practical scanning on real volunteers and simulators using a maximum ratio of 1 tutor to 4 students
- Didactic presentations
- Online video, powerpoints and directed reading
- Case study submissions including images, with detailed constructive and honest feedback by a dedicated tutor
- Peer group support forum moderated and assisted regularly by skilled tutors

Benefits of Learning Outcomes:
- Expedited patient management pathways
- Improved patient outcomes
- Reduced healthcare costs through improved management, choice of further testing and base knowledge of ultrasound applications and reports

Faculty:
-Only experienced highly skilled ultrasound practitioners (Sonographers) and clinical specialists are employed to conduct the education program

Course Title: Ultrasound for Shoulder - Level 1 (Acquisition) (Classroom and Asynchronous)
Course Description:
Objective:
To acquire a complete set of diagnostic quality views to answer the following protocol based clinical question, 'Is there hydronephrosis or urinary retention?'

Expected Learning Outcomes:
1) Demonstrate effective use of an ultrasound machine
2) Optimise an ultrasound image
3) Recognise normal ultrasound anatomy and describe using sonographic terminology
4) Acquire images of ta kidney in 2 planes and a post micturition bladder volume

Learning Strategies:
- Hands-on practical scanning on real volunteers and simulators using a maximum ratio of 1 tutor to 4 students
- Didactic presentations
- Online video, powerpoints and directed reading
- Case study submissions including images, with detailed constructive and honest feedback by a dedicated tutor
- Peer group support forum moderated and assisted regularly by skilled tutors

Benefits of Learning Outcomes:
- Expedited patient management pathways
- Improved patient outcomes
- Reduced healthcare costs through improved management, choice of further testing and base knowledge of ultrasound applications and reports

Faculty:
-Only experienced highly skilled ultrasound practitioners (Sonographers) and clinical specialists are employed to conduct the education program

Course Title: Ultrasound for Renal Tract - Level 2 (Interpretation) (Classroom, Synchronous & Asynchronous)
Course Description:
Expected learning outcomes:
Course Goal
To utilise diagnostic ultrasound to answer the urgent clinical question: "Is there a hydronephrosis or urinary retention?" in the emergency environment

Course expected outcomes
1/ Consolidate the student's existing skills to accurately and safely operate diagnostic ultrasound equipment to acquire images of the kidney in two planes and post micturition bladder volume
2/ Understand how to interpret the degree of hydronephrosis and differentiate from parapelvic cysts Ae well as determine the residual bladder volume
3/ To interpret the complexity of anatomy and pathologies encountered when incorporating Point of Care UltraSound (PoCUS) of the renal tract into an urgent patient management pathway in suspicion of a hydronephrosis or urinary retention
-Become a safe, experienced Point of Care UltraSound (PoCUS) practitioner

-Detailed educational outcomes can be viewed on a pdf document at this link:
https://aiuappboxcom/s/65ow6eakiv7te2n4y0xk2bfifjawnxo0

Course Title: Ultrasound for Renal Tract - Level 3 (Integration) (Classroom, Synchronous & Asynchronous)
Course Description:
Expected learning outcomes:

- To obtain the skills to  integrate  renal tract Point of Care UltraSound (PoCUS) findings into the patient management pathway in the urgent assessment of suspected renal tract pain.
-Become a safe, experienced PoCUS practitioner.
-A practitioner who completes this program will be recognised and acknowledged as being competent in performing point of care ultrasound for their patient's welfare. This standard currently does not exist, making Singapore a leader in this field
-Please review the detailed educational outcomes which may be viewed on the pdf document at this link: https://aiu.app.box.com/s/1njp8gbm26265xz6c5jlpdee97b5aocx

Course Title: Ultrasound for Shoulder - Level 2 (Interpretation) (Classroom, Synchronous & Asynchronous)
Course Description:
Expected learning outcomes:
Course Goal
To utilise diagnostic ultrasound to answer the urgent clinical question: "Is there a joint/bursal effusion or GHJ dislocation ?" in the emergency environment

Course expected outcomes
1/ Consolidate the student's existing skills to accurately and safely operate diagnostic ultrasound equipment to acquire images of the glenohumeral joint (GHJ) and Subacromial Subdeldoid Bursa (SASB)
2/ To interpret the complexity of anatomy and pathologies encountered when incorporating Point of Care UltraSound (PoCUS) of the shoulder joint into an urgent patient management pathway in suspicion of a joint/bursal effusion or GHJ dislocation
-Become a safe, experienced PoCUS practitioner

-Detailed educational outcomes can be viewed on a pdf document at this link:
https://aiuappboxcom/s/p26w1mrdnnbspukbv1mpe2yl5ks0ej52

Course Title: Graduate Certificate of Clinical Mental Health
Course Description:
The GC CMH programm aims to provide higher education in mental health for the growing and evolving post-graduate learning needs of mental health professionals and practitioners in Singapore and the region. The programme is designed to provide an opportunity for clinically trained individuals, who wish to gain advanced clinical knowledge in neuroscience and mental health conditions (across the age continuum from children, adolescents, adults and elderly). 

This graduate certificate programme is also ideal for students who are keen to embark on a career as a mental health service practitioner, or mid-career professionals seeking to make a career switch to the healthcare industry and gain relevant knowledge in the area of mental health.

Course Title: Graduate Certificate In Critical Care Nursing
Course Description: The programme aims to deepen the critical care competencies (clinical, technical, and interpersonal) adopting a work-study approach. The curriculum aligns with the American Association of Critical Care Nurses (AACN) Essentials.

Course Title: Master of Science in Behavioural and Implementation Sciences in Health (MScBIS)
Course Description:
The Master of Science in Behavioural and Implementation Sciences in Health (MScBIS) is offered by the Yong Loo Lin School of Medicine (NUSMed) as a full time (1 year) or part-time (2 years) postgraduate degree programme by Coursework that aims to produce graduates with in-depth knowledge and expertise in behavioural and implementation sciences, applied to healthcare settings through

Learning state-of-the-art behavioural and organisational change methods, which you will then apply in healthcare organisations in Singapore – and further afield,
Mastering methodological and statistical approaches to evaluate the implementation, dissemination and clinical and population health impact of evidenced interventions, and
Applying theoretical principles and practical techniques of economic analysis essential to inform health policy and health care decision-making.
The programme will be taught by leading specialists at the forefront of behavioural and implementation sciences research and practice, as well as senior policy makers and leaders in healthcare. It has a rigorous focus on the cross-disciplinary theoretical, empirical, and applied fundamentals of behavioural and implementation sciences. You can check our faculty members here.

MScBIS is targeted at healthcare professionals, management teams, policy makers and thought leaders.
