This programme is also relevant to anyone at any stage of their career who are conducting or are involved in health services or quality improvement projects and implementation work in their workplace, and anyone who seeks knowledge in the areas of behavioural and implementation sciences as key in solving the challenges you currently face.

Course Title: Master of Science in Infectious Disease Emergencies (MSc IDE)
Course Description:
The MSc IDE will provide participants with advanced knowledge and skills relating to preparation for and response to infectious disease crises. Our curriculum combines rigorous academic study with practical, real-world applications across all aspects of outbreak management. The qualification will enable careers in health ministries and international, national and subnational public health organisations.

The four main objectives that we hope to meet with this programme are:
- Education: Provide a training pathway for education and provide the required trainings for leadership, operations, clinical management, and academic career tracks in infectious disease emergencies.
- Clinical Initiatives: Enable learners to appreciate the components of outbreak response so that in their line of work they can better prepare the region and its countries for a holistic coordinated infectious disease emergency response.
- Real World Experiences: Exposure of learners to real-world challenges and experiences through experiential learning and thought sharing amongst peers and international experts.
- Thought Leadership: Build international networks and thought leadership in IDEs.

Course Title: Advanced Skills in Pharmacy Practice (Classroom and Asynchronous)
Course Description: To familiarize the  student with important skills in patient interviews, history taking, communications, as well as basic physical exam techniques utilized in the retail, ambulatory care and acute care settings. Students will be assessed mostly by Objective Structured Clinical Examinations (OSCEs).

Course Title: Developing health proposals using DIME skills & tools
Course Description:
At the end of the course, the participants will be able to:

1. List 5 necessary skills to develop to become a good proposal-writer.
2. Cite 8 characteristics against which to judge a good proposal.
3. Outline a full proposal with 7 obligatory headings.
4. Write good programme goals and Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Realistic, and Timely (SMART) objectives.
5. Understand and apply basic proposal terminology.

Course Title: Contemporary Global Health Issues
Course Description:
1. Articulate how key transitions have shaped the current global disease trends and burden, including global health security, health governance, the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), the new global health architecture, global health financing, mobility and
migration, universal health coverage, and climate change.
2. Recognize how complex and intertwined social and commercial determinants of health contribute to health and wealth inequalities within and between countries, and debate some of the contemporary solutions proposed.
3. Critically examine global health policies and responses to infectious diseases, non-communicable diseases, and humanitarian emergencies and their potential to improve health and quality of life, including the role of existing and proposed global health innovations and partnerships, using multi-disciplinary perspectives.

Course Title: Advanced Quantitative Methods I
Course Description:
Upon completion of the course, the participants will be able to:
1. Build statistical models for outcomes involving binomial, normal, survival data or repeated measures data.
2. Assess model fit, confounding and effect modification, and interpret the effect estimates
3. Discuss the uses and limitations of multi-variable analyses.

Course Title: Advanced Quantitative Methods II
Course Description:
Upon completion of this course, you will be able to:
1. Build statistical models for outcomes involving binomial, normal, survival or repeated measures data.
2. Discuss and test the validity of the assumption underlying each model.
3. Assess model fit, confounding and effect modification.
4. Interpret the effect estimates meaningfully and make appropriate inferences.
5. Discuss the uses and limitations of multi-variable analyses.
6. Apply the statistical models learnt to real life medical or health outcome data.
7. Independently analyse medical or public health research data.

Course Title: Age Related Cataract and Symptomatic Anterior Eye Diseases (Classroom & Asynchronous e-learning)
Course Description:
This module provides clinical optometrists a refresher on the aetiology, pathophysiology, clinical presentation, diagnostic methods and management strategies of anterior eye diseases, in particular age related cataracts.

The module will allow clinical optometrists to make use of the clinical skills learnt in the previous module to fulfil the following Competency Framework of Clinical Optometry & Community Screening (COCS), approved by Ministry Of Health.

•	posterior polar cataract
•	anterior subcapsular cataract
•	white cataract
•	brunescent cataract
•	subluxated cataract
•	Anterior Chamber Intra-Ocular Lens (ACIOL)
•	multifocal/toric Intra-Ocular Lens (IOL)
•	Anterior Chamber (AC) cell gradings
•	post-operative corneal oedema
•	Posterior Capsular Opacities (PCO)
•	yttrium-aluminum-garnet (YAG) capsulotomy
•	aphakia

Course Title: The Collaborative Practitioners Prescribing Programme
Course Description:
The specific objectives are to equip the nurses and pharmacists with skills and knowledge to
•	Assess patients and perform the diagnostic phase of care, and
•	Prescribe pharmacological and non-pharmacological treatment and perform the therapeutic phase of care independently 
in the context of team-based care that promotes care accessibility and affordability, and under the governance of institution-based Collaborative Practice Agreements 

The instructional methods in The Collaborative Practitioners Prescribing Programme (CP3) are predicated on adult learning theories to ensure an appropriate balance of cognitive knowledge and hands-on clinical skills practice that are relevant to the APNs and pharmacists The programme of learning uses a hybrid of delivery methods: 
•	Lectures and case discussions via teleconferencing that enhance convenience and efficiency without compromising quality of learning, 
•	Medical interview and communication skills practice with standardised patients (aka role-players) via teleconferencing and face-to-face in classroom with immediate feedback under the guidance of experienced facilitators, and
•	Physical examination practice with standardised patients and real patients face-to-face in classroom with immediate feedback under the guidance of experienced facilitators 
The lecturers and facilitators are doctors, senior pharmacists, and APNs, who are knowledgeable and nurturing in the way they guide the trainees

Course Title: Doctor of Philosophy (CSI)
Course Description: This program aims to produce high-caliber researchers who are capable of independent research investigation, who will contribute original ideas that advance their specific field of cancer research, and are well-equipped to pursue careers in clinical science.

Course Title: Doctor of Philosophy (PhD)
Course Description: The PhD programme is designed for nurses and professionals in all healthcare disciplines with the keen interest to delve into the complexities of healthcare, explore cutting-edge research questions, and drive innovation in nursing practice, education, and healthcare policy.

Course Title: Doctor of Philosophy
Course Description: PhD degree program is primarily to prepare students for advanced research and academic careers by developing original contributions to knowledge, mastering research methodologies, and deepening expertise in a specialized field.

Course Title: Ultrasound for Extended Focused Assessment with Sonography in Trauma(eFast) - Level 2 (Interpretation) (Classroom, Synchronous & Asynchronous)
Course Description:
Course Goal
To utilise diagnostic ultrasound to answer the urgent clinical question: "Is there free fluid in the chest/abdomen or a pneumothorax?" in the emergency environment

Course expected outcomes
1) Consolidate the student's existing skills to accurately and safely operate diagnostic ultrasound equipment to acquire images of Pericardium (subxiphoid view), Right Upper Quadrant (RUQ), Left Upper Quadrant (LUQ), pelvis and lung (windows for pneumothorax and pleural effusion) 
2) To interpret the complexity of anatomy and pathologies encountered when incorporating Point of Care UltraSound (PoCUS) into an urgent patient management pathway in suspicion of free fluid or free air in chest/abdomen
-Become a safe, experienced PoCUS practitioner

Course Title: Refraction in Children and Evidence Based Myopia Control
Course Description: To equip participants with skills and knowledge on good practices and standard for pediatric myopia assessment, monitoring and management.

Course Title: High Performance Computing for Precision Medicine
Course Description:
Learners should be able to 
1) Evaluate and explain the runtime complexity of algorithms used in analysing data for precision medicine 
2) Describe the architecture of high-performance systems and relate it to the latencies in processing data 
3) Evaluate data processing workflows and identify bottlenecks using profiling methods 
4) Explain the different models of parallel computing 
5)Evaluate and identify suitable applications that utilize parallel computing for accelerating workflows 
6) Parallelize a high-throughput workflow and optimize the bottlenecks where necessary
7) Containerize applications and binaries for reproducible computing in a shared high-performance system environment

Course Title: Techniques in Biomedical Research
Course Description:
Learning Objectives:

1) Understand the fundamental principles underlying common experimental techniques
2) Compare and contrast different techniques to identify the one best suited for each application
3) Evaluate the advantages and limitations of each technique to better troubleshoot or repurpose different tools for various purposes
4) Maintain awareness of evolving technology and keep up to date with new advances in techniques
5) Critically analyze self-authored experimental data to determine the degree of relevance to the original hypothesis
6) Critically analyze third parties’ published papers and work to evaluate the robustness of the conclusions

Course Title: Master of Clinical Mental Health and Psychotherapy
Course Description:
The Master’s degree in Clinical Mental Health and Psychotherapy is a comprehensive, applied, and skill-based academic training programme, providing a robust foundation in mental health conditions, psychology interventions, psychotherapy & counselling, and case & care coordination.

It aims to provide higher education and specialised clinical training for the growing and evolving post-graduate learning needs of mental health professionals and practitioners in Singapore and the region.

Clinical mental health professionals require a diverse set of therapy skills to effectively address the needs of their clients. The programme encompasses research, screening and assessment for common mental health disorders, cognitive behaviour therapy, dialectical behaviour therapy, interpersonal psychotherapy, mindfulness, parenting, group psychotherapy, social skills training, and behavioural interventions.

Course Title: Master of Dental Surgery
Course Description: To provide residency training programmes leading to postgraduate specialist qualification in dentistry.

Course Title: NUSMed Graduate Research Degree Programme - Doctor of Philosophy (PhD)
Course Description: Training of  biomedical- and clinician-scientists.

Course Title: Executive Certificate in Evidence-informed Practice and Policies in Healthcare
Course Description: This specialised course is tailored for middle to senior-level healthcare professionals who aim to lead change in their settings. With a focus on developing clinical practice guidelines and public policies, the course offers a rigorous, evidence-based approach to healthcare decision-making at various levels. Participants will journey from understanding the fundamentals of healthcare leadership and the role of evidence, to mastering economic evaluation and implementation strategies. The course concludes with practical application, guiding students in translating evidence into clinical guidelines and public policy recommendations.

Course Title: Executive Certificate in Fundamentals of Behavioural and Implementation Sciences
Course Description: This course will equip students with the foundational understanding of Behavioural science and Implementation science and how to apply their core concepts in healthcare.

Course Title: Executive Certificate in Intermediate Behavioural and Implementation Sciences in Health and Healthcare
Course Description: This course provides students with a deeper understanding of how Behavioural and Implementation sciences can be used together to improve health.

Course Title: Executive Certificate in Intermediate Research Methods for Behavioural and Implementation Sciences
Course Description: This course provides students with an understanding of how to conduct a research project and a working knowledge of the research methodologies used in Behavioural and Implementation Sciences.

Course Title: Executive Certificate in Introductory Research Methods in Behavioural and Implementation Sciences
Course Description: This course will familiarise students with the concepts in how to conduct a research project and a working knowledge of the research methodologies used in Behavioural and Implementation Sciences.

Course Title: Executive Certificate in Programme Evaluation in Health
Course Description: This course will equip the students in skills to conduct different forms of health and healthcare programme evaluations in different contexts (as an internal or external evaluator of a programme). The students will acquire practical skills on how to prepare for an evaluation, conduct an evaluation and appropriately disseminate the evaluation results to the relevant stakeholders and decision makers.

Course Title: Holistic Care for School-Aged Children
Course Description:
Knowledge and Insight (Understanding)
• General Practitioner’s (GP’s) role in prevention and early identification of common learning, emotional, behavioural and health problems in school-going children, and how this role complements our school health service and specialist care
• Typical social emotional development in children, the concept of resilience, and how parents, teachers, our community & society can help to develop these skills
• Expected literacy and numeracy skills in school children
• Up-to-date epidemiology of common learning, emotional, behavioural and health problems in school-going children, and how this impacts epidemiology of chronic diseases in our future adult population, and changes public health priorities (The concept of “Healthy Weight, Healthy Children; Healthy Child, Brighter Future”)
• Up-to-date local epidemiology of adverse childhood events (ACEs) and non-accidental injury, and associated biopsychosocial, emotional and behavioural issues in children; governmental, community and social support/resources
Biopsychosocial challenges in lifestyle modification in school-going children and their families; how socioeconomic status contributes to change sustainability & stages of behavioural change

Application of Knowledge & Insight
• With the Touchpoints approach (Module GCP1004), continue to utilize opportunistic touchpoints to screen for learning, emotional, behavioural and health issues as the pre-schooler advances into primary school education

Course Title: Adolescent Health
Course Description:
Knowledge and Insight (Understanding)
• Erikson’s psychosocial tasks for adolescents: identity formation versus confusion; relations with parents, peers, partners.
• Biopsychosocial approach to sexuality and sexual health. 
• Biopsychology of sexual orientation versus gender dysphoria.
• Biopsychosocial developmental effects of chronic conditions on adolescents, including effects on autonomy and compliance.


Application of Knowledge & Insight
• Psychosocial assessment using H.E.A.D.S.S. framework (used in ideal settings versus time-pressured busy clinic settings). 
• Sleep physiology, sleep hygiene and identifying risks for sleep disorders.
• Mental health screening: spirituality, moods, anxiety, stress, bullying/cyberbullying, depression, suicidal threats, including maladaptation, coping mechanisms and resilience building.  
• Addiction screening: gaming, social media, internet, smoking, alcohol, substance use, other risk-taking behaviours.

Appraisal (formation of a judgement)
• Growth and development: pubertal stages, early mid to late adolescence.
• Normal versus abnormal behaviours: adolescent adjustment reactions versus behavioural problems; oppositional defiant disorders, conduct issues.
• Learning issues: dyslexia, attention deficit hyperactivity disorders, autistic spectrum disorders.
• Eating disorders: normal versus abnormal dieting. 
• The pregnant teen.
• Contraception, Sexually Transmitted Infection (STI) screening and management. 
• Skin problems such as acne, and related psychosocial effects.
Functional pains (psychosomatisation), identifying red flags for organic conditions, including heavy menstruation bleeding.

Communication Skills
• Youth friendly consultation: confidentiality, ethics, medico-legal.
• Motivational interviewing: strategies to facilitate adolescent behaviour change.
• Anticipatory guidance for adolescents (for parents/ caregivers).

Course Title: Ultrasound for Knee - Level 3 (Integration) (Classroom, Synchronous & Asynchronous)
Course Description:
Expected learning outcomes:

- To obtain the skills to integrate  Point of Care UltraSound (PoCUS) of the knee into the patient management pathway in assessment of urgent knee pain.
-Become a safe, experienced PoCUS practitioner.
-A practitioner who completes this program will be recognised and acknowledged as being competent in performing point of care ultrasound for their patient's welfare. This standard currently does not exist, making Singapore a leader in this field
-Please review the detailed educational outcomes which may be viewed on the pdf document at this link: https://aiu.app.box.com/s/pauzgp520ce4c0ynvag5q6js0jinfyic

Course Title: Quality Improvement in Healthcare
Course Description:
Expected Learning Outcomes: 

 - Understand quality improvement principles and tools
 - Apply the tools to problem areas in the healthcare environment

Course Title: Introduction to Health Econometrics (Synchronous e-Learning)
Course Description: This course aims to equip students with fundamental knowledge in econometric methods commonly applied in health economics and outcomes research (HEOR). Students start from understanding basic statistical theory and learn to conduct real world effectiveness studies using common methods that control for selection and other biases.

Course Title: Introduction to Integrated Care
Course Description:
Through this module, the student will learn to:
1. Appreciate the societal context and the foundational concepts (of populations, consumers, systems and policies) that undergird theory and practice in Integrated Care.
2. Understand the constructs for the delivery of Integrated Care, including services and models, capabilities and tools, and resources and financing.
3. Contextualise their own programmes within these theories and practices in relation to their own health and social care organisations and to apply insights and processes to their real-world work.
4. Critique the pilots and implementations underway with appreciation of the complexities and subtleties of a relatively under-understood domain.

Through points 1 to 4, the student will:
5. Plan and design Integrated Care programmes in the health and care delivery services and the community.
6. Pilot and scale such programmes for actual patient, family and societal impact.
7. Market, enable and activate patients and caregivers to make good use of such programmes.
8. Evaluate the efficiency, effectiveness and impact of such programmes, and conduct research to further improve the delivery of health and social care.

Course Title: Graduate Certificate of Psychotherapy
Course Description:
The GCP programme is an applied, skill-based academic training programme that aims to provide training for psychology interventions and psychotherapy skills for mental health conditions. The programme is designed to provide higher education and clinical skills training for the growing and evolving post-graduate learning needs of mental health professionals and practitioners in Singapore and the region. The programme is designed to provide an opportunity for allied health professionals or professionals working in the mental health industry such as case managers who want to augment their current roles.

The demand for professionals with a GCP is driven by the increasing recognition of the importance of mental health and the need for specialised psychotherapeutic skills in various sectors.

Course Title: Graduate Certificate in Intermediate Behavioural & Implementation Sciences in Health
Course Description:
This Graduate Certificate is offered as part of the new Master of Science in Behavioural and Inplementation Sciences in Health (MBIS) in order for interested candidates to take a bite size course load that gives them a good flavor of what is behavioural and implementation sciences as well as grounding in the fundamentals of behavioural and implementation sciences so as to serve their current needs or interests.

This graduate certificate consists of three courses within MScBIS which are also available as individual Executive certificates to provide a more flexible pathway that may better meet the needs of busy working adults/professionals. This GC or the individual ECs with a minimum CAP of 3.5 and at least 90% attendance is stackable towards MScBIS.

Course Title: Graduate Certificate in Integrated Health
Course Description: The programme aims to deepen competencies in the integration of healthcare services and practices to improve patient outcomes and healthcare delivery efficiency, adopting a work-study approach.

Course Title: Master of Science (MSc) in Applied Biomedicine
Course Description:
The MSc-ABM seeks to develop students in their critical and analytical thinking in the complex and evolving healthcare landscape; training them to be resourceful and adaptable independent learners and leaders in the biomedical workforce.  The degree also aims to impart the following skill sets and knowledge to the students.

In-depth and updated specialist knowledge and technologies in biomedicine
Ability to apply biomedical knowledge to practise
Ability to formulate a hypothesis and design a scientifically sound project
By arming students with the necessary skills and knowledge, the MSc-ABM will provide a steady supply of skilled professionals to the healthcare and biopharmaceutical industries in particular research institutes, ministries, and hospitals where the disciplines of infectious diseases management, vaccine development/ immunotherapy and drug discovery/ development are critical.

Course Title: Master of Science (MSc) in Biomedical Informatics
Course Description: The Master of Science in Biomedical Informatics (MSc in BMI) offered by the Yong Loo Lin School of Medicine (NUSMed) as a full time (1 year) or part-time (2 years) postgraduate degree programme by Coursework. It is a game-changing program designed to prepare healthcare professionals for the future. Situated at the intersection of healthcare and technology, this program equips students with core skills such as Large Language Models (LLM), evidence-based clinical decision-making, machine learning and artificial intelligence, data visualization, effective communication, strategic leadership, and entrepreneurship.

Course Title: Master of Science (Nursing)
Course Description: The MSc (Nursing) programme is designed to empower students to advance their research knowledge and skills in healthcare. Whether they are a nurse or a professional in other healthcare disciplines, with the aspiration to take their career to new heights, our programme offers a comprehensive curriculum tailored to meet their professional goals. Our MSc (Nursing) programme will equip students with the knowledge, skills, and confidence to excel in their chosen path either as a healthcare professional in pursuit of a career in health research, or to become a nurse practitioner, nurse educator, nurse leader with critical thinking and research knowledge and skills. There is also the opportunity to progress into our Doctor of Philosophy programme.

Course Title: NUSMed Graduate Research Degree Programme - Master of Science (MSc)
Course Description: Training of  biomedical- and clinician-scientists.

Course Title: Advanced Community Case Studies
Course Description:
Upon completion of the course, students should be able to:
1. Describe patient’s journey through acute illness and transition processes between care settings
2. Perform patient assessment, which includes individual history-taking
3. Draft pharmaceutical care plans, through performing pharmacotherapy work-up and identifying patient-specific care goals and plan
4. Communicate (verbal/written) care plan to patient and/or recommendations to health care professionals
5. Perform necessary documentation of patient encounter and care plan
6. Conduct clinical case presentations

Course Title: Graduate Diploma in Geriatric Medicine
Course Description: The Graduate Diploma in Geriatric Medicine [GDip (Geri Med)] Programme is offered by the Division of Graduate Medical Studies (DGMS), NUS Yong Loo Lin School of Medicine, in collaboration with the Society for Geriatric Medicine Singapore (SGMS). The Programme is designed to provide primary care physicians with basic skills in caring for the older adults..

Course Title: Advanced Epidemiology II
Course Description:
1. Understand the strengths, limitations, and principles of different study designs.
2. Identify and interpret effect modification
3. Identify potential sources of selection and information bias, reverse causation and regression to the mean, confounding and judge how this may affect results. Understand how to control bias by appropriate study design and rigorous conduct of epidemiological studies
4. Identify potential sources of confounding and understand how to address confounding in the design and analysis of epidemiological studies
5. Explain considerations for causal inference and models of causality
6. Understand the concepts ‘validity’ and ‘precision’, ‘random’ and ‘systematic measurement error’, ‘differential’ and ‘non-differential misclassification’, and the use of validation and reproducibility studies for epidemiological research
7. Understand the general principles and strengths and limitations of systematic reviews and meta-analyses and judge when these are useful
8. Understand the different steps of conducting a systematic review, causes of publication bias, and the importance of evaluating heterogeneity in study results and able to interpret the main statistical measures and graphs used in meta-analyses
9. Understand major considerations specific to the design and interpretation of lifestyle, molecular epidemiology and cancer epidemiology studies
10. Judge the quality of articles on epidemiological studies and be able to interpret these articles
11. Use causal diagrams to describe hypothetical relationships between variables
12. Understand the use of instrumental variables and mediation analysis to assist causal inference for relationships between exposures and health outcomes
13. Understand important considerations in attempts to translate epidemiological findings to public health policy
14. Develop a research grant application. Apply the methods to the design of a research study

Course Title: Advanced Epidemiology I
Course Description:
Upon completion of this course, you will be able to:

1. Understand the strengths, limitations, and principles of different study designs including retrospective casecontrol, nested case-control, case-cohort, cohort, cross-sectional, and ecological studies and randomised controlled trials

2. Identify and interpret effect modification

3. Identify potential sources of selection and information bias, reverse causation and regression to the mean, judge how this may affect results, and understand how to control bias by appropriate study design

4. Identify potential sources of confounding and understand how to address confounding in the design and analysis of epidemiological studies

5. Explain commonly used considerations for causal inference and models of causality

6. Understand the concepts ‘validity’ and ‘precision’, ‘random’ and ‘systematic measurement error’, ‘differential’ and ‘non-differential misclassification’, and the use of validation and reproducibility studies for epidemiological research

7. Understand the general principles and strengths and limitations of systematic reviews and meta-analyses and be able to judge when these are useful

8. Understand the different steps of conducting a systematic review, causes of publication bias, and the importance of evaluating heterogeneity in study results and be able to interpret the main statistical measures and graphs used in meta-analyses

9. Understand major considerations specific to the design and interpretation of molecular epidemiology studies.

10. Judge the quality of articles on epidemiological studies and be able to interpret from these articles

Course Title: Economic Methods in Health Technology Assessment
Course Description:
At the end of this course, participants  
1) To be able to interpret and determine quality of HTA research conducted by others.
2) To be able to conduct economic assessments of public health and medical interventions at the basic level.

Course Title: Measuring and Managing Quality of Care
Integrating and Sustaining Total Workplace Safety and Health at the Workplace
Course Description:
At the end of the course, participants will be able to:
1. Describe the challenges and solutions to implementing Total Workplace Safety and Health(WSH).
2. Employ Total Workplace Safety and Health(WSH) principles to integrate and sustain Total WSH at the workplace.

Course Title: Introduction to Total Workplace Safety and Health
Course Description:
At the end of the course, participants will be able to:
1. Describe the Total Workplace Safety and Health (WSH) Framework and how it relates to the practice of worker safety, occupational health, and well-being in the workplace.
2. Employ the Assessment-Intervention-Monitoring & Evaluation (A-I-ME)  framework to implement Total WSH at the workplace.

Course Title: Supporting Learning at the Workplace
Course Description:
The aim of this course is to provide an opportunity for practitioners to acquire knowledge and skills to guide learners in applying classroom knowledge to real world situation. Practitioners are require to draw on theoretical knowledge and clinical experience gained through the course to conduct a clinical teaching session at the point of care.

Expected Learning Outcomes of the Course:
1. Discuss the characteristics, roles and responsibilities of an effective preceptor
2. Apply the adult learning theory and clinical teaching skills to enhance clinical learning of learners
3. Assess learners’ performance and provide effective feedback to learners to improve their learning
4. Conduct a clinical teaching session to learners using the clinical teaching moments

Course Title: Voice Care for Teachers and Professional Voice Users – Basic
Course Description:
From the course, participants will be able to:
•	Describe principles of vocal health and healthy voice production.
•	Recognise when the voice is insufficient to support day-to-day function.
•	Identify and experience how voice impacts communication and the message.
•	Apply and adapt vocal techniques for use in different professional situations, e.g., classroom management and communicative scenarios.
•	Evaluate how flexibility and mastery over voice can impact job performance and career opportunities.

Course Title: Ultrasound for Gallbladder and Common Bile Duct - Level 1 (Acquisition) (Classroom and Asynchronous)
Course Description:
Objective:
To acquire a complete set of diagnostic quality views to answer the following protocol based clinical question, 'Is there common bile duct dilatation or evidence of cholecystitis?'

Expected Learning Outcomes:
1) Demonstrate effective use of an ultrasound machine
2) Optimise an ultrasound image
3) Recognise normal ultrasound anatomy and describe using sonographic terminology
4) Acquire images of a gallbladder in two planes and the Common Bile Duct (CBD) at porta hepatis
5) Record accurate measurements of the Gall Bladder (GB) wall and CBD diameter

Learning Strategies:
- Hands-on practical scanning on real volunteers and simulators using a maximum ratio of 1 tutor to 4 students
- Didactic presentations
- Online video, powerpoints and directed reading
- Case study submissions including images, with detailed constructive and honest feedback by a dedicated tutor
- Peer group support forum moderated and assisted regularly by skilled tutors

Benefits of Learning Outcomes:
- Expedited patient management pathways
- Improved patient outcomes
- Reduced healthcare costs through improved management, choice of further testing and base knowledge of ultrasound applications and reports

Faculty:
-Only experienced highly skilled ultrasound practitioners (Sonographers) and clinical specialists are employed to conduct the education program

Course Title: Ultrasound for Knee - Level 1 (Acquisition) (Classroom and Asynchronous)
Course Description:
Objective:
To acquire a complete set of diagnostic quality views to answer protocol based clinical questions

Expected Learning Outcomes:
1 Demonstrate effective use of an ultrasound machine
2 Optimise an ultrasound image
3 Recognise normal ultrasound anatomy and describe using sonographic terminology
4 Acquire images of the suprapatellar recess and postero-medial popliteal fossa

Learning Strategies:
- Hands-on practical scanning on real volunteers and simulators using a maximum ratio of 1 tutor to 4 students
- Didactic presentations
- Online video, powerpoints and directed reading
- Case study submissions including images, with detailed constructive and honest feedback by a dedicated tutor
- Peer group support forum moderated and assisted regularly by skilled tutors

Benefits of Learning Outcomes:
- Expedited patient management pathways
- Improved patient outcomes
- Reduced healthcare costs through improved management, choice of further testing and base knowledge of ultrasound applications and reports

Faculty:
-Only experienced highly skilled ultrasound practitioners (Sonographers) and clinical specialists are employed to conduct the education program

Course Title: Ultrasound for Gallbladder and Common bile duct (CBD) - Level 3 (Integration) (Classroom, Synchronous & Asynchronous)
Course Description:
Expected learning outcomes:

- To obtain the skills to integrate Gall bladder and biliary tree Point of Care UltraSound (PoCUS) findings into the patient management pathway in the urgent assessment of upper abdominal pain
-Become a safe, experienced PoCUS practitioner
-A practitioner who completes this program will be recognised and acknowledged as being competent in performing point of care ultrasound for their patient's welfare This standard currently does not exist, making Singapore a leader in this field
-Please review the detailed educational outcomes which may be viewed on the pdf document at this link: https://aiuappboxcom/s/81fx9dxbexsp4e2z3e4kkedozi60tcid

Course Title: Ultrasound for Gallbladder and Common bile duct - Level 2 (Interpretation) (Classroom, Synchronous & Asynchronous)
Course Description:
Objective:
To acquire a complete set of diagnostic quality views to answer the following protocol based clinical question, 'Is there hydronephrosis or urinary retention?'

Expected Learning Outcomes:
1 Demonstrate effective use of an ultrasound machine
2 Optimise an ultrasound image
3 Recognise normal ultrasound anatomy and describe using sonographic terminology
4 Acquire images of ta kidney in 2 planes and a post micturition bladder volume

Learning Strategies:
- Hands-on practical scanning on real volunteers and simulators using a maximum ratio of 1 tutor to 4 students
- Didactic presentations
- Online video, powerpoints and directed reading
- Case study submissions including images, with detailed constructive and honest feedback by a dedicated tutor
- Peer group support forum moderated and assisted regularly by skilled tutors

Benefits of Learning Outcomes:
- Expedited patient management pathways
- Improved patient outcomes
- Reduced healthcare costs through improved management, choice of further testing and base knowledge of ultrasound applications and reports

Faculty:
-Only experienced highly skilled ultrasound practitioners (Sonographers) and clinical specialists are employed to conduct the education program

Course Title: Ultrasound for Knee - Level 2 (Interpretation) (Classroom, Synchronous & Asynchronous)
Course Description:
Expected learning outcomes:
Course Goal
To utilise diagnostic ultrasound to answer the urgent clinical question: "Is there a joint effusion or a Baker's cyst?" in the emergency environment

Course expected outcomes
1/ Consolidate the student's existing skills to accurately and safely operate diagnostic ultrasound equipment to acquire images of the suprapatellar recess and postero-medial popliteal fossa
2/ To interpret the complexity of anatomy and pathologies encountered when incorporating Point of Care UltraSound (PoCUS) of the knee joint into an urgent patient management pathway in suspicion of a joint effusion or a Baker's cyst
-Become a safe, experienced PoCUS practitioner

-Detailed educational outcomes can be viewed on a pdf document at this link:
https://aiuappboxcom/s/g8tc25ahihorku92l4ikt7rafjjj5zsh

Course Title: Maternal and Infant Health Across Perinatal Period (Classroom & Asynchronous)
Course Description:
Learners will be able to:
1. illustrate the purpose, rationale, activities, and performance measures for promoting care to pregnant women, new mothers, and their new-borns.
2. understand importance of incorporating family-centred care in enhancing overall well-being of pregnant couples, new mothers, and the infants across the perinatal period; 
3. identify factors to promote breast-feeding.
4. practice and promote family-centered care to support new parents in the community settings;
5. provide breast-feeding support and education to new mothers.
6. advocate health and well-being of pregnant women, new mothers and their new-borns across the perinatal period; and
7. adopt culturally appropriate care for maternal and infant health.

Course Title: Maternal and Infant Health Across Perinatal Period (Synchronous & Asynchronous)
Course Description:
Course Objectives
Learners will be able to:
1. illustrate the purpose, rationale, activities, and performance measures for promoting care to pregnant women, new mothers, and their new-borns.
2. understand importance of incorporating family-centred care in enhancing overall well-being of pregnant couples, new mothers, and the infants across the perinatal period; 
3. identify factors to promote breast-feeding.
4. practice and promote family-centered care to support new parents in the community settings;
5. provide breast-feeding support and education to new mothers.
6. advocate health and well-being of pregnant women, new mothers and their new-borns across the perinatal period; and
7. adopt culturally appropriate care for maternal and infant health.

Course Title: Working Safely in Biosafety Level 3 (BSL-3) Laboratories
Course Description:
This course aims to equip the learners with the following expected learning outcome:
- Understand essential concept of biorisk management (BRM) system and the importance of BRM system for managing biological hazards in BSL-3 facilities. 
- Understand and identify the hazards associated with biological agents and biological toxins, as well as other types of hazards (e.g., chemical, physical, animals, etc.) related to working in BSL-3 Facilities and implement appropriate risk controls and operational measures. 
- Understand and identify biosecurity related hazards (personal security, information security, facility security, etc.) associated with biological agents and biological toxins, as well as to implement appropriate control measures.
- Recognise the responsibilities as user of the BSL-3 facility.
- Manage biological risks (biosafety & biosecurity), conduct risk assessment, apply safe practices, and control measures arising from activities pertaining to biological agents and toxins. 
- Manage and respond to incidents due to spillage of hazardous materials, exposure to infectious hazardous agents or any other emergency situations in the laboratory. 
- Understand the requirements for audits, inspections, and follow-up with corrective and preventive measures. 
- Contribute to continuous improvement and understand the procedures and follow-up from management review. 
- Maintain necessary documentation and records pertaining to biosafety, biosecurity, occupational safety and health management, audit and inspection reports, incident and emergency response reports as required by legislations.

Course Title: How Informatics can enable 'Value Based Healthcare'
Course Description:
Expected Learning Outcomes: 

1)	Understand "Value Based HealthCare" and the key enablers that are needed to make the shift to a value-based healthcare approach from a traditional healthcare setting

2)	Understand the role of Data and Health informatics when implementing "Value Based HealthCare"

3)	A Case Study: How National University Health System (NUHS) has attempted to implement "Value Based Healthcare" in Singapore’s context.

Course Title: Introduction to Health Economic Modelling (Synchronous e-Learning)
Course Description: This course aims to equip students with basic skills required to perform their own cost-effectiveness analyses of health technologies and to critically assess the quality of evaluations conducted by others. Students will understand different types of economic evaluation methods and how to value health gains and costs. Thus students will be equipped fundamental skills and knowledge in research design, preparing them to conduct independent research or contribute to research projects in academic, professional, or organizational settings.

Course Title: Health Technology Assessment, Cost-Effectiveness and Decision-making (Synchronous & Asynchronous e-learning)
Course Description:
At the end of the course, students will be able to:

1) Complete their own health technology assessment project and understand the rationale and methods for doing one.
2) Understand the principles of welfare economics, market failures and how they apply to the supply of health care services.
3) Be able to build and evaluate a cost-effectiveness model
4) Be able to read and interpret a published cost-effectiveness study, able to collaborate with a health economist for a new study.
5) Understand the scientific paradigms of decision making and how they come into conflict with traditional scientific approaches.

Course Title: Urban Outbreak Management (Classroom and Synchronous e-learning)
Course Description:
At the end of the module, participants should be able to:
1 . Apply field epidemiologic methods to deal with clusters of unusual symptoms, including:
• Verifying the existence of an outbreak
• Assessing threat to vulnerable populations
• Performing surveillance and data display
• Designing field studies
• Finding cause and effects
2. Implement outbreak investigation and control using a systematic stepwise approach, including:
• Conducting case definition and active case finding
• Giving timely and accurate outbreak descriptions
• Formulating hypotheses and making inferences
• Carrying out control measures
• Communicating public health findings
3. Acquire skills as a disease detective (public health specialist) to effect preventive actions, including:
• Articulating public health policy dilemmas
• Engaging in negotiations with stakeholders
• Getting community participation
• Instituting epidemic intelligence & capacity building
• Promoting awareness on global health matters
4. Perform evidence-based risk management by learning from historical outbreaks through interactive case studies.

Course Title: Health Systems and Policy Analysis
Course Description:
1. Understand public health issues and relate it to the health system in Singapore and the region.
2. Understand the process of how policies are formulated.
3. Understand how to utilize the different types of data in the policymaking process.
4. Apply various theoretical frameworks to analyse health policy issues.
5. Apply various theoretical frameworks to analyse a health system.
6. Utilize research methods to critique and analyse health policies and health systems.
7. Develop a research proposal to analyse a current policy.
8. To understand health systems reforms within a wider ecosystem.

Course Title: Informatics for Health
Course Description:
Upon completion, students will be able to use knowledge of patient care combined with understanding of informatics concepts, methods, and tools to:
1. Assess information and knowledge needs of health care professionals and patients.
2. Characterize, evaluate, and refine clinical processes.
3. Develop, implement, and refine clinical decision support systems, and lead or participate in the procurement, customization, development, implementation, management, evaluation, and continuous improvement of clinical information systems.

Course Title: Introduction to Health Services Research
Course Description:
Upon completion of the course, participants will be able to:
1. Understand mainstream health services research methods and their applications.
2. Evaluate and use instruments for assessing self-reported health outcomes.
3. Understand and critique economic evaluation studies of health technologies and interventions.

Course Title: Public Health Communication
Course Description:
Upon completion of the course, the participant will
be able to: 
• Explain a conceptual model of the factors affecting population health and health inequalities; and define the individual-level and contextual-level social determinants of population health 
• Select and apply social and behavioural science theories and concepts to analyse health-related behaviour and decisional bases for public health- related actions 
• Select and apply social, psychological and behavioural science theories to change behaviour to promote health 
• Persuade and communicate individuals, groups and the community to adopt healthy lifestyle behaviours 
• Explain and describe the basic principles of health promotion programme evaluation 
• Communicate public health research evidence effectively to diverse
audiences including the public and policy makers to promote health in the community

Course Title: Qualitative Methods in Public Health
Course Description:
The main premise of this course is that qualitative research is a “skilled craft” that can provide a unique understanding of people’s lives, perceptions, experiences and the social phenomena that form and shape them.

Upon completion, students will be able to:
Describe qualitative methods and when the different types are most appropriately used.
Understand the debate related to qualitative methods contrasted to other approaches in public health.
Discuss Social Science traditions in healthcare
Consider the role of theory in health research
Design a research question
Discuss ethics, responsibilities and values
Undertake data collection for in-depth-interviews.
Identify a sampling frame.
Setup and facilitate focus group discussions.
Apply different approaches to observation (structured; participatory) and related data collection methods.
Manage and code data for thematic analyses.
Undertake a content analyses using media data.
Critically review the qualitative method identifying and discussing reflexivity, validity, reliability and approaches to generalisation.
Undertake framework analyses, following the Ritchie and Lewis’s method.
Write up analyses (using the COREQ statement), and present it, clearly, concisely and creatively.

Course Title: Women’s, Children’s and Adolescents’ Health
Course Description:
1)  Assess the current state and trends of women's children's and adolescent's health and well-being globally
2)  Link key factors (gender, behavioral, socio-economic, geographic, and policy etc.) influencing women's, children's and adolescent's vulnerability to disease and/or death
3) Critically evaluate progress on commitments, action and accountability to reduce or eliminate as a public health problem preventable deaths and illnesses among women, children and adolescents
4)  Identify the critical enablers to improve women's, children's and adolescent's health and well-being.

Course Title: Graduate Certificate in Fundamental of Behavioural & Implementation Sciences in Health
Course Description:
This Graduate Certificate is offered as part of the new Master of Science in Behavioural and Inplementation Sciences in Health (MBIS) in order for interested candidates to take a bite size course load that gives them a good flavor of what is behavioural and implementation sciences as well as grounding in the fundamentals of behavioural and implementation sciences so as to serve their current needs or interests.

This graduate certificate consists of three courses within MScBIS which are also available as individual Executive certificates to provide a more flexible pathway that may better meet the needs of busy working adults/professionals. This GC or the individual ECs with a minimum CAP of 3.5 and at least 90% attendance is stackable towards MScBIS.

Course Title: Graduate Diploma in Public Health
Course Description: The Graduate Diploma in Public Health is a programme designed to equip students with a comprehensive understanding of the interdisciplinary nature of public health. It provides a foundational knowledge of public health concepts and practices, as well as an appreciation of the importance of interdisciplinary collaboration to promote health and prevent disease.

Course Title: Graduate Diploma in Child and Adolescent Health
Course Description:
Jointly organised by the Division of Graduate Medical Studies and the Department of Paediatrics, NUS Yong Loo Lin School of Medicine, the Graduate Diploma in Child and Adolescent Health (GDCAH) is designed to provide continued professional development for physicians (general practitioners, family physicians and community paediatricians) who are motivated to advance their clinical expertise in the delivery of child and adolescent health in the community and in their family practice.

The GDCAH intends to build a community of practice made up of community physicians serving in our primary healthcare who are driven to pursue a journey of lifelong learning to continually improve our practice in delivering care to children and adolescents. This community of practice is scaffolded by provision of longitudinal mentorship and development of an enduring, interprofessional, collaborative relationship amongst family physicians and paediatricians.

Course Title: Graduate Diploma in Geriatric Dentistry
Course Description: “Deepen understanding of the epidemiology of medical and dental conditions which can impact geriatric oral health; Appreciate the influence of psychosocial determinants of health, oral health and oral health care among the elderly; Understand the impact of public health policy on the delivery of oral health care to older adults; Deliver holistic patient-centric oral health care to older adults in a variety of settings including primary care and IL TC; Diagnose, formulate patient-centric treatment plans and competently perform evidence-informed treatment and prevention procedures for older adults to address their oral health needs and associated medical conditions; Improve the oral health of the older adult population through individual patient care and public health advocacy.”

Course Title: Bachelor of Science (Nursing Practice)
Course Description: Please use the link to claim your SkillsFuture Credit if you are 40 years old and below. The BSc (Nursing Practice) aims to allow learners who are practising Registered Nurses in the workforce to be able to connect the learning with their work roles, develop their capabilities and improve patient outcomes.

Course Title: Motivational Interview Training for Health Professionals (Classroom & Synchronous)
Course Description: With the growing incidence of chronic diseases in Singapore, there is a need to ensure and enhance patient compliance to treatment. This programme is designed to enable health professionals to acquire relevant and related skills in motivating patients to embrace behaviour change and adopt habits in promotion of well-being.

Course Title: Management of Infectious Diseases at the Workplace
Course Description:
At the end of the course, participants will be able to:
1. Describe the principles of managing infectious diseases at the workplace.
2. Employ Total Workplace Safety and Health (WSH) principles to implement programmes to manage infectious diseases at the workplace.

Course Title: Ethics and Professionalism in Healthcare (Synchronous e-learning)
Course Description:
At the end of this course, learners will have an
• Overview of Health Informatics
• Overview of the different types of Healthcare Data and Information
• Understanding of Health Information Privacy and Security Laws
• Understanding of Health Informatics Ethics and Professionalism

Course Title: Empowering Elderly Mental Wellness & Revitalize Aging (Synchronous e-learning)
Course Description: In this course, participants will undertake a comprehensive exploration of the mental health challenges unique to the elderly. Emphasizing practical skills such as mindfulness and stress management, the course aims to promote emotional health in older adults. Learners will delve into the impacts of aging on cognitive functions and acquire strategies for maintaining cognitive wellness. The significance of social interactions in mitigating loneliness and isolation is a key focus, alongside managing conditions like depression, anxiety, and dementia. The course equips participants with resilience-building techniques and coping strategies to support the elderly through life changes and health challenges. Furthermore, it provides insights into ethical and cultural aspects of elderly mental health care, ensuring a sensitive approach to diverse senior populations. This holistic approach makes the course invaluable for both caregivers and those working with the elderly.

Course Title: Health Informatics & Data Visualisation
Course Description:
Expected Learning Outcomes: 

• Understand the  relevance of informatics in healthcare 
• Appreciate foundational concepts of healthcare informatics
• Understand healthcare data and its uses
• Understand the regulations around responsible use of healthcare data  
• Gain experience with consumer health devices
• Discuss case studies in healthcare
• Introduction to and demonstrate use of a data visualization tool

Course Title: Good Biosafety and Biosecurity Practices when Working with Experimental Animals
Course Description:
This course aims to equip learners with the following insights:
• Identify and assess hazards and risks associated with animals and agents to determine appropriate laboratory containment level
• Plan and develop appropriate programmes for addressing biorisk as well as the import, export and quarantine of animals and agents in accordance with national and international biosafety standards, guidelines and legislations
• Plan and develop appropriate programmes for addressing decontamination, waste and pest management as well as occupational health to minimise incidents and accidents at the workplace.
• Assist in resolving conflicts between biocontainment and animal welfare in order to ensure ethical and humane care and use of animals

Course Title: Postgraduate Course in Palliative Medicine for Doctors (Synchronous & Asynchronous)
Course Description:
At the end of the course, all participants will 
- understand and appreciate the principles and approach of supportive and palliative care and its importance in Singapore
- understand the principles of pain and symptom management in end of life care
- understand the principles of effective communication in supportive and palliative care
- understand palliative care in cancer and non-cancer conditions 
- understand the practical aspects of palliative care nursing 
- understand emergency conditions in palliative care
- understand ethical issues surrounding palliative care
- understand the principles of terminal care
- understand the principles of palliative care in frailty

Course Title: Cardiometabolic, Renal & dIabetes Optimisation Unified Services (C-RIOUS) Workshop
Course Description:
This workshop seeks to be an all-in-one solution to provide new budding practitioners a broad understanding of cardiometabolic diseases collectively and provide practical tips for management. Objectives include:

- Define what does cardiometabolic disease encompasses.  
- Summarize the interconnectedness between major chronic diseases like diabetes, cardio, renal and obesity. 
- Critic on the various management options available for cardiometabolic disease, from both pharmacological and non-pharmacological perspectives. 
- Relate to the multiple complications that can arise due to uncontrolled cardiometabolic disease. 
- Appreciate the need for inter-disciplinary care when dealing with a collective & inter-related disease like cardiometabolic disease. 
- Experience clinical attachments with a diverse pool of healthcare providers from different disciplines and settings.

Course Title: Pathology for Researchers: Histology and Basic Lab Techniques (Synchronous e-learning)
Course Description:
1  Laboratory techniques in tissue-based research
- Learn about the different types of tissue samples handled in a histopathology lab 
- Appreciate the spectrum of types of tests on tissue to detect proteins and nucleic acid
- Understand the workflow of tissue processing into formalin-fixed paraffin embedded (FFPE) tissue
- Understand the workflow of immunohistochemical testing 
- Be able to conduct basic interpretation of Immunohistochemistry (IHC) and Fluorescence in situ hybridization (FISH) 
- Know the workflow for extraction of nucleic acids from FFPE tissues for molecular testing (eg next-generation-sequencing [NGS])
2 Histology 
Learning objectives:  
- Be able to recognise normal histology of different tissue types (eg epithelial tissue,  mesenchymal tissue)  
- Learn how to recognise inflammatory cells in tissue   
- Be familiar with normal histology of a range of organs 
- Recognise histology of selected common cancers 
- Know how to perform hands-on examination of pathology microscopic slides using virtual microscopy

Course Title: Pharmacology Essentials for Pharmaceutical Professionals (Synchronous and Asynchronous e-learning)
Course Description:
The course aims to equip participants with knowledge and skills in the following:
1) Basic pharmacological concepts underlying how drugs work
2) Understand, interpret, and answer questions pertaining to information given on medication package inserts
3) Carry out searches of scientific literature concerning efficacy and adverse effects of specific drugs
4) Evaluate the quality of evidence in the literature and summarizing them for healthcare professionals 
5) Communicate the evidence behind the marketing materials of the pharmaceutical products they represent

Course Title: Introduction to Design Thinking and Artificial Intelligence in Healthcare Innovations
Course Description:
In collaboration with A*STAR (Agency for Science, Technology and Research) and the Department of Biomedical Informatics, NUS Yong Loo Lin School of Medicine, the workshop is tailored for healthcare professionals new to the field and eager to explore the synergy of Design Thinking with Artificial Intelligence (AI) in healthcare. 

It highlights key concepts in AI, Large Language Models (LLM), and Machine Learning through speaker sharing and National University Health System (NUHS) use case discussions. Through group work, participants will also gain hands-on experience with the design thinking process in identifying unmet healthcare needs, defining problems, and crafting innovative solutions. By the end of the workshop, participants will be equipped with the knowledge to contribute to better healthcare delivery through the seamless integration of AI and empathetic design.

Course Title: Establish, Implement and Maintain Biorisk Management System for Biomedical Laboratories
Course Description:
This course aims to equip learners with the following insights:
• Set up and implement a laboratory Biorisk Management (BRM) System
• Assess/evaluate and monitor of BRM performance through laboratory inspections and audits.
• Review of BRM system peformance and identify opportunities for continuous improvement.
• Laboratory Accreditation and Certification

Course Title: Ultrasound for eFAST - Level 1 (Acquisition) (Classroom and Asynchronous)
Course Description:
Objective:
To acquire a complete set of diagnostic quality views to answer protocol based clinical questions for an Extended Focused Assessment with Sonography in Trauma (eFAST) scan

Expected Learning Outcomes:
1) Demonstrate effective use of an ultrasound machine
2) Optimise an ultrasound image
3) Recognise normal ultrasound anatomy and describe using sonographic terminology
4) Acquire images of: Pericardium (subxiphoid view), Right Upper Quadrant (RUQ), Left Upper Quadrant (LUQ), pelvis and lung (windows for pneumothorax and pleural effusion)

Learning Strategies:
- Hands-on practical scanning on real volunteers and simulators using a maximum ratio of 1 tutor to 4 students
- Didactic presentations
- Online video, powerpoints and directed reading
- Case study submissions including images, with detailed constructive and honest feedback by a dedicated tutor
- Peer group support forum moderated and assisted regularly by skilled tutors

Benefits of Learning Outcomes:
- Expedited patient management pathways
- Improved patient outcomes
- Reduced healthcare costs through improved management, choice of further testing and base knowledge of ultrasound applications and reports

Faculty:
-Only experienced highly skilled ultrasound practitioners (Sonographers) and clinical specialists are employed to conduct the education program

Course Title: Restoring tooth, from apex to crown (Classroom and Practical Training)
Course Description:
Through the workshop, participants will be able to:
1. appreciate the various techniques of root canal obturation
2. apply the use of obturation material and equipment in safe and efficient manners
3. appreciate the various principles of direct and indirect foundation restorations
4. will have sufficient understanding of restorative spiral, and need for conservative dentistry
5. will be familiar with the indications and contra-indications of the various direct foundation restorations
6. will be familiar with the indications and contra-indications of the various indirect foundation restorations
7. will be able to prepare, and assess quality of fit of foundation restorations
8. will be familiar with the indication and contra-indication of the various extra-coronal restorations for cuspal protection
9. will be able to prepare, and provisionalise the different types of cuspal protection restorations

Course Title: Pharmacy-Based Immunization Delivery Certificate Training Program (Classroom and Asynchronous e-learning)
Course Description:
Educate pharmacists about: 
- The impact of vaccines on public health
- Pharmacists’ roles in immunization
- Immunologic principles of vaccine development and immunizations
- Vaccine-preventable diseases and the vaccines used to prevent them
- Strategies for improving immunization rates
- Requirements for pharmacists who provide immunization services
Prepare pharmacists to: 
- Read an immunization schedule and identify appropriate vaccines for individuals across the life span and with special needs
- Educate patients about the benefits of vaccines and dispel myths about vaccines
- Safely administer vaccines to patients via subcutaneous and intramuscular routes
- Operate an immunization service in compliance with legal and regulatory standards
- Partner with immunization stakeholders to promote immunizations and the immunization neighborhood

Course Title: Renal Supportive Care Course (Synchronous & Asynchronous)
Course Description:
The course will cover the following, 
- symptom control in advanced chronic kidney disease (CKD) in patients
- care of the dying patient with advanced CKD 
- role of supportive care in advanced CKD 
- geriatric syndromes (e.g., frailty, Dementia) in CKD
- serious illness communication in advanced CKD
- shared decision making with advanced CKD patients and their families
- Anxiety and depression in advanced CKD patients
- Ethical considerations in treatment decisions

Course Title: Cadaveric Dissection Workshop for Oral Maxillofacial Surgery Residents
Course Description:
The cadaveric workshop will allow participants to practice and enhance their skills in a safe and guided enviroment The workshop offers an excellent opportunity for OMS residents to expand their knowledge and skills in OMS and other surgical specialties Surgical dissection using fresh frozen cadavers allows teaching of human anatomy and intensive practical training in a wide variety of surgical techniques
Through this course, we hope that residents can learn:
i Basic anatomy principles
ii Develop and improves knowledges and skills in dissection
iii Bridge basic anatomical understanding with clinical applications in the field of OMS
iv Build a strong foundation for safe surgical practice

Course Title: Advanced Pharmacotherapy in Geriatrics (Classroom & Asynchronous)
Course Description:
Upon completion of the course, students should be able to:
1.  Assess and identify the clinical care needs of geriatric patients (of the disease states and/or conditions covered) 
2. Provide patient-specific recommendations relating to pharmacotherapy and non-pharmacotherapy interventions to meet the clinical care needs of geriatric patients 

Specific learning objectives will also be provided for each topic

Course Title: Women and Reproductive Health (Classroom & Asynchronous)
Women and Reproductive Health (Sychronous & Asynchronous)
Care of the New-born & Common Paediatric Surgical Issues
Course Description:
Knowledge and Insight (Understanding)
• General Practitioner’s (GP’s) role in our healthcare system in the care of the newborn 
• Components of newborn screening after birth, and what congenital abnormalities may surface later in the neonatal period
• Up-to-date knowledge and epidemiology of common neonatal conditions in neonates, and the importance of breastfeeding, timely immunizations, and parental guidance for neuro-stimulation 
• How socioeconomic status, psycho-emotional health of parents affects neonatal outcomes, and a child’s neurodevelopment from the very beginning
• Common congenital and surgical conditions presenting to primary healthcare, (e.g hypospadias and undescended testes)
• Range of presentation of tongue-tie in a newborn

Application of Knowledge & Insight
• Utility of National Neonatal Jaundice Guidelines to guide management of neonates with jaundice (including anticipatory parental advice)
• Counselling on breastfeeding; important tricks and tips in the first 2 weeks after childbirth, and troubleshooting common problems 
• Counselling first-time parents on parentcraft (common things to expect in the first 90 days of life, for example: expected weight gain, feeding frequency, fussing, sleeping position and habits, stool changes, common neonatal rashes) 
• Recognition of emotional changes and social issues in caregivers/families post-arrival of a newborn (such as postnatal blues, anxiety, depression)
• Recognize common lumps and bumps, including lymph node swelling
• Recognize urogenital complaints including phimosis and balanoposthitis 
• Recognize umbilical granuloma in the neonate and initiate treatment 
• Recognize variations of common acute complaints in children with prior paediatric surgical interventions (e.g nephrostomy, mitrofanoff) with identification of red flags for specialist intervention

Course Title: Fundamental Field Epidemiology
Course Description:
Upon completion of this course, participants will be able to:
1. Apply field epidemiologic methods to undertake rapid response on the ground
2. Assist in surveillance and outbreak investigation using a systematic approach; and
3. Acquire skills to effect preventive and preparedness actions.

Course Title: Essentials of Law for Healthcare Professionals (Classroom & Synchronous & Asynchronous e-learning)
Course Description:
Learners should be able to be able to:
- Engage in critical reflection on the issues covered in this module
- Understand the core legal principles pertaining to clinical practice
- Apply ethical analytical skills and ethical values when reviewing a particular case
- Identify and apply the relevant legal principles and legal precedents that apply to a particular case

Course Title: Management of Older Patients (Classroom & Asynchronous)
Course Description:
Upon completion of the course, students should be able to:
1. Describe the profile of older patients and their caregivers in various care environment/services available in Singapore.
2. Discuss the psychosocial, and self-management behaviours among older patients, as well as the associated factors/interventions that can influence medication management, adherence and use outcomes by older adults in the community.
3. Discuss the potential challenges and issues regarding chronic medication management by older patients in the community in a holistic manner, with regards to ethical considerations, medical, psychosocial and economic factors).
4. Identify situations and appropriate communication skills (and counselling framework) that can be applied during pharmacist-patient interactions in community-based pharmaceutical care services to promote collaborative self-management of illness, treatment and health by older adults and/or their caregivers.
5. Discuss signs and symptoms that require referrals, with due considerations of appropriate resources to refer to and the immediate response to be provided.

Course Title: Ultrasound for Extended Focused Assessment with Sonography in Traumac(eFast)  - Level 3 (Integration) (Classroom, Synchronous & Asynchronous)
Course Description:
Expected learning outcomes:
1) To obtain the skills to integrate  an Extended Focused Assessment with Sonography in Trauma (eFAST) protocol Point of Care UltraSound (PoCUS) into the patient management pathway in emergency assessment of shortness of breath and chest/abdominal pain.
2) Become a safe, experienced PoCUS practitioner.
3) A practitioner who completes this program will be recognised and acknowledged as being competent in performing point of care ultrasound for their patient's welfare. This standard currently does not exist, making Singapore a leader in this field
4) Please review the detailed educational outcomes which may be viewed on the pdf document at this link: https://aiu.app.box.com/s/n40c7tsvk1eia3hfq4cvmlzvykg15uzk

Course Title: Ultrasound for Shoulder - Level 3 (Integration) (Classroom, Synchronous & Asynchronous)
Course Description:
Expected learning outcomes:

- To obtain the skills to integrate  Shoulder Point of Care UltraSound (PoCUS) findings into the patient management pathway in the urgent assessment of  shoulder pain/dislocation.
-Become a safe, experienced PoCUS practitioner.
-A practitioner who completes this program will be recognised and acknowledged as being competent in performing point of care ultrasound for their patient's welfare. This standard currently does not exist, making Singapore a leader in this field
-Please review the detailed educational outcomes which may be viewed on the pdf document at this link: https://aiu.app.box.com/s/zmebr0le95vwf2v91ddcqwtur9y6u8ni

Course Title: Respond to Incidents and Accidents in Biomedical Facilities/Laboratories
Course Description:
This course aims to equip learners with the following insights:
• Plan and develop standard operating procedures (SOPs) to deal with laboratory incidents and accidents
• Conduct on-site assessment of incidents and accidents to determine appropriate immediate actions
• Conduct post-incident investigation to establish probable causes of the event as well as appropriate recommendations
• Report incidents and accidents to the appropriate party or authority in accordance with internal standard operating procedures and applicable legislations
• Implement appropriate recommendations in accordance with internal standard operating procedures and applicable legislations
• Monitor and review the effectiveness of recommendations to eliminate repeated incidents and accidents

Course Title: Glaucoma Detection & Monitoring (Classroom & Asynchronous e-learning)
Course Description:
This module provides clinical optometrists a refresher on the aetiology, pathophysiology, clinical presentation, diagnostic methods and management strategies for glaucoma.

Optometrists will learn to recognise the urgency in referring each case of glaucoma. They will monitor and manage the patients under their duty of care and refer the patients to the ophthalmologists in cases that requires secondary or tertiary eyecare.

The module will allow clinical optometrists to make use of the clinical skills learnt in the previous module to fulfil the following Competency Framework of Clinical Optometry & Community Screening (COCS), approved by Ministry Of Health (MOH).

•	Peripheral Iridotomy (PI) 
•	pseudoexfoliation
•	pigment dispersion
•	silicone oil
•	neovascular 
•	increased cup/disc (CD) ratio
•	Drance Haem
•	notching
•	asymmetric discs
•	iridectomy
•	diffuse bleb
•	avascular bleb
•	low-lying bleb
•	encapsulated bleb
•	filtration tubes
•	Minimally Invasice Glaucoma Surgery (MIGS)

Course Title: Interprofessional Collaborative Practices
Course Description:
The key generic skills are (1) service orientation, (2) communication, (3) teamwork, (4) problem solving, and (5) lifelong learning. The competency level of this course is set at basic level.
These non-technical skills are the identified top 5 generic skills in the Skills Framework for Healthcare professionals. 
These skills will equip healthcare professionals to enable and energise the working culture that is coherent with the World Health Organisation's Framework for Action on Interprofessional Education and Collaborative Practice.

Course Title: Foundations in Advanced Pharmacy Practice (Classroom & Asynchronous)
Course Description: This course is aimed at having students acquire effective drug information retrieval skills, literature evaluation skills and gaining a basic understanding of common biostatistical and study design principles

Course Title: Advanced Health Technology Assessment Methods (Synchronous e-Learning)
Course Description: Students will also gain hands-on experience with specialized cost-effectiveness modelling software enhancing their ability. Using TreeAge Pro, students will learn to build probabilistic sensitivity analyses, cost-effectiveness acceptability curves, and more sophisticated modelling strategies, including microsimulation and time-to-event modelling.

Course Title: Regulation of Innovative Therapies (Classroom and Asynchronous e-learning)
Course Description: On successful completion of this course,  you will be able to describe the concepts of regulation of Innovative Therapies (including cell, tissue and gene modalities and biologics), list the relevant regulatory guidelines for development these innovations, and explain the key requirements for the regulatory management and compliance of innovative therapies across the product life cycle.

Course Title: Evidence-based Practice
Course Description:
Upon completion of this programme, participants will be able to: 
1.Develop the ability to conduct comprehensive literature searches, ensuring paticipants from healthcare scetors can find and utilize the best available evidence.
2.Foster the capacity to apply evidence-based interventions effectively in patient care.
3.Evaluate the effectiveness of the evidenced-based practice on clinical outcomes.
4.Cultivate leadership and teamwork skills to advocate for and implement evidence-based practices within healthcare teams and organizations.
5.Prioritize evidence-based practice as a key component of quality improvement initiatives in healthcare settings.

Course Title: Human Genomics in Precision Medicine
Course Description:
Learners should be able to be able to:
1)	Know how genetics/genomics play roles in different types of diseases including Inherited, Complex and Infectious Diseases
2)	Learn how modern Omics and Analytic tools can be employed to identify disease biomarkers, predict, diagnose and prognose disease.
3)	Learn how genetic / genomic strategies can be developed to prevent / mitigate severity / treat disease. 
4)	Teach how Omics / Analytic tools or genetic/genomic strategies can be employed to identify, predict, diagnose, prognose, prevent, mitigate or treat disease

Course Title: Proteomics and Metabolomics in Precision Health and Medicine
Course Description:
Learners should be able to: 
1) Gain an understanding of key proteomic and metabolomic technological and their relevance to clinical research and implementation in Precision Health and Medicine. 
2) Develop a sense for trends in molecular systems biology and applications in human health. 
3) Be inspired in critical thinking of technological and clinical implementation challenges of new technologies.

Course Title: Care of the Well Child
Course Description:
Knowledge and Insight (Understanding)
• Standard recommendations for calorie and dietary requirements at various time points in childhood (neonate, infancy, early childhood, school-going children), including appropriate weaning during infancy.
• Enuresis and encopresis (normal development of bowel and bladder control in children) 
• Normal puberty and its variation including onset, progression and tempo 

Application of Knowledge & Insight
• Recognize normal growth and deviation from norms in weight, height and head circumference, including identifying early risks of malnutrition and obesity.
• Assess a child’s nutritional history and calorie consumption (physical activity) and identify areas for intervention 
• Provide appropriate advice on mandatory and optional childhood vaccination schedules, vaccine administration and potential adverse effects
