• Provide travel vaccines and travel advice, noting special circumstances (for example: child with Glucose-6-phosphate dehydrogenase deficiency (G6PD deficiency) , child on immunosuppressants, child on prolonged steroids)

Appraisal (formation of a judgement)
• Perform developmental assessment, and identify red flags of delay or regression
• Evaluate common rashes, institute appropriate first-line management and identify red flags for specialist referral
• Evaluate the fussy infant and identify red flags for specialist referrals, including offering advice for infant colic

Communication Skills
• Effectively communicate advice for vaccines, diet and developmental issues
• Counselling skills (providing reassurance and anticipatory guidance)

Course Title: Care Across Diverse Contexts
Course Description:
Learners will be able to:  
1.	Apply the knowledge and principles of integrated care in various outpatient care settings.
2.	Implement effective multidisciplinary collaboration strategies within an integrated care framework to ensure continuous patient care within various inpatient settings.
3.	Implement holistic care management strategies for community and home-based patient care using an integrated care approach.
4.	Demonstrate the ability in utilizing technology to deliver integrated care including remote patient monitoring.

Course Title: Multisystem Nursing Practice II
Course Description:
Learners will be able to:  
Knowledge
1. Apply knowledge of the pathophysiology, pharmacology and principles of management of common multisystem (neurological, gastrointestinal and renal) disorders to the care of patients.

2. Relate knowledge of the principles of health assessment and clinical reasoning skills in the conduct of focused health assessment on patients with multisystem (neurological, gastrointestinal and renal) disorders.

3.  Apply knowledge of the various treatment modalities related to multisystem (neurological, gastrointestinal and renal) disorders to the care of patients.

4.  Apply knowledge of the complications and management related to multisystem (neurological, gastrointestinal, and renal) disorders to the care of patients.

Skill
5.  Respond to patients who are exhibiting early signs and symptoms of complications related to multisystem (neurological, gastrointestinal, and renal) disorders to the care of patients.

6.  Perform the essential skills and management related to multisystem (neurological, gastrointestinal and renal) nursing. 

Attitude
7. Adopt patient safety principles to the care of patients with multisystem (neurological, gastrointestinal and renal) disorders.

Course Title: Post-Market Activities for Pharmaceuticals (Classroom & Asynchronous e-learning)
Course Description: This course will allow participants to understand the damaging implications of poor quality medicines to public health, as well as the evolving challenges in minimising the entry of these substandard medicines and falsified products. Participants will learn the regulatory controls along the entire distribution and supply chain of medicines, and the good practices and standards expected. Through practical exercise, participants can gain practical insights on the assessment of quality defects and detection of falsified medicines.

Course Title: Post-Market for Medical Technologies (Classroom & Asynchronous)
Course Description: This course aims to equip students with the knowledge of the post-market regulatory requirements of medical devices, and the activities involved in managing adverse events and conducting Field safety corrective action. Through this course they will learn about the construction and the benefit-risk assessment of a Corrective Action Preventive Action (CAPA), and its effectiveness in addressing safety concerns from the use of medical devices. Students should also expect a better understanding of the key regulatory considerations in product changes from safety issues, and articulate guidance documents related to post-market vigilance.

Course Title: Regulation of Digital Health Products (Classroom & Asynchronous)
Course Description: Through this course, students will learn to describe and differentiate the relevant standards and guidance required in Digital Health products verification and validation. Students will also be acquainted with the regulatory activities through medical device software development, testing and documentation, and will be able to describe key regulatory considerations in product life cycle including change management and post-market activities.

Course Title: Assessment of Employee Well-being and Implementation of Behavioural Intervention Programmes
Course Description:
At the end of the course, participants will be able to:
1. Describe the use of health screening and other related tools in the assessment of employee well-being.
2. Employ Total Workplace Safety and Health (WSH) principles to implement behavioural intervention programmes.

Course Title: Assessment and Implementation of Occupational Health Programmes
Course Description:
At the end of the course, participants will be able to:
1. Describe the practice of occupational health in the workplace, in relation to Total Workplace Safety and Health (WSH) .
2. Employ Total WSH principles to implement occupational health programmes.

Course Title: Core Module (Healthcare Ethics Law and Professionalism) (Classroom and Asynchronous e-Learning)
Course Description: This one day course is to train healthcare professionals to identify and address common ethical and moral issues in the clinical setting.

Course Title: Core Module (Healthcare Ethics Law and Professionalism) (Asynchronous and Synchronous e-learning)
Course Description: This one day course is to train healthcare professionals to identify and address common ethical and moral issues in the clinical setting This course is designed to be interactive - consisting of short didactic sessions and online learning The online learning will provide you the foundation of ethical core values while the didactic sessions will hone your understanding of these values through group discussions, analysis of cases and application of ethical values and principles You will have an opportunity to share your experiences and exchange ideas with other learners on the application of ethical knowledge to existing work practices

Course Title: Advanced Skills and Techniques for Diagnostic Radiology
Course Description: This course aims to equip learners with the necessary skills to interpret and report radiological findings from multiple imaging modalities (e.g. X-ray, ultrasound, fluoroscopy, Computed Tomography, Magnetic Resonance Imaging, nuclear medicine, angiography etc). Learners are also trained on the techniques of verbal discussion of radiological findings. Skills acquired from the course help the learners to: i) improve clinical competency in daily work; ii) apply in the Master of Medicine (Diagnostic Radiology) / Final Fellowship of the Royal College of Radiologists (FRCR) (Part B) Examination; iii) improve their communication skills in multi-disciplinary discussions and meetings.

Course Title: Community Optometrist’s Guide to Glaucoma: From Detection to Co-monitoring
Course Description:
1. To equip participants with skills and knowledge on good practices and standard for glaucoma detection and referral. 
2. To allow participants to gain further insights on co-managing stable glaucoma patients with the tertiary public health institution.

Course Title: Community Optometrist’s Guide to Navigate Common Retinal Disease: From Detection to Monitoring
Course Description:
1. To equip participants with skills and knowledge on detection, recognition of disease stages and time and urgency of referral of some common retinal diseases.  
2. To allow participants to gain further insights on co-management of retinal diseases with tertiary health institutions, in particular diabetic retinopathy (DR) and age related macular degeneration (AMD)

Course Title: Community Optometrists' Guide in Cataract Detection and Pre-and-Post Surgery Care
Course Description:
1. To equip participants with skills and knowledge on good practices and standard for cataract detection and referral. 
2. To allow participants to gain further insights on patient’s journey on cataract management – surgical consideration and post-surgical care.

Course Title: Containment Principles in Planning and Design, Construction, Maintenance and Certification of Containment Facilities
Course Description:
This course aims to equip learners with the following insights:
• Assist with facility design and construction in accordance with applicable standards, guidelines and legislations as well as biosafety and biosecurity principles and practices.
• Conduct pre-commissioning inspections to assess readiness of facilities to meet requirements for commissioning
• Plan and develop management procedures related to facilities, personnel, laboratory processes as well as health and safety to maintain safe working environments.
• Assist in designing operations and maintenance programmes to ensure optimal facility performance and integrity of containment.
• Implement appropriate recommendations to address non-conformities detected during pre-commissioning inspection.

Course Title: Assessment and Evaluation
Course Description:
This module prepares participants to apply principles of assessment and select appropriate assessment methods to assess learners’ knowledge and skills In addition, this module focuses on the process of programme evaluation

Learners are expected to display competency in the following after course completion:
1 Distinguish the difference between assessment and evaluation
2 Apply the principles of assessment and rules of
evidence in designing assessment strategies 
3 Select appropriate assessment methods that align with learning outcomes of the curriculum and
4 Evaluate a program utilizing model to meet the professional and regulatory standards

Course Title: Curriculum Design (Synchronous & Asynchronous)
Clinical Decision-Making: Ethical and Legal Aspects (Classroom & Asynchronous)
Course Description:
Learners should be able to be able to:
- Identify ethical issues and ethical values pertaining to medical decision-making
- Understand the core legal principles pertaining to medical decision-making
- Apply ethical analytical skills and weigh up competing values to support recommendations when reviewing a case
- Engage in critical reflection on issues pertaining to medical decision-making

Course Title: Applied Anatomy and Histology for Dental Implant Surgery
Course Description:
By end of the course, learners will be able to apply the following knowledge:
- Understand the relevance of gross anatomy of the orofacial region, including pertinent muscles, blood supply, foramina, and nerve innervations that may be encountered during implant procedures
- Understand the relevance of histology of the histology of oral and nasal mucosa, the periodontium, bone, and nerves, and their interactions with implant or bone augmentation materials
- Understand the relevance of dental occlusion, oral parafunction, sleep apnea, and cerebral hyperactivity that contribute to bruxism – the effect on abnormal forces on implants, and implant failures
- Understand the importance of facial spaces and routes of spread of oral infection, the pain pathway leading to neuropathic or post-operative pain, etc in the management of possible implant complications

Course Title: Contemporary Science of Infection Control & Prevention (Classroom, Synchronous and Asynchronous e-Learning)
Course Description: This course is designed to provide learners with an in-depth understanding of the contemporary scientific principles that underpin infection control and prevention practices. Participants will explore the latest advancements in the field, incorporating evidence-based approaches to effectively manage and mitigate the risks of healthcare associated infections. The course emphasizes the integration of theoretical knowledge with practical applications to enhance the participants' ability to contribute effectively to the management and prevention of healthcare associated infectious diseases in acute, intermediate and community settings.

Course Title: Applied Statistics for Precision Medicine
Course Description:
Learners should able to: 
1) Explain principles of statistical modelling of data for precision medicine 
2) Explain the challenges of analyzing high throughput datasets for precision medicine 
3) Design high throughput experiments in precision medicine to minimize confounders and increase statistical power to detect differences 
4) Evaluate data quality (batch effects, missing data, noise) and perform preprocessing where appropriate (batch correction, imputation, normalization) 
5) Analyze high throughput data to find and evaluate differences in samples using appropriate statistical tools

Course Title: Community Governance and Management
Course Description:
Learners will be able to:  
1. Understand the principles and concepts of clinical governance, quality programs, and risk management to improve practice and clinical outcomes.
2. Understand proactive risk management principles to quality and patient safety.
3. Acknowledgement of individual roles and responsibilities can improve clinical and service outcomes.
4. Contribute to community development of governance framework that drive high reliability and safe care.
5. Drive professional development that focus on empowerment, enablement and collaboration to gain sustainable improvement.

Course Title: Clinical Studies and Evaluation of Health Products (Classroom & Asynchronous e-learning)
Course Description: This course will provide an overview of the design and phases of human clinical trials, as well as the regulatory requirements to support market approvals of pharmaceuticals, medical devices and technologies. Participants will also learn about the standards and processes for submitting an application and evaluating the benefit-risk profile of a health product using a structured framework. Upon completion of this course, students would appreciate the contribution of these regulatory controls on the product life cycle and the role in ensuring quality, safety and efficacy of the products.

Course Title: Behavioural and Implementation Science for Health and Healthcare Services
Course Description:
Participants will be equipped with the knowledge  to 
i) identify the specific behaviour they want to change and the outcome of this change 
ii) build an implementation team 
iii) identify potential intervention strategies (i.e., design a feasible solution for the problem)  
iv) Identify barriers and facilitators to implementation of intervention strategies 
v) develop an implementation plan addressing barriers and promoting facilitators 
vi) manage the Implemention,  evaluation and adaptation of an intervention and implementation plan.

Course Title: Cadaver Dissection Workshop for Plastic Surgery Residents
Course Description: Objectives include enabling the participants to reinforce and strengthen their anatomical knowledge, recognize the key steps and crticial moments in harvest of flaps, improve general surgical technique and skill in flap raising/fracture reduction/fixation and to understand the indications and implications of performing a particular procedure.

Course Title: Selection, Installation and Safe Use of Biosafety Cabinet, Autoclave and Other Common Laboratory and Safety Equipment
Course Description: This course aims to equip learners with the knowledge of selection, installation, safe use and maintenance of various critical laboratory equipment.

Course Title: Early Childhood Development (Classroom & Synchronous)
Course Description:
Knowledge and Insight (Understanding)
• Describe the Touchpoints approach

Application of Knowledge & Insight
• Apply the Touchpoints developmental and relational frameworks during their interactions with families and children
• Apply the Touchpoints approach to acquire knowledge of the social-emotional development of young children
Apply the Touchpoints approach to build up parental competencies

Appraisal (formation of a judgement)
• Practice the Touchpoints principles and assumptions through reflective practice
• Practice the Touchpoints approach to foster stronger parent-child bonds
• Practice the Touchpoints approach to strengthen therapeutic alliances with families

Communication Skills
• Use the principle ‘The behaviour of the child as your language’ as the cornerstone of the Touchpoints approach

Course Title: Early Childhood Development - The Touchpoints Approach
Course Description:
Brazelton Touchpoints is a practical approach for building strong family-child relationships from birth through the earliest years, laying the foundation for children's early learning and healthy development Research and experience have shown that simply 'telling' parents what to do is not effective Touchpoints is a model of practice that enables providers (including caregivers and educators) to establish collaborative and encouraging relationshps with families based on a shared interest in the healthy development of their child Touchpoints provides for transformation through:                                   
1) changing practice from prescription to collaboration   
2) building capacity within communities through engagement and promoting strengths within families      
After completion of this training, participants will be able to:
• Understand and describe the Touchpoints Approach
• Apply the Touchpoints Approach in the Early Childhood Care & Education (ECCE) setting to understand the themes of 'gatekeeping' between the provider and the family
• Understand and apply the Touchpoints Principles and Assumptions through Reflective Practice
• Understand and apply the Touchpoints Developmental and Relational Frameworks
• Use ‘The Behaviour of the Child as Your Language’ as the cornerstone of the Touchpoints Approach

Course Title: Basic Health Literacy Workshop (Synchronous e-learning)
Course Description:
The workshop aims to educate/equip participants with knowledge and skills in the following healthcare topics:
1) Nutrition 
2) Exercise
3) Medications
4)Health Screening and vaccinations
5) Financial literacy for Healthcare Needs 
6) Traditional Chinese Medicine (TCM) / Complementary and Alternative Medicine
7) Mindfulness
The participants should be able be well versed and be able to effectively communicate the following:
- Healthy eating for adults and older persons
- Physical and mental benefits of exercise  
- Exercise dos and don’ts for older adults and those with chronic diseases
- Common medications and how they work
- Self-care for common chronic conditions
- Rationale for health screening and vaccinations
- Financial schemes available for management of chronic conditions, hospitalisation & other medical needs
- how TCM works and the do's and don's of consuming TCM
- mindfulness practices for greater resilience, focus, and healthy ageing

Course Title: Cadaveric Dissection Workshop for Cardiothoracic Surgery Residents (Classroom & Synchronous)
Course Description:
This cadaveric workshop will allow participants to practice and enhance their skills in a safe and guided environment.  This workshop offers an excellent opportunity for cardiothoracic surgery residents and trainees to expand their knowledge and skills in cardiothoracic surgery & other surgical specialties.  Surgical dissection using fresh frozen cadavers allows teaching of human anatomy and intensive practical training in a wide variety of surgical techniques.
Through this course, we hope that participants can learn:
- Basic Anatomy principles
- Develop and improve knowledge and skills in dissection
- Bridge basic anatomical understanding with clinical applications in the field of cardiothoracic surgery
- Build a strong foundation for safe surgical practice.

Course Title: Cadaver Dissection Workshop for Surgery Residents
Course Description:
This cadaveric workshop will allow participants to practice and enhance their skills in a safe and guided environment.  This workshop offers an excellent opportunity for surgery residents and trainees to expand their knowledge and skills in general surgery and other surgical specialties.  Surgical dissection using fresh frozen cadavers allows teaching of human anatomy and intensive practical training in a wide variety of surgical techniques.
Through this course, we hope that participants can learn:
- Basic Anatomy principles
- Develop and improve knowledge and skills in dissection
- Bridge basic anatomical understanding with clinical applications in the field of general surgery
- Build a strong foundation for safe surgical practice.

Course Title: Integrative Workplace Learning
Course Description:
Integrative Workplace Learning bridges theory and practice by enabling learners to apply academic knowledge in real-world settings. The course provides learners with insights into integrating various learning models for workplace development, designing tailored professional development plans, and evaluating the impact of these integrative approaches. Ultimately, it equips learners to navigate dynamic work environments and pursue continuous professional growth.
Upon completion of the course, the learners will be able to:
1.	Integrate multiple learning models for workplace development
2.	Design a customized professional development plan and
3.	Evaluate the impact of integrative learning approaches

Course Title: Basic Outbreak Rapid Response
Course Description:
Upon completion of this course, participants will be able to:
1. Apply field epidemiologic methods to undertake rapid response on the ground
2. Assist in surveillance and outbreak investigation using a systematic approach; and
3. Acquire skills to effect preventive and preparedness actions.

Course Title: Executive Certificate of Public Mental Health
Course Description:
The EC PMH programme aims to provide higher education for the growing and evolving post-graduate learning needs of mental health professionals and practitioners in Singapore and the region. The programme is designed to provide an opportunity for professionals in the mental health industry who want to expand their knowledge of programme development, monitoring, research and evaluation. In addition, this programme is also ideal for students who are keen to embark on a career in mental health service provision and clinical management, or mid-career professionals seeking to make a career switch to the healthcare industry and to gain clinically relevant knowledge and skills in the area of mental health.

The EC PMH equips professionals with the knowledge and skills to address mental health needs in diverse settings, making them valuable contributors in a range of industries. Job roles can vary widely, from direct clinical care to program evaluation, research, policy development, and education. The demand for these professionals is driven by the increasing recognition of the importance of mental health and the need for evidence-based practice in various sectors.

Course Title: Executive Certificate of Causal Inference in Precision Medicine
Course Description:
At the end of this course, the student will be able to

 - Understand causal inference and associated techniques.
 - Apply econometric techniques to make causal inferences.
 - Implement basic multi-armed bandit algorithms
 - Implement common predictive algorithms
 - Understand the basics of machine learning

Course Title: Executive Certificate of Clinical Perspective In Precision Medicine
Course Description:
By the end of the course, the student will:

 - Know how prevalent genetic disorders are in the population
 - Know how patients present to doctors (symptomatic, pre-symptomatic, asymptomatic)
 - Know how doctors diagnose patients (history, family history, physical examination, clinical criteria, tests)
 - Know how clinical genetic and genomic tests are employed in clinical practice
 - Know how clinical genetic and genomic test reports look like and how to interpret them
 - Know how genetic counselling is applied in clinical practice
 - Know how genetic conditions are treated
 - Know the promises and potential pitfalls in clinical genetics and genomics

Course Title: Executive Certificate of Precision Biomarkers
Course Description:
By the end of the course, the student will:

 - Know What are the characteristics of an ideal biomarker
 - Know What the various classifications of biomarkers
 - Understand the workflow from biomarker discovery to development
 - Discuss challenges in biomarker development
 - Discuss at least ONE clinical application of omics-based biomarkers including its advantages and limitations

Course Title: Executive Certificate of Precision Diagnostics
Course Description:
By the end of the course, the student will:

 - Know How classical cytogenetics and DNA diagnostics are performed.
 - Know How targeted testing is performed for common adult and pediatric monogenic disorders.
 - Know How to identify novel genes involved in genetic disorders of unknown etiology.
 - Know How prenatal and pre-implantation genetic screening and testing are performed.
 - Know How common cancers are diagnosed molecularly.
 - Know How common infectious diseases are diagnosed molecularly.

Course Title: Executive Certificate of Precision Drug Discovery and Development
Course Description:
At the end of this course, the student will

 - gain a better understanding and latest information on precision drug discovery and development.
 - grasp novel paradigms and advanced knowledge on drug discovery using data sets and AI, development, and evaluation.
 - understand drug discovery from target identification to clinical trials of precision medicine currently used.
 - be informed on the latest development of biomarker screening pipeline for patient stratification, treatment selection and management of adverse effects.

Course Title: Executive Certificate of Precision Health and Medicine for Cardiometabolic Diseases
Course Description:
At the end of this course, the student will be able to

1. Know major forms of heart diseases: genetic vs. acquired.

2. Know major forms of metabolic disorders: genetic vs. acquired.

3. Tools and methods in genetic, molecular and metabolic diagnosis of cardiometabolic diseases;

4. Imaging modalities for disease diagnosis 

5. Interaction between metabolic and cardiovascular diseases

Course Title: Executive Certificate of Precision Oncology
Course Description:
By the end of the module, the student will:

 - Know what methods are employed in Precision Oncology
 - Learn how Precision Oncology is applied to Lung Cancer
 - Learn how germline genetic testing in Precision Oncology is performed.
 - Learn how DNA damage response (DDR) can be employed as a therapeutic target for cancer.
 - Learn how the immune system is targeted in Precision Oncology

Course Title: Executive Certificate of Precision Therapeutics
Course Description:
By the end of the course, the student will be able:

 - To understand current clinical applications of N-of-1 Medicine.
 - To understand modern approaches of vaccine development.
 - To understand advances in immunotherapy for precision oncology.
 - To understand stem cell-based therapies for precision medicine.
 - To understand current developments in gene therapy.

Course Title: Executive Certificate of Technological Innovation in Precision Medicine
Course Description:
Overall learning outcomes include appreciation and understanding of key innovative technologies in precision medicine with real-world examples.

Through the project work, students will have an opportunity to perform in-depth research and present their findings on a key innovation making precision medicine a reality.

Course Title: Assessment and Implementation of Safety Programmes
Course Description:
At the end of the workshop, the participants will be able to:
1. Describe the legislated and non-legislated components and principles of the safety framework, in relation to Total Workplace Safety and Health (WSH) .
2. Employ Total WSH principles to implement safety programmes.

Course Title: Essential Topics in Clinical Ethics : Elements of Decision-making (Classroom and Asynchronous e-Learning)
Course Description:
Master and Elevate Ethical Standards in Healthcare

The Essential Topics in Clinical Ethics (ETCE) course is an intermediate-level short course intended primarily for those involved in clinical ethics committee (CEC) work as well as healthcare practitioners in general.
This course consists of two parts :
Part 1 : Autonomy, Informed Consent, Capacity and Best Interests
Part 2 : Shared Decision-making and Decision-making at End-of-Life

Course Title: Essential Topics in Clinical Ethics : Elements of Decision-making (Synchronous and Asynchronous e-learning)
Course Description:
Master and Elevate Ethical Standards in Healthcare

The Essential Topics in Clinical Ethics (ETCE) course is an intermediate-level short course intended primarily for those involved in clinical ethics committee (CEC) work as well as healthcare practitioners in general.
This course consists of two parts :
Part 1 : Autonomy, Informed Consent, Capacity and Best Interests
Part 2 : Shared Decision-making and Decision-making at End-of-Life

Course Description:

This course consists of two parts :
Part 1 : Ethical Reasoning, Ethical Guidance and Relationship with Healthcare Professionals
Part 2 : Ethics and Law in Defensive Medicine and Therapeutic Innovations

Course Title: Behavioural and Implementation Science for Health and Healthcare Services (Synchronous e-learning)
Course Description: Participants will be equipped with the knowledge to i) identify the specific behaviour they want to change and the outcome of this change ii) build an implementation team iii) identify potential intervention strategies (i.e., design a feasible solution for the problem)  iv) Identify barriers and facilitators to implementation of intervention strategies v) develop an implementation plan addressing barriers and promoting facilitators (vi) manage the implementation,  evaluation and adaptation of an intervention and implementation plan.

Course Title: Cadaver Dissection Workshop for Obstetrics & Gynaecology (O&G) Residents
Course Description:
This cadaveric workshop will allow participants to practice and enhance their skills in a safe and guided environment  This workshop offers an excellent opportunity for Obstetrics & Gynaecology (O&G) residents and trainees to expand their knowledge and skills in O&G  Surgical dissection using fresh frozen cadavers allows teaching of human anatomy and intensive practical training in a wide variety of surgical techniques
Through this course, we hope that participants can learn:
- Basic Anatomy principles
- Develop and improve knowledge and skills in dissection
- Bridge basic anatomical understanding with clinical applications in the field of obstetrics & gynaecology (O&G)
- Build a strong foundation for safe surgical practice

Course Title: Head & Neck Dissection Workshop for Otolaryngology (ENT) Residents
Course Description:
This cadaveric workshop will allow participants to practice and enhance their skills in a safe and guided environment  This workshop offers an excellent opportunity for otolaryngology (ENT) residents and trainees to expand their knowledge and skills in ENT & other surgical specialties  Surgical dissection using fresh frozen cadavers allows teaching of human anatomy and intensive practical training in a wide variety of surgical techniques
Through this course, we hope that participants can learn:
- Basic Anatomy principles
- Develop and improve knowledge and skills in dissection
- Bridge basic anatomical understanding with clinical applications in the field of ENT surgery
- Build a strong foundation for safe surgical practice

Course Title: How to make the most out of EPIC
Course Description:
At the end of the course, the learner should be able to: 

1. Utilise several QuickWin features within EPIC
a)	Perform a rapid Chart Search
b)	Set default home workspace
c)	Optimize main toolbar for personal usage
d)	Enter visit diagnosis efficiently
e)	Know and be aware of keyboard shortcuts which can speed up work

2. Utilise SmartPhrases and Smartlinks individually and at department level
a)	Create and edit SmartPhrases, including the use of SmartLinks
b)	Share these SmartPhrases with colleagues

3. Utilise SmartLists 
a)	Create and edit SmartLists for personal productivity
b)	Share personal SmartLists with colleagues

4.Set up letters as a means of communication within EPIC 
a)	Set up individual letter templates for productivity and clearer communication

5. Customise Preference List 
a)	Customise personal preference lists for efficient ordering

6. Set up Smartsets/Order Sets 
a)	Customize and personalise Smartsets / Order Sets, which will help clinicians work through a patient encounter efficiently

7. Set up Personal Dashboard
a) How to configure and customise personal dashboards within EPIC (under 
consideration and development)

8. Appreciate healthcare more from a patient’s perspective – crucial Interprofessional elements
a) Using patient examples to bring across what would be required from each discipline (doctors, nurses, allied health) at each particular point

Course Title: Certificate in Adolescent Health (for Healthcare PMET) (Classroom and Asynchronous e-learning)
Course Description:
The objectives of the course are to:
1.	Better manage a adolescent depending on the following health issues based on theoretical and clinical knowledge. 
a.	Dental issues of children (10 to 21 years old)
b.	Health growth & normal puberty
c.	Variations of norms versus pathological states. (E.g.: Scoliosis)
d.	Vaccination issues

2.	Educate learners about basic mental health relevant to adolescents (10 to 21 years old).

Course Title: Certificate in Child Health (for Healthcare PMET) (Classroom and Asynchronous e-learning)
Course Description:
The objectives of the course are to:
1. Better manage a child depending on the following health issues based on theoretical and clinical knowledge. 
a.	Dental issues of children (0 to 10 years old)
b.	Growth issues
c.	Vaccination issues

2.	Educate learners about basic mental health relevant to children (0 to 10 years old).

Course Title: Fundamentals of Systems Thinking in Healthcare
Course Description:
This course will enable participants to apply systems thinking to define and understand health systems and healthcare services design and delivery, analyse causes and constraints of current gaps and limitations, and identify high-impact improvement opportunities and solutions. Specific learning objectives include:

- Seeing Systems: Learn Systems Thinking (ST) as a new language to individually and/or collectively reflect, appreciate and discuss about our own health systems and services.
- Understanding Systems: Harness commonly used frameworks and models to understand the patterns and behaviours of health systems and services.
- Working with Systems: Embody and prescribe more effective actions and interventions in your health systems and services that optimise outcomes while minimising unexpected and undesirable side effects or consequences of your actions.

Course Title: Principles of Child and Family Health (Classroom & Asynchronous)
Course Description:
Learners will be able to:  
1 describe determinants of health and life course perspective in influencing population health  
2 identify facilitators and barriers in effective communication across multiple situations such as cultural diversity and children of different age groups  
3 understand principles of Child and Family Health (CFH)  and the factors influencing primary care for CFH  
4 demonstrate effective communication skills with parents and multidisciplinary team to provide holistic care and support to children and their families  
5 advocate reflection in promoting effective interpersonal relationships and therapeutic communication and
6 adopt culturally appropriate care for child and family

Course Title: Principles of Child and Family Health (Synchronous & Asynchronous)
Course Description:
Course Objectives
Learners will be able to: 
1. describe determinants of health and life course perspective in influencing population health; 
2. identify facilitators and barriers in effective communication across multiple situations such as cultural diversity and children of different age groups; 
3. understand principles of CFH and the factors influencing primary care for CFH. 
4. demonstrate effective communication skills with parents and multidisciplinary team to provide holistic care and support to children and their families. 
5. advocate reflection in promoting effective interpersonal relationships and therapeutic communication; and
6. adopt culturally appropriate care for child and family.

Course Title: Child Health (0-6 years old) (Classroom & Asynchronous)
Course Description:
Learners will be able to:  

1	analyse and apply the principles of primary health care to promote and maintain overall wellbeing of children in the community settings 

2	understand importance of engaging and enabling parents to promote physical and mental health as well as safety of the children and 

3	recognize common health and safety issues experienced by children aged 0-6-year-old  

4	demonstrate evidence-based and policy informed care  

for children aged 0-6 years’ old 

5	perform growth and developmental screening and initiate appropriate referrals for children with developmental delays  

6	advocate parental involvement to promote well-being and ensure safety of children aged 0-6 years old and

7adopt culturally appropriate care for child health

Course Title: Educators’ Course (Healthcare Ethics Law and Professionalism) (Classroom & Asynchronous e-learning)
Course Description: The course is designed to be interactive - consisting of short didactic sessions and online learning. The online learning will provide you with the foundation of ethical core values while the didactic teaching will enable you to hone your understanding of these values through group discussions, scenario analyses, and application of ethical values and principles. You will have an opportunity to share your experiences and exchange ideas with other learners on the application of ethical knowledge in the existing work practice and teaching.

Course Title: Bachelor of Science (Nursing Practice)
Course Description: Please use the link to claim your SkillsFuture Credit if you are 40 years old and above. The BSc (Nursing Practice) aims to allow learners who are practising Registered Nurses in the workforce to be able to connect the learning with their work roles, develop their capabilities and improve patient outcomes.

Course Title: Advanced Health Econometrics (Synchronous e-Learning)
Course Description:
This advanced course will further develop students’ understanding of data features unique to health economics and appropriate model specifications that accommodate
them, which is critical for conducting applied econometric analyses in the health field. Students will become proficient in the building of appropriate econometrics models and estimating them with real-world data using Stata enabling them to choose model specifications for given data and research questions of interest.

Course Title: Implementation Science for Health Services
Course Description: Through this course, participants will learn to design and implement effective interventions to translate evidence into practice via identifying community, patient, clinician and organizational factors that serve as barriers and facilitators to translating findings into clinical practice, policy and public health. Participants will design evaluations of interventions that translate evidence into practice. This will be achieved by applying the basics of process and outcome evaluation, and by identifying the appropriate qualitative and quantitative measures of effect.

Course Title: Acute Paediatrics
Course Description:
Knowledge and Insight (Understanding)
• Spectrum of play and sport-related injuries, and red flags of non-accidental injuries
• Fever patterns in children
• Prescription patterns and antibiotic susceptibilities 

Application of Knowledge & Insight
• Recognize urogenital, gastroenterology and hepatobiliary complaints and identify red flags (including vulvovaginitis, haematuria, dysuria, jaundice and per rectal bleeding) 
• Recognize haematological presentations and identify red flags (including pallor and bleeding tendencies)  
• Recognize Ear, Nose & Throat (ENT) issues and make timely specialist referrals where necessary (including foreign body, epistaxis and ear discharge)
• Recognize common orthopedic issues e.g. pes planus, genu valgus, patella instability, pulled elbow and make timely specialist referrals where necessary
Recognize food allergies and initiate appropriate specialist referral.

Appraisal (formation of a judgement)
• Approach to fevers, including Kawasaki disease 
• Approach to lymph node swellings
• Manage common childhood infections (including urinary tract infection and gastroenteritis)
• Manage chronic asthma and asthma exacerbation
• Approach to acute respiratory distress, including croup, bronchiolitis, bronchitis, viral-induced wheeze and pneumonia
• Manage emergencies, stabilize and provide timely specialist referral for conditions including anaphylaxis, shock and seizures.
• Approach to headaches, giddiness and palpitations

Communication Skills
• Counsel on seizure and anaphylaxis first aid
• Communicate referral plans for acute conditions requiring specialist and emergency referrals

Course Title: Understanding Commonly Used Healthcare Terminologies for Caregiver
Course Description: The course aims to familiarize caregivers with commonly used healthcare terminologies to improve communication with healthcare professionals and improve quality of care.

Course Title: Understanding Physical Changes of Aging for Caregiver
Course Description: The course aims to educate caregivers on the physical changes associated with aging hence, enhancing better care and support to elderly individuals.

Course Title: Evidence-Based Practice - Part of Post Diploma Certificate in Applied Sciences in Advanced Nursing (ADPCN)
Course Description: This module aims to provide the students with the knowledge and application underpinnings the evidence-based practice. Based on the 5 steps of evidence-based practice, the students will learn to convert information into an answerable clinical question (ask), track down best evidence for answering the question (acquire), critically appraise evidence (appraise), integrate evidence into clinical decision making (application), and evaluate and seek ways to improve the next time (audit).

Course Title: Health Assessment and Clinical Decision Making - Part of Post Diploma Certificate in Applied Sciences in Advanced Nursing (ADPCN)
Course Description: This module aims to provide the students with comprehensive foundation and principles of health assessment in clinical nursing practice. It will also provide comprehensive skills necessary to obtain data through complete health history taking and physical examination. Emphasis will be placed on the holistic and comprehensive approach of assessment. Variations of normality in the older persons will be taken into considerations in the assessment findings. Together with the relevant laboratory data, this module also equips the students with necessary knowledge underpinning the skills to make informed clinical decisions.

Course Title: Principles and Practice of Palliative Care - Part of Post Diploma Certificate in Advanced Palliative Care Nursing (ADPCN)
Course Description: This module introduces the principles of palliative care, encompasses the knowledge of palliative care services in Singapore and the seamless transition of care across settings through understanding the multi-disciplinary roles.  It also aims to ensure the students are able to demonstrate comprehensive history taking and systematic physical assessment of palliative care patients.

Course Title: Serious Illness Management - Part of Post Diploma Certificate in Advanced Palliative Care Nursing (ADPCN)
Course Description: This module covers the current understanding of palliative care in common cancers and end stage organ diseases. It includes cancer biology, the diagnosis of common cancers, cancer screening, pathophysiology of non-cancer progressive diseases, general principles, and modalities of treatment for both cancers and non-cancer advanced diseases; focusing on nursing management of patients' unique response and needs.

Course Title: Evidence-Based Practice - Part of Post-Diploma Certificate in Applied Sciences in Advanced Nursing (ADCR2)
Course Description: This module aims to provide the students with the knowledge and application underpinnings the evidence-based practice. Based on the 5 steps of evidence-based practice, the students will learn to convert information into an answerable clinical question (ask), track down best evidence for answering the question (acquire), critically appraise evidence (appraise), integrate evidence into clinical decision making (application), and evaluate and seek ways to improve the next time (audit).

Course Title: Advanced Perioperative Nursing Science II - Part of Post-Diploma Certificate in Advanced Perioperative Nursing (ADPO2)
Course Description: This module provides students with an overview of the perioperative nursing consideration in specialised surgical procedures. Perioperative nursing is a team concept and includes receiving the surgical patient to the operating room, planning for the position for surgery, skin preparation, choice of instrumentation and anticipating the sequence and needs of surgery.

Course Title: Advanced Perioperative Nursing Science I - Part of Post-Diploma Certificate in Advanced Perioperative Nursing (ADPO2)
Course Description: This module provides students with an overview of the perioperative nursing consideration in various surgical procedures. Perioperative nursing is a team concept and includes receiving the surgical patient to the operating room, planning for the position for surgery, skin preparation, choice of instrumentation and anticipating the sequence and needs of surgery.

Course Title: Counselling Skills 101 for Caregiver
Course Description: The course aims to equip caregivers with essential counselling skills to effectively communicate and provide emotional support to their loved ones.

Course Title: Applied Pathophysiology and Pharmacology - Part of Post Diploma Certificate in Applied Sciences in Advanced Nursing (ADPCN)
Course Description: This module aims to provide students with knowledge and understanding of pathophysiologic and pharmacologic considerations. This module will enable the students to gain a better understanding of underlying disease processes and relate the basic principles of pharmacology and the nursing process to selected drugs and their therapeutic use.

Course Title: Communication and Psychosocial Skills - Part of Post Diploma Certificate in Advanced Palliative Care Nursing (ADPCN)
Course Description: This module examines the psychosocial perspectives in palliative care; focusing on commonly encountered psychosocial needs, including caregiver and bereavement support. Students will explore the challenges of communication with patients of advanced diseases, and the role of nursing in accordance to their preferred care plans.

Course Title: Applied Pathophysiology and Pharmacology - Part of Post-Diploma Certificate in Applied Sciences in Advanced Nursing (ADCR2)
Course Description: This module aims to provide students with knowledge and understanding of pathophysiologic and pharmacologic considerations. This module will enable the students to gain a better understanding of underlying disease processes and relate the basic principles of pharmacology and the nursing process to selected drugs and their therapeutic use.

Course Title: Applied Pathophysiology and Pharmacology - Part of Post-Diploma Certificate in Applied Sciences in Advanced Nursing (ADPO2)
Course Description: This module aims to provide students with knowledge and understanding of pathophysiologic and pharmacologic considerations. This module will enable the students to gain a better understanding of underlying disease processes and relate the basic principles of pharmacology and the nursing process to selected drugs and their therapeutic use.

Course Title: Certified Mental Wellness Coach & Facilitator
Course Description:
At the end of the course, you will be able to:

1. Understand the scientific bases of work stress, positive psychology and mental well-being, with an emphasis on evidence-based models and concepts
2. Understand a coachee’s mindset, thinking patterns, underlying motives, perceived stressors, and their related behaviours at work and in life
3. Use assessment and measurement tools to profile a coachee’s traits and tendencies at work and in life
4. Apply a positive psychology coaching process with relevant exercises and interventions to enhance coachee well-being through resilience coping strategies and mindsets
5. Apply facilitation strategies and intervention tools for small group sessions (e.g., interactive workshop)
6. Understand the ethical issues in profiling, coaching and group facilitation

Course Title: Managing Oral Medications at Home Setting for Caregiver
Course Description: The course aims to equip caregivers with knowledge and skills necessary to manage and give oral medications safely at home.

Course Title: Safety and Fall Prevention for the Elderly at Home
Course Description: The course aims to provide caregivers with the knowledge and skills to ensure safety of the elderly individuals and prevent falls at home.

Course Title: Infection Prevention and Control for Home Care
Course Description: The course aims to educate caregivers on best practices in the infection prevention and control in a home care setting.

Course Title: Advanced Critical Care Nursing Practice - Part of Post-Diploma Certificate in Advanced Critical Care Nursing (ADCR2)
Course Description: This module aims to provide students with an understanding and comprehensive foundation on the principles of critical care nursing practice. It will also provide comprehensive skills necessary to obtain data through health history, physical examination together with identifying relevant laboratory data, monitoring adjuncts and advanced nursing skills that are performed commonly in the acute care setting. This module serves to equip critical care practitioners with the necessary skills for critical thinking to make informed clinical decisions in the management of critically ill patients.

Course Title: Advanced Critical Care Nursing Science II - Part of Post-Diploma Certificate in Advanced Critical Care Nursing (ADCR2)
Course Description: This module aims to provide students with an understanding and comprehensive foundation on the principles of critical care nursing practice and a continuity of basic nursing science from general nursing training. Gastro- intestinal, hepato-biliary, endocrine and nephrology diseases and altered conditions of these major body systems are covered in depth with integration of pathophysiology and applied human anatomy. Interpretation of relevant laboratory and diagnostic studies results are included and advanced nursing skills that are performed commonly in the acute care setting, this module serves to equip critical care practitioners with the necessary knowledge of diseases and skills for critical thinking to make informed clinical decisions.

Course Title: Advanced Critical Care Nursing Science I - Part of Post-Diploma Certificate in Advanced Critical Care Nursing (ADCR2)
Course Description: This module aims to provide students with an understanding and comprehensive foundation on the principles of critical care nursing practice and a continuity of basic nursing science from general nursing training. Coronary, cardiovascular, respiratory, neurological diseases and altered conditions of these major body systems are covered in depth with integration of pathophysiology and applied human anatomy. Interpretation of relevant laboratory and diagnostic studies results are included and advanced nursing skills that are performed commonly in the acute care setting, this module serves to equip critical care practitioners with the necessary knowledge of diseases and skills for critical thinking to make informed clinical decisions.

Course Title: Fundamentals in Critical Care Nursing - Part of Post-Diploma Certificate in Advanced Critical Care Nursing (ADCR2)
Course Description: This module aims to provide students with an understanding and comprehensive foundation on the fundamentals of critical care nursing. Critical care practices, ethical and legal issues, patient and family education, psychosocial issues, sleep alterations and management, nutrition, pain management, sedation, agitation, delirium assessment and management and end of life issues are covered in depth with integration of pathophysiology and applied human anatomy and relevant laboratory and diagnostic studies results are included and advanced nursing skills that are performed commonly in the acute care setting, this module serves to equip critical care practitioners with the necessary knowledge and skills for critical thinking to make informed clinical decisions in a critical care unit.

Course Title: Fundamentals in Perioperative Nursing II - Part of Post-Diploma Certificate in Advanced Perioperative Nursing (ADPO2)
Course Description: This module will provide the student with an understanding of the nursing implications and skills required for a patient undergoing anaesthesia in the perioperative setting. Students will be able recognize complications and manage emergency situations for the surgical patient. The module will also equip students with understanding of the therapeutic needs of patients experiencing surgical intervention. It includes basic theory and skills for counselling to prepare the student to manage challenging situations in the perioperative setting.

Course Title: Fundamentals in Perioperative Nursing I - Part of Post-Diploma Certificate in Advanced Perioperative Nursing (ADPO2)
Course Description: This module will provide the student with an overview of the natural sciences pertaining to the practice of nursing in the operating theatre and its environment. It explores scientific concepts underpinning the practice and allows the student to have an overall analysis of knowledge and skills required by the operating theatre clinical specialist. It includes the theory and techniques for the preparation of sterile supplies as well as planning for the surgical procedure. The student will also understand ethical and legal issues pertaining to perioperative practice and understand the role of a perioperative nurse as a patient advocate to provide safe and competent care for the surgical patient.

Course Title: Symptom Management in Serious Illness - Part of Post Diploma Certificate in Advanced Palliative Care Nursing (ADPCN)
Course Description: This module covers the pathophysiology, assessment, and management of common symptoms in serious life- limiting illnesses including common emergencies encountered in palliative care. Students will be able to apply medical and nursing knowledge and skills, in caring for patients with serious life-limiting illness.

Course Title: Advanced Clinical Practicum in Palliative Care Nursing 1 - Part of Post Diploma Certificate in Advanced Clinical Practicum in Palliative Care Nursing (ADPCN)
Course Description: This module provides the learners with the opportunities to apply the knowledge and skills they have learnt during a 2-week clinical posting at the palliative healthcare setting. The practitioners at the hospices and palliative care settings will guide the learners.

Course Title: Post Diploma Certificate in Applied Sciences in Advanced Nursing - Part of Advanced Diploma in Palliative Care Nursing (ADPCN)
Course Description: The certificate equips the students with comprehensive knowledge and skills required for better understanding of individual patient journey across setting-based and population-based health care. The modules covered are Health Assessment and Clinical Decision Making, Applied Pathophysiology and Pharmacology, and Evidence-Based Practice. The modules aim to develop the students to be more adapt at managing the complexity of patients related to ageing, common acute and chronic diseases, common psychosocial issues and preventive care.

Course Title: Post-Diploma Certificate in Applied Sciences in Advanced Nursing - Part of Advanced Diploma in Critical Care Nursing (ADCR2)
Course Description: The certificate equips the students with comprehensive knowledge and skills required for better understanding of individual patient journey across setting-based and population-based health care. The modules covered are Health Assessment and Clinical Decision Making, Applied Pathophysiology and Pharmacology, and Evidence- Based Practice. The modules aim to develop the students to be more adapt at managing the complexity of patients related to ageing, common acute and chronic diseases, common psychosocial issues and preventive care.

Course Title: Post-Diploma Certificate in Applied Sciences in Advanced Nursing - Part of Advanced Diploma in Perioperative Nursing (ADPO2)
Course Description: The certificate equips the students with comprehensive knowledge and skills required for better understanding of individual patient journey across setting-based and population-based health care. The modules covered are Health Assessment and Clinical Decision Making, Applied Pathophysiology and Pharmacology, and Evidence- Based Practice. The modules aim to develop the students to be more adapt at managing the complexity of patients related to ageing, common acute and chronic diseases, common psychosocial issues and preventive care.

Course Title: Post Diploma Certificate in Advanced Palliative Care Nursing - Part of Advanced Diploma in Palliative Care Nursing (ADPCN)
Course Description: The certificate equips the learners with advanced knowledge and skills essential for delivering palliative care to individuals facing life-limiting illnesses. Four modules in this certificate are palliative nursing specialty, including Principles and Practice of Palliative Care, Serious Illness Management, Symptom Management in Serious Illness, and Communication and Psychosocial Skills. Learners will learn the management of serious illnesses and their symptoms, and effective communication and psychosocial skills that are fundamentals in palliative care nursing.

Course Title: Post-Diploma Certificate in Advanced Critical Care Nursing - Part of Advanced Diploma in Critical Care Nursing (ADCR2)
Course Description: The certificate equips students with comprehensive knowledge and skills specifically required for the care of critically ill patients. The modules covered include Fundamentals in Critical Care Nursing, Advanced Critical Care Nursing I, Advanced Critical Care Nursing II and Advanced Critical Care Nursing Practice. The students will learn the critical care nursing fundamentals and management of ethical and legal issues, patient and family education, and alterations in psychosocial, sleep, nutrition, comfort and state of consciousness. The students also will learn the management of critically ill patients based on organ system-based alterations, and critical care nursing procedures.

Course Title: Post-Diploma Certificate in Advanced Perioperative Nursing - Part of Advanced Diploma in Perioperative Nursing (ADPO2)
Course Description: The certificate equips students with knowledge and skills required by the operating theatre clinical specialist. The modules encompass the phases of perioperative nursing care including receiving the patient to the operating room, planning for anesthesia, positioning of patient, skin preparation, preparing choice of instrumentation and anticipating the sequence and needs of surgery as well as post-operative care.

Course Title: Advanced Clinical Practicum in Palliative Care Nursing 2 - Part of Post Diploma Certificate in Advanced Clinical Practicum in Palliative Care Nursing (ADPCN)
Course Description: This module provides the learners with the opportunities to further deepen their clinical skills while synthesizing and applying their knowledge and skills at various hospices and in-patient palliative care settings during a 8-week clinical posting. The practitioners at the hospices and palliative care settings will guide the learners.

Course Title: Advanced Clinical Practicum in Critical Care Nursing - Part of Post-Diploma Certificate in Advanced Clinical Practicum in Critical Care Nursing (ADCR2)
Course Description: The module provides the student opportunities to provide direct patient / family care and gain clinical experience in critical care settings, acute and caring of older adults with acute and chronic disorders in acute setting like coronary care, cardiovascular, neurosurgical and medical surgical intensive care units. Nurses will provide care for the critically ill patients and support their families. These clinical practices provide an overall analysis of the practice of critical care nursing.

Course Title: Advanced Clinical Practicum in Perioperative Nursing - Part of Post-Diploma Certificate in Advanced Clinical Practicum in Perioperative Nursing (ADPO2)
Course Description: The module provides the student opportunities to synthesize the knowledge learned from the other eight modules and gain clinical experience in perioperative settings. The clinical practice provides an overall analysis of the practice of perioperative nursing.

Course Title: Post Diploma Certificate in Advanced Clinical Practicum in Palliative Care Nursing - Part of Advanced Diploma in Palliative Care Nursing (ADPCN)
Course Description: The certificate provides the learners with opportunities to apply their palliative care knowledge and skills in the hospices and palliative care healthcare settings, guided by experienced practitioners. The clinical posting enables the learners to integrate what they have learnt on-campus into clinical practice, further refining their skills in various palliative healthcare settings.

Course Title: Post-Diploma Certificate in Advanced Clinical Practicum in Critical Care Nursing - Part of Advanced Diploma in Critical Care Nursing (ADCR2)
Course Description: The certificate equips students with learning opportunities in various critical care settings and dialysis centre during 10-week clinical postings. The students will apply the knowledge and skills they learnt in Post- Diploma Certificate 2 (Post- Diploma Certificate in Advanced Critical Care Nursing) when they provide care to the patients and support their families in the critical care environments.

Course Title: Post-Diploma Certificate in Advanced Clinical Practicum in Perioperative Nursing - Part of Advanced Diploma in Perioperative Nursing (ADPO2)
Course Description: The certificate equips students with skills to assist in surgical procedures and apply the nursing process in the management of patient undergoing surgery. The students will learn to apply clinical reasoning and decision- making skills for a patient, and implement measures to maintain the physical, microbiological and radiation safety of the patient undergoing surgery.

Course Title: Advanced Diploma in Critical Care Nursing (ADCR2)
Advanced Diploma in Palliative Care Nursing (ADPCN)
Course Description: The Advanced Diploma in Palliative Care Nursing (ADPCN) addresses Singapore’s demand for palliative care services by equipping Registered Nurses (RNs) with advanced competencies essential for caring of patients and families facing serious illnesses. The course enables the RNs to progress to Advanced Diploma level and enables them to obtain profound insights and skills to deliver and coordinate high-quality end-of-life care using evidence-based practices.

Course Title: Advanced Diploma in Perioperative Nursing (ADPO2)
WSQ Comply with Workplace Safety and Health Practices (Classroom and Asynchronous e-learning)
Course Description: The Workplace Safety and Health (WSH) Act is a legislation relating to the safety, health, and welfare of persons at work in a workplace. While employers have duties under the act to assess and control risks in the workplace, individual employees also play a very important role to identify and report risks, and to comply with safety practices so that to avoid injuries and potential health issues. This course equips learners with the necessary understanding on one’s responsibilities relating to safety and health in the working environment. They are to gain knowledge and skills in delivering healthcare that are in line with workplace safety and health practices.

Course Title: WSQ Client Education in Therapy Support (Classroom and Asynchronous e-learning)
Course Description:
Provide support therapists to reinforce client education to clients and caregivers, as instructed and guided by the therapists to achieve each client’s treatment or health promotion goals

Therapy Assistants (TAs) play an integral role in the rehabilitation of the patient. They are key players in supporting the therapists in carrying out the relevant routine interventions and reporting to the thera-pists if any changes are observed in the client’s performance while they are exercising with the client. A competent TA can follow the instructions of the therapist duly and perform the interventions as ac-curately as possible. He/she also reinforces client and caregiver education on intervention, exercises and/or assistive devices under the supervision of the therapist. This course will teach the relevant skills required for the TA to aid in client and caregiver education.

Course Title: WSQ Inventory Control and Equipment Maintenance in Rehabilitation Care (Classroom and Asynchronous e-learning)
Course Description:
Perform inventory control and maintain equipment for the therapy department.

Therapy Assistants (TAs) play an integral role in the rehabilitation of the patient. They are key players in supporting the therapists in carrying out the relevant routine interventions and reporting to the thera-pists if any changes are observed in the client’s performance while they are exercising with the client. A competent TA is able to follow the instructions of the therapist duly and perform the interventions as accurately as possible. He/she also helps in inventory management and maintaining the equipment that is used for therapy intervention. This course will teach the relevant skills required for the TA to perform inventory control and maintain therapy related equipment.

Course Title: Industrial Attachment - (SCTP) Certificate in Therapy Support (Healthcare)

Course Title: WSQ Respiratory Care in Nursing (L2) (AOP)
Course Description:
This course aims to equip Healthcare Assistant (HA) and Patient Service Associate (PSA) with knowledge and skills in performing record respiration and oxygen saturation rate for patients. 

By undergoing this programme, learner will gain fundamental knowledge on structure and functions of the respiratory system of the human body. 

Learner will also pick up the necessary knowledge on the importance of adhering to the organisation’s infection control policies/practices and how to apply them when performing record respiration and oxygen saturation rate for patients. 

In addition to this, the consequences and implications will also be emphasized to the learners if they did not follow infection control policies/practices when performing respiration and oxygen saturation rate monitoring for patients. 

This course will also impart learners on conducting evaluation of respiration rate and oxygen saturation for patients after the trainer delivery with necessary knowledge such as measurement of respiration rate, influencing factors and common respiration range and saturation.

Covering the type of devices needed, the indications and contraindication for administration of oxygen therapy will enable learners to performing administration of oxygen on patients.

Lastly, learners will take away with them on how to record observation on patients conditions based on organisational policies and procedures required and when to report and escalate such conditions to the relevant parties.

Course Title: WSQ Support Patient Care Delivery (AOP)
Course Description: The inability to accomplish activities of daily living (ADL) and other essential daily tasks may lead to unsafe condition and poor quality of life in patients. Healthcare workers should be aware of the importance of assessing ADL to identify and help patients who require assistance. This course introduces learners the basic care needs of patients. It equips learners with knowledge and skills that will enable them to perform basic patient assessment and to assist patient in ADL and other essential daily care activities. The course also provides learners with fundamental knowledge on infection control and prevention which is required in performing patient care activities.

Course Title: WSQ Infection Prevention and Control in Nursing Practice (L2) (AOP)
Course Description:
Learners will be introduced to the medical terminologies related to infection prevention and control. Having gained the knowledge will help learn to better appreciate the importance of organisation infection prevention and control procedures and its contributions and benefits to the patients’ values and needs.  

- Adhere to Organisation infection prevention and control procedures to better appreciate patients' values and needs
- Adhere and practice standard and transmission based precautions in accordance with patient’s conditions
- Administer appropriate infection control practices to patients and caregivers
- Appraise proper disinfection and sterilisation procedures
- Handle General and Biohazard Waste in accordance with organisation’s procedures

Course Title: WSQ Provide Mobility and Ambulation Assistance (AOP)
Course Description: Falling is one of the most common causes of patient injury in healthcare facilities. Falls occur for a variety of reasons ranging from dizziness, sudden pain, or poor transferring technics by care workers. This course provides learners an overview of risk factors related to fall. It equips learners with knowledge and skills on fall prevention through minimising environmental factors and applying proper technics that assist to ambulate, mobilise, position, and transfer stable patients safely.

Course Title: WSQ Patient Care Delivery in Nursing (L3)
Course Description:
This is a 3-day programme aiming to equip learners with necessary knowledge and skills in providing comprehensive, evidence-based nursing care according to plan of care to achieve optimal patient outcomes by carrying out the prescribed plans of care.

Step-by-Step sequencing process was adopted to sequence the Learning Units:

Prior to attending the face-to-face class, learners will be required to complete 5 hours of asynchronous e-learning covering an introductory for the selected LUs so that they will be well-prepared when coming in to class.

1. Ethical, Legal and Governance requirement in relation to nursing health practices 
2.. Clinical practice guidelines / pathway and types of assessment tools 
3. Psychiatric and mental health condition 
4. Geriatric and palliative care 
5. Common disease-specific to nursing care 
6. Nursing diagnosis nursing care plans and discharge procedures 
7. Monitoring the performance of junior staff

Course Title: WSQ Support Patient Care Delivery (Classroom and Asynchronous e-learning)

It equips learners with knowledge and skills that will enable them to perform basic patient assessment and to assist patient in ADL and other essential daily care activities. The course also provides learners with fundamental knowledge on infection control and prevention which is required in performing patient care activities.

Course Title: WSQ Clinical Support for Patient Service Associates (L4)
Course Description:
This is a 4-day programme aiming to equip learners with necessary knowledge and skills to effectively support clinical activities within a healthcare setting. This training program typically focuses on providing support in various clinical procedures and tasks while ensuring adherence to organizational protocols, standards, and guidelines.

To sequence these learning outcomes effectively, it's important to consider a logical progression that builds upon foundational skills and knowledge while gradually introducing more complex tasks.

Step-by-Step sequencing process was adopted to sequence the Learning Units:
Prior to attending the face-to-face class, learners will be required to complete 1 hour of asynchronous e-learning covering an introductory for the selected LUs so that they will be well-prepared when coming into class.
1. Establish effective communication with patients and healthcare professionals while recognising the significance of upholding patient confidentiality
2. Inspect and assess the physical appearance of drugs, referencing the date of their initial opening and following medication checking procedures, to accurately evaluate their condition.
3. Verify the prescriptions and the medication labelling against patient's identity, medication log and orders to ensure the right medications in the right quantities is given to the right patient.
4. Dispose of waste materials in accordance with organisational procedures. Clean and store all reusable equipment in accordance with organisational procedures.
5. Identify the types of eyedrops and its purpose.
6. Adhere to organisational protocol for venepuncture, uroflowmetry and electrocardiogram (ECG) by obtaining consent from patient regarding the procedures. Prepare the Patient for venepuncture, uroflowmetry and electrocardiogram (ECG). Perform clinical procedures including venepuncture, ECG and uroflowmetry according to protocol and guidelines and document the procedures administered.

Course Title: WSQ Medication Management in Nursing (L2)
Course Description:
This is a 1-day programme aims to equip learners with necessary knowledge and skills in performing safe handling and administration of medication so that they can assist in serving of oral and topical medications to the patients. Step by step sequential is used for the rational for sequencing.

Prior to face-to-face class, learners will undergo 1.5 hours of asynchronous e-learning covering K4: Common medication terminologies used in clinical practice in LU1, and K6: Common drug allergy reactions to medications and K7 Methods of disposal for medication in LU3.

The face-to-face class will begin with a recap of key learning points for asynchronous e-learning of K4 in LU1 before touching on the basics of Pharmacokinetics and Pharmacodynamics, providing learners with an understanding of how drugs interact with the body and vice versa. This is followed by a discussion on Drug Interactions, which builds on the previous topic by exploring how different drugs can affect each other’s performance. The lesson will delve into Side Effects and Adverse Reactions, equipping learners with the knowledge to anticipate and manage potential negative outcomes of medication use. The subsequent topics, Prescription Terminology and Dosage Forms, further enhance learners’ familiarity with common medication terminologies. The session then progresses to Routes of Administration and Dosage Terminology, which are crucial for understanding the different ways medications can be administered and their respective dosage instructions. This knowledge is essential for the safe and effective use of medications. The session will conclude with Safe Administration of Medications and Medication Errors. These topics are vital in clinical practice as they focus on the prevention of common errors in handling medications, thereby ensuring patient safety and the efficacy of treatment.

Course Title: Patient Empowerment on Self-care
Course Description: In today’s healthcare setting, patients are increasingly empowered with active involvement in decisions and actions affecting their health. Hence, learners should first be introduced to patient-centred practice as an approach to care that respects the patients and involves patients to co-own their healthcare journey. One of the important skills in patient-centred practice is communication skills. Learners acquire effective communication skills to understand patients' preferences and show that they respect patients’ perspectives. This eventually helps to build trust and rapport with patients and caregivers and encourage collaboration toward the healthcare goals.

Course Title: Patient Care Delivery in Nursing

This will enhance their role so they can take on higher responsibilities to better support therapist and nurses in the healthcare industry.  Performance gaps are identified from stakeholders’ feedback from assessments of support care staff competencies from their Learning Needs Analysis. This is one of the courses that has been identified as essential for career progression for support care staff so that they can better support qualified therapist and nurses, and as a refresher for existing staff who require re-assessment of competencies when there is a performance gap.

Course Title: Mobility and Ambulation in Nursing
Course Description: The sequence of topics reflects a patient-centered approach, addressing the needs and safety of the patients directly. This ensures that the learner is equipped with the necessary knowledge and skills to provide comprehensive and safe care. By following this sequence, the support care staff learners can develop a strong foundation in safety procedures and progressively acquire the skills needed for hands-on patient care, ultimately enhancing the quality of care provided. .
