Course Title: Effective Communication in Nursing
Course Description: This course targets senior support care staff who have been in the industry for at least two years and do not have prior knowledge or skills on Effective Communication in Nursing.  In healthcare practice, it is critical that staff are able to effectively communicate with their clients as well as within the healthcare team to enable accurate diagnosis and treatment as well as for continuity of care .  This knowledge will enhance the role of the senior support staff such that they can better support therapist and nurses to ensure safe and effective treatment and interventions.

Course Title: Infection Prevention and Control in Nursing Practice
Course Description: As infection prevention and control is an integral part of healthcare delivery, all healthcare workers have an important role to play in ensuring that their own safety and those of the patients under their care are not compromised. First, the learner must learn to adhere to Standard and Transmission-based Precautions. Standard precautions are the work practices required to achieve a basic level of infection prevention and control..  This section builds upon the foundation required for all healthcare workers to practise for all patients in all situations. Learners will learn the different types of transmission-based infections and the measures to take against infectious diseases. The key to the management of a patient with infectious disease is early detection, so learners will be taught on what symptoms to look out for, as well as when and how to escalate to supervisors. Once there is a confirmed infectious case, learners need to know how to conduct contact tracing to identify and monitor probable cases of spread. Part of the precautions will require learners to ensure proper control measures are in place for Equipment and Environment Management. Learners must have acquired the knowledge and skills above before they can educate Infection Control Practices for Patients and Caregivers to ensure their safety as well as to break the chain of infection. To do this, learners will learn the values and needs of patients and caregivers and how these values impact the infection control measures they will take.

Course Title: Group Therapy Planning and Implementations

Course Title: Knowing Your Nutritional Needs

Course Title: Conducting safe physical activities for seniors
Course Description: Engaging in regular physical activities (PA) can boost a senior’s physical and mental health significantly. A senior can continue to maintain functional independence and enjoy good quality of life when he/she stays physically active. It is important to prevent and reduce risks when conducting PA for seniors. An unsafe physical activity routine may lead to injuries, falls and more stress among seniors. Thus, the aim of this short course is to equip learners with the relevant knowledge & skills which will empower them to conduct a safe physical activity routine for seniors.

Course Title: HUR SmartTouch Health Coaching Technology Certification
Course Description: This course will develop competencies in participants to design, implement and evaluate safe and effective resistance training exercise programmes and routines in a community care setting. Foundations on exercise anatomy, physiology and principles will be introduced, based on current industry standards for practitioners in the field. Participants will also learn how to carry out baseline fitness assessments using validated testing procedures and use this as a basis to develop customised programmes for senior clients, with an emphasis on exercise safety, risk management and motivational factors.

Course Title: Phlebotomy Principles and Practice

Describe the pathological basis of common diseases including skin conditions in humans
Understand the role of a Phlebotomist as a healthcare professional
Follow proper patient identification procedures, patient communication and professional behaviour
Describe and perform proper skin puncture and venepuncture procedures
Identify, describe and troubleshoot procedural errors that can lead to failure in drawing blood
Demonstrate patient care following the completion of venepuncture
Demonstrate the safety precautions involved when drawing blood; and justify the need for safety precautions when drawing blood and quality assurance issues
List and explain effects of exercise, posture, and tourniquet application upon laboratory results

Course Title: Thinking Memory Series : Basic Course for Adult Mental Wellness (Classroom and Asynchronous e-learning)
Course Description:
The “Thinking Memory' programme developed by Dr. Refael Feuerstein is based on the late Prof. Reuven Feuerstein’s idea that memory is an active process of recreating reality. The goal of the program is to improve the awareness and control of memory functions of adult learners including older adults. This 60 hour programme starts with a dynamic assessment that provides you with a detailed cognitive profile. You will also take the dynamic assessment at the end so you can see progress in your own cognitive and memory abilities.
This course is designed for the volunteers or anyone who works in the care economy especially senior care. They will learn skills that will help them apply the skill of mediated learning to help others with memory and cognition. This course uses an online Learning Potential Assessment Device - Digital (LPAD-D) as a dynamic assessment to help provide a cognitive function profile so that individuals can be more targeted in their learning approach.

Course Title: Child First Aid (Asynchronous e-learning)
Course Description: The aim of the course is to provide relevant knowledge and skills in rendering first aid treatment to a child or infant during any emergency at the childcare or pre-school centres.

Course Title: Advanced Clinical Exercise Physiotherapy for Metabolic, Cardiovascular, Pulmonary and Oncologic Diseases
Course Description:
The Advanced Clinical Exercise Testing and Prescription training course consists of a series of workshops which aim to equip physiotherapists with advanced principles and theories in clinical exercise physiology to understand the impact of exercise or activity on the human physiological system in specialised populations. Each workshop is designed to be standalone, covering a specific group of disease populations. 
This “Advanced Clinical Exercise Physiotherapy for Metabolic, Cardiovascular, Pulmonary & Oncologic Diseases workshop” focuses on the development of effective management and prevention strategies, for metabolic, oncologic, cardiovascular and pulmonary diseases through exercise, lifestyle, and behaviour modifications. It builds on the understanding of exercise physiology and clinical exercise prescription knowledge acquired from the intermediate workshop (insert title) and    is suitable for those with a minimum of 3 years of working experience in physiotherapy with some experience in cardiopulmonary, musculoskeletal and/or geriatrics/neurological rehabilitation.  
The course will discuss the application of the current concepts to assessing and managing cardiovascular and pulmonary diseases, helping to deepen the understanding of physiological responses to exercise in individuals with chronic conditions. With the programme designed specifically for adult learners, the learning of in-depth and clinically relevant topics is achieved through the blended learning approach via self-directed learning (SDL), flipped classroom, team-based learning (TBL), problem-based learning (PBL), discussion seminar and practicum. The course will include analysis and critical appraisal of the supporting theory and available evidence, along with clinical reasoning for applying assessment and techniques in managing patients with cardiovascular and pulmonary diseases.

Course Title: Actions for Healthcare Sustainability 101
Course Description:
It is highly beneficial for individuals who play sustainability-related role to possess actionable knowledge and skillsets needed to make measurable advancement towards a green economy. 

This one-day course introduces a holistic and deeper understanding of sustainability, in terms of the current global context and the essential measurement and management techniques that are critical for the green transition. The course is designed for the healthcare sector, and it will cover the relationships of each sustainability concept to the healthcare sector. The learning will be scaffolded over the course, and by the end, an ‘action plan’ for each learner will be shaped by consolidating and applying the learning through a project. 

Upon successful completion of this course, learners should possess the knowledge and skillsets that allow them to start making measurable contribution to the green agenda. Learners will bring back a shared motivation, language and toolset for collaborative work.

Course Title: Inter-professional Workshop on Cognitive Rehabilitation for Persons with Cognitive-Perceptual Dysfunction (Synchronous e-Learning)
Course Description: This two-day introductory workshop is targeted at occupational therapists, nurse clinicians and other allied health professionals who are working with cognitive-perceptual issues seen in persons with a range of neurological and psychiatric conditions. The aim is to equip healthcare professionals with interdisciplinary knowledge and skills in basic cognitive-perceptual assessments, as well as intervention strategies.

Course Title: Functional Fitness Screening and Falls Efficacy Prescription for Older Adults (Classroom & Asynchronous e-Learning)
Course Description: This course educates participants on the importance of physical activity for older adults and the key fitness components needed for functional fitness. It also covers falls efficacy, equipping participants with the skills to help older adults build confidence, manage fall risks, and maintain greater independence.

Course Title: Interprofessional Low Vision Rehabilitation: Basic Foundations
Course Description:
This customised basic low vision rehabilitation course provides occupational therapists, physiotherapists and registered nurses with basic knowledge and skills to manage patients with low vision. It will provide additional best practice knowledge for low vision beyond pre-registration professional education. 

Participants will apply visual assessments to identify basic problems in vision limiting daily activities, and develop basic management strategies around visual skills, environmental modifications, functional mobility training and assistive technology devices. Participants will also develop skills to support their patients with visual impairment in psychosocial adjustment and seek resources to better manage the needs of such patients.

Course Title: PTY6202 Advanced Musculoskeletal Practice 2 (Classroom, Synchronous & Asynchronous)
Course Description: The course is designed for musculoskeletal physiotherapy practitioners who wish to advance practical and reasoning skills used in an established musculoskeletal setting, such as outpatient clinics in hospitals or polyclinics, or a nascent setting, such as community rehabilitation centres.

Course Title: Intermediate Clinical Exercise Testing and Prescription
Course Description: This course aims to equip physiotherapists with principles in clinical exercise physiology to understand the impact of exercise or activity on the human physiological system. For the effective management and prevention of chronic non-communicable diseases (NCDs) through modifications of exercise, lifestyle, and behaviours. This course aims to build on the understanding of exercise physiology and clinical exercise prescription knowledge acquired from the entry-level physiotherapy training and is appropriate for early-career physiotherapists with two (2) to three (3) years of post-registration experience. It will enrich the application of the current concepts to the assessment and management of non-communicable diseases. The course helps to deepen the understanding of physiological responses to exercise in individuals who exercise regularly and those who not. With the programme designed specifically for adult learners, the learning of in-depth and clinically relevant topics is achieved through the blended learning approach via self-directed learning (SDL), flipped classroom, team-based learning (TBL), problem-based learning (PBL), discussion seminar and practicum.  The course will include analysis and critical appraisal of the supporting theory and available evidence along with clinical reasoning for practical application of assessment and management techniques In the management of patients with NCDs and other co-morbidities.

Course Title: Clinical Reasoning in Assessment and Therapy Planning for Older Persons with Dysphagia - Basic
Course Description:
This two day basic introductory workshop is targeted at speech-language therapists working with older persons with swallowing difficulties across various settings. In this workshop, participants will be equipped with the knowledge and skills to apply clinical reasoning in information gathering, assessment, and management of swallowing difficulties in the geriatric population. 

The workshop is designed in 2 parts, and participants may choose to attend this workshop only, or choose to extend their learning at the intermediate level workshop. In this workshop, participants will acquire knowledge and skills required to apply clinical reasoning to case-based discussions of older persons with swallowing difficulties.

Speakers include practicing speech-language therapists, and academic faculty.

Course Title: Clinical Reasoning in Assessment and Therapy Planning for Older Persons with Dysphagia - Intermediate (Classroom & Asynchronous e-Learning)
Course Description:
This two day workshop builds upon the earlier workshop "Clinical Reasoning in Assessment and Therapy Planning for Older Persons with Dysphagia - Basic". It is targeted at speech-language therapists working with older persons with swallowing difficulties across various settings. In this workshop, participants will be equipped with the knowledge and skills to apply clinical reasoning in information gathering, assessment, and management of swallowing difficulties in the geriatric population. 

In this workshop, participants will demonstrate their ability to show their clinical reasoning skills in both case discussions and simulated patient scenarios and peer discussions/presentations. 

Speakers include practicing speech-language therapists, and academic faculty.

Course Title: Defusing Difficult Situations
Course Description: Diffusing Difficult Situations is meticulously designed to address the complexities of conflict within healthcare settings. It begins with an introduction to the essence of conflict and its significance, followed by an exploration of various conflict styles, helping participants understand their own approaches as well as those of others. The course delves into the cognitive, emotional, and behavioral skills that are pivotal for resolving conflicts effectively. Participants will have the opportunity to apply these skills through real-life scenarios, ensuring they are well-prepared to handle actual cases in their professional environment. The workshop culminates with a conclusion that encapsulates the key takeaways and outlines actionable steps for attendees. By the end of this program, healthcare professionals will be adept at recognizing the sources and ramifications of conflict, identifying and applying different conflict styles and preferences, executing conflict resolution and management strategies in diverse situations, conducting crucial conversations with patients and their families, and ultimately enhancing teamwork, productivity, and satisfaction for both patients and employees. This holistic approach ensures that participants not only understand the theoretical aspects of conflict resolution but also are equipped to apply these strategies practically, fostering a harmonious and efficient workplace.

Course Title: Cognitive Rehabilitation for Occupational Therapists (Synchronous e-Learning)
Course Description:
This three-day introductory workshop is a continuation of the two-day inter-professional primer on cognitive rehabilitation and focuses on knowledge and skills supporting occupational therapy practice for cognitive-perceptual issues seen in persons with a range of neurological and psychiatric conditions. The aim is to provide occupational therapists with the knowledge and skills in occupational therapy centric cognitive-perceptual assessments, problem identification, goal setting and intervention plans.

The objectives of the workshop are to equip occupational therapists with the knowledge and skills in the following areas:

1. Occupation-focused models and approaches in cognitive and perceptual dysfunctions.
2. Occupational Therapy assessments for cognitive and perceptual problems.
3. Problem identification and goal setting for cognitive perceptual issues.
4. Occupational Therapy intervention strategies and plans for management of cognitive-perceptual problems.

Course Title: Internationalisation Series: Smart Business and Healthcare - Japan
Course Description:
As a super-aged society, Japan holds many useful lessons for Singapore, as Singapore aspires to become a healthier Blue Zone 3.0 state. Japan has confronted many social issues, including a declining birth rate, a super-aged population (30% population above 65 years old), social inequality and social isolation, while experiencing a paradigm shift in its values, culture, and economy, including changes in lifestyles, increased employment of foreigners, and advancements in Information Technology (IT). 

These challenges and opportunities led to the establishment of the Japanese Digital Agency in Sep 2021 to maximize use of digital technology through regulatory and administrative reforms in healthcare. Significant progress was made since 2022 in various digital health areas, such as expansion of remote medical care, increase in healthcare-related apps, and the utilisation of healthcare data and the metaverse to improve care quality and value.

- Apply tools to manage and communicate information in a crisis

Course Title: Ethical Practices in Social Service Management
Course Description:
Singapore’s ageing population is rising rapidly. By 2030, an estimated one million Singaporeans will be aged 65 and above. Thus, an increasing number of seniors will need access to support services in the community to age in place. A strong community- based support system which aims to achieve satisfactory physical, cognitive, psychosocial well-being of seniors and keeping them as long as possible in the community by minimising the admission, re-admission rates cum premature institutionalisation in nursing homes was envisioned. To make this possible, a strong social-health integration services and programs i.e. proactive, broad based and integrated programs partnering with various government agencies and community partners; will be crucial. Therefore, understanding and training on legal, ethical and regulatory requirements is important as all SOPs in community health-social services programme delivering are legally bound and/or linked, which means that there will be legal implications if there are any breach in protocols. Hence, to understand the laws, processes and ethical issues in the sectors is essential.

This course is administered by Hua Mei Training Academy, an initiative of Tsao Foundation.

Also, with the use of effective communication, to recognise the behavioural change, factors that can affect interactions, and the importance of social activities or engagements for Persons with dementia. Not to be missed, learners will also pick up skills to identify the various ways to prevent and cope with personal stress and burnout.

This course is administered by Hua Mei Training Academy, an initiative of Tsao Foundation.

Course Title: Essential Workplace Health and Nursing Practices HC
Course Description:
Course Objective
The objective of the course is to equip learners with the skills and knowledge to understand the various issues that accompany ageing which includes Geriatric syndrome, medication and fall risk, mobility and nutrition issues; and the relevant support and skills training techniques for transfer and position, infection prevention and control, perineal and catheter care, health monitoring, escort services and suitable exercises to enable them to age well in the community.

This course is administered by Hua Mei Training Academy, an initiative of Tsao Foundation.

Course Title: Customised Eldercare Training by Registered Nurse
Course Description: Caregivers are able to care for the basic needs of the senior.

Course Title: Managing Challenges in Alzheimer
Course Description: This course aims for caregivers to be able to identify and anticipate behavioural issues and challenges in people with Alzheimer.

Course Title: Special Care: Respiratory Care & Suctioning
Course Description: Aim for caregivers to learn the correct techniques to respiratory care through aseptic technique of suctioning, applying the oxygen mask or prongs, delivering medication through puff, spacer or nebuliser and relieving breathlessness phlegm build up

Course Title: Ageing-in-place with physical exercise and cognitive stimulation
Course Description: This course aims at helping caregivers perform safe and essential exercises and rehabilitation for their care recipient.

Course Title: Customised Disability Training (Below 65 years old)
Course Description: Caregivers are able to care for the basic needs of the person needing care.

Course Title: CPR & First Aid
Course Description: This course aims for caregivers to be able to identify symptoms of choking, heart attack, bleeding and fractures. And to be able to perform first aid skills and perform CPR in the event of an emergency.

Course Title: Special Care: Nasogastric/PEG tube
Course Description: Aim at helping caregivers learn and apply the basic skill of feeding liquid through the feeding tube and the daily steps to care for it.

Course Title: Special Care: Urinary Catheter Care
Course Description:
This course aims to help caregivers understand about the care and management for urine catheters. By
learning about the signs and symptoms of urinary tract infection, we hope caregivers can learn how to practice
correct techniques and avoid practices that may cause infection.

Course Title: Special Care: Wound Care
Course Description: Aim for caregivers to learn identify, manage and prevent simple cuts, wound and pressure sores

Course Title: Basic Cardiac Life Support + AED Course
Course Description: The Basic Cardiac Life Support Automated External Defibrillation Course will equip you with knowledge and skills in resuscitating collapsed victims. It is a very comprehensive course encompassing critical life-saving skills, ranging from the rescuing of a choked victim (conscious and unconscious), to performing quality CPR, for both adults and infants according to the guidelines provided by the Singapore Resuscitation and First Aid Council.

Course Title: Standard First Aid Course
Course Description: The Standard First Aid Course (SFAC) prepares people to act as first responders in the workplace. Recognized as the CERT First Aider Course, it provides vital training in first aid, CPR, and AED use. In addition to developing the ability to respond to typical accidents, injuries, and medical emergencies, participants actively recognize, plan, and manage situations.

Course Title: Physical Care for the Elderly
Course Description: This 4 hour course aims to provide family caregivers and foreign domestic workers with a basic level of knowledge and skills required for the physical care of the elderly. The course focuses on areas of personal hygiene, dietary regime, exercise and mobility, as well as how the caregiver can identify and manage common elderly ailments.

Course Title: Physical and Mental Care for the Elderly
Course Description: This 7.5 hours course aims to provide family caregivers and foreign domestic workers with a basic level of knowledge and skills required for the mental and physical care of the elderly. On the mental aspect, the course focuses on how caregivers can communicate with the elderly as well as looking out for signs of negative emotions. On the physical aspect, the course focuses on areas of personal hygiene, dietary regime, exercise and mobility, as well as how the caregiver can identify and manage common elderly ailments.

Course Title: Ageing and Public Policy
Course Description: This course introduces learners from the ageing care industry to a range of key policy issues that are faced by societies with ageing population. The course draws on experiences of mainly Asian countries to provide learners with a grounded understanding of ageing policies in multiple areas of ageing. The focus will be on exploring the evidence driven process of crafting public policies, their implementation, differentiating the various policy tools, while addressing the hurdles in execution, along with examining their effects on the welfare of older persons in Asia. This course equips learners with knowledge and skills which are essential to living, working and communicating with older persons and critically evaluating ageing-related issues and policies.

Course Title: Foundations of Health Coaching
Course Description:
At the end of the course, participants should be able to:
1)Understand what health coaching is and its benefits 
2)Apply basic motivational interviewing skills to help build self-efficacy 
3)Display core coaching competencies during patient interaction
4)Use newly gained skills and tools to have better conversations 
5)Empower others to develop personalized health goals 

Currently there are a lack of training courses that focuses solely on health coaching. Therefore this course aims to equip participants with the necessary skills and knowledge of health coaching, in order for them to empower others to set and achieve personalized health goals.

Course Title: BASIC CARDIAC LIFE SUPPORT COURSE + AUTOMATED EXTERNAL DEFIBRILLATION
Course Description: The Basic Cardiac Life Support Automated External Defibrillation Course will equip you with knowledge and skills in resuscitating collapsed victims. It is a very comprehensive course encompassing critical life-saving skills, ranging from the rescuing of a choked victim (conscious and unconscious), to performing quality CPR, for both adults and infants according to the guidelines provided by the Singapore Resuscitation and First Aid Council

Course Title: Health Coaching for Older Adults
Course Description:
Health coaching is a health intervention through which a health coach engages with a client by listening and observing so as to provide personalized solutions for facilitating positive health behaviour change.

This course aims to provide foundational knowledge and skills required to perform health coaching for older adults living in community settings. Participants will also learn key concepts, ranging from gerontology, nutrition, and physical exercise, that can be used to facilitate effective health coaching. The course also offers participants with an overview of job opportunities related to the growing popularity of health coaching in Singapore.

Course Title: Data Privacy, Protection and Cybersecurity (Synchronous)
Course Description: The core business of hospitals delivering just care services is of the past. Hospitals' business is rapidly evolving towards meeting customers’ demand for a seamless care experience. To achieve this, hospitals are investing resources to review its processes so that staff and clinicians can deliver the right care, in the right place and at the right time. This helps hospitals reduce costs and retain revenue, and also improving patient health outcomes and experience.
Training Company: Hmi Institute Of Health Sciences Pte. Ltd.

Course Title: Team Building and Development
Course Description: For any organization to thrive and excel, it is paramount that individuals within teams can work well together to support each other. Team Building and development are critical aspects of creating a successful and high-performing team. However, there are often challenges exacerbated with the ever-changing team dynamics and fast paced accelerated changing society.
Training Company: Hmi Institute Of Health Sciences Pte. Ltd.

Course Title: Facilitating Language and Communication Skills in Children with Developmental Delays – The Hanen Way
Course Description:
•Learn essential, basic concepts about communication and stages of communication development
•Understand the importance of the child's active participation in frequent, extended communicative interactions in daily routines at home
•Learn strategies and ideas to facilitate and enhance communication in their children with communication delays
•Learn to apply language facilitation strategies across contexts so intervention becomes a natural part of parent-child interactions.

Course Title: Foundational Skills for Facilitating Eating, Drinking, and Swallowing Skills in Children
Course Description:
•The process of normal swallowing, including the body parts involved
•How safe feeding and swallowing can be compromised
•Appropriate posture for eating and drinking
•Food and fluid modifications
•Introduction to the various type of feeding and drinking adaptive devices

Course Title: Fundamental Principles of Seating and Positioning
Course Description:
•Understand the importance of posture and positioning and how it affects performance of functional activities
•Understand the basic seating & 24 hour positioning needs of clients in a simplified manner
•Identify common seating difficulties and lying positions that clients experience
•Learn strategies for proper positioning in wheelchairs and classroom chairs as well as lying in bed
•Learn basic tips and strategies for transferring children to and from wheelchairs
•Learn wheelchair and cushion maintenance strategies, specific to the seating/positioning systems that are commonly used in CPAS
•Be familiar with the process of purchasing a seating and positioning system in CPAS

Course Title: Sensory Processing Dysfunction Commonly Found in Children with Special Needs
Course Description:
•Overview of the sensory system -Theory of Sensory Integration.
•The different sensory systems.
•Identification of sensory modulation disorder- common sensory issues and their manifestation.
•Praxis –Dyspraxia
•Preparing sensory Routine for Home and preparation of home programme
•Accommodation for classroom /Environmental modification
Oral sensitivities
•Oral motor and sensory exercises for sensitive mouth.

Course Title: Foundation Skills in Handling Children with Food Refusal Issues
Course Description:
•The process of swallowing.
•Manifestations of food refusal behavior.
•Underlying principles for food refusal behavior.
•General guidelines for reducing food refusal behavior.

Course Title: Strategies in Training Persons with Physical Disabilities to perform basic ADL - Feeding, Dressing and Grooming
Course Description:
•Analyze how Gross Motor Skills affect Fine Motor Skills.
•Understand and Apply various strategies in facilitating care and independence in Basic Activities of Daily Living – Feeding/Eating, Dressing, Grooming.
•Gain knowledge about assistive devices appropriate to facilitate care and independence in Basic Activities of Daily Living – Feeding/Eating, Dressing, Grooming.

Course Title: Toy Adaptation Workshop
Course Description:
•	How can I make my toys more accessible?
•	Selecting a suitable toy.
•	Hands-on guided practice in modifying the toy, which includes disassembling, drilling and soldering.

Course Title: Assistive Technology for Cerebral Palsy
Course Description:
•Understand the definition and classification of Assistive Technology
•Assistive Technology Framework and its application
•Types of Assistive Technology and its use in different contexts e.g. home and living, school and workplace and play
•Considerations for prescription of Assistive Technology for persons with Cerebral Palsy

Course Title: Hand Function, Pre-Writing & Handwriting Skills for Children
Course Description:
•Understand basic hand functions and development of hand skills. 
•Know what the pre-writing skills and pre-requisites for writing are. 
•Gain knowledge about different developmental stages of pre-writing skills. 
•Apply various activities to develop pre-writing skills. 
•Learn foundation skills of handwriting. 
•Identify common handwriting difficulties, and their causes. 
•Gain some ideas and learn some strategies for children with hand function difficulties or fine motor difficulties. 
•Gain some ideas and learn some strategies for children with handwriting difficulties. 
•Understand basic pre scissoring, scissoring development and development of scissoring grip.  
•Apply various activities to develop pre-scissoring and scissoring skills and use of special scissors. 
•To understand the Handwriting without Tears approach, learn some strategies and materials that can be found online or off the shelves.

Course Title: Toilet Training for Children with Special Needs
Course Description:
•Understand the importance to manage toileting
•Identify signs of toilet readiness
•Practice strategies to manage toileting difficulties
•Tailor resources from booklet for their child’s needs
•To be able to use visuals in classroom and toilet to facilitate toilet training

Course Title: NSA- Solution Focused Counselling - Mandarin
Course Description: This course will share Solution-Focused Counselling techniques with participants, helping them to focus more on positive approaches to problem-solving.

Course Title: NSA- Resistance Bands 101
Course Description: Participants will learn to differentiate between the various Resistance Bands (Thera-band, Compact R-band, Fit Loop, Figure 8, and Resistance Ring) available to them.  They will then be able make informed decisions to select which offers the most health benefits and avoid injuries.

Course Title: NSA - Enhancing Sleep Quality and Managing Insomnia with TCM
Course Description: This course aims to provide seniors with practical knowledge and techniques from Traditional Chinese Medicine TCM to improve sleep quality and manage insomnia. Participants will learn how to incorporate herbal medicated diets and acupressure massage into their daily routines to promote restorative sleep, enhance overall well being, and reduce sleep disturbances

Course Title: (eCornell) Assessing the Effects of Obesity
Course Description: In this course, you will examine both types of factors and discover why the environment, encompassing everything from cultural and economic influences to government policies, is believed to significantly contribute to the increase in obesity rates.

Course Title: (eCornell) Data Management in Healthcare
Course Description: In this course, you will examine the efficient storage, management, and processing of healthcare data, with a focus on implementing relational data models that emphasize structure, integrity, and manipulation

Course Title: (eCornell) Designing Digital Healthcare Tools
Course Description: In this course, you will delve into the human elements that affect the design, implementation, and evaluation of tools in the healthcare industry

Course Title: (eCornell) Foundations of Metabolic Health
Course Description: In this course, you will examine metabolism and its role in energy balance, homeostasis, and the body's response to dietary intake and physical activity. You will discover metabolism's significance to an individual's overall health.

Course Title: (eCornell) Machine Learning in Healthcare
Course Description: In this course, you will explore supervised and unsupervised learning, two key machine learning approaches that can help you maximize your data's potential

Course Title: (eCornell) Natural Language Processing in Healthcare
Course Description: In this course, you will discover how natural language processing (NLP) can help you transform free text into structured data for extracting insights

Course Title: (eCornell) Optimizing Metabolic Health Through Nutrition
Course Description: In this course, you will examine what's needed for sustained fat loss. Which is more effective: diet or exercise? You will explore the answer to that question and gain skills that will help you design a diet based on the scientific evidence.

Course Title: EFFECTIVE COMMUNICATION IN NURSING (AOP)
Course Description: This module equips learners with knowledge and skills to communicate assessment results, intervention plans and outcomes to patients, families and/or caregivers and healthcare teams for routine cases.
Training Company: Hmi Institute Of Health Sciences Pte. Ltd.

Course Title: GROUP THERAPY PLANNING AND IMPLEMENTATION (AOP)
Course Description: This module follows a simple-to-complex sequencing approach, gradually building learners' skills and confidence as they progress through more challenging concepts. Starting with fundamental knowledge, learners first develop a strong foundation on group therapy interventions progresses to practical skills like client selection, facilitation, modifying group activities and addressing challenging behaviour with management of group dynamics to enhance therapy outcomes.
Training Company: Hmi Institute Of Health Sciences Pte. Ltd.

Course Title: INFECTION PREVENTION AND CONTROL IN NURSING PRACTICE (AOP)
Course Description: This module equips learners with knowledge and skills to carry out organisation's infection control and prevention.
Training Company: Hmi Institute Of Health Sciences Pte. Ltd.

Course Title: INTERVENTION IMPLEMENTATION (AOP)
Course Description: This module adopts a simple-to-complex sequencing approach, progressively building learners' skills and confidences as they advance through increasingly challenging tasks and responsibilities. The sequence begins by establishing a solid foundation in care planning before transitioning to the application of this knowledge in clinical settings. As learners progress, they are gradually introduced to more complex responsibilities, such as decision-making in monitoring care, modifying plans and instructing junior staff to ensure the effective implementation of individualized care plans
Training Company: Hmi Institute Of Health Sciences Pte. Ltd.

Course Title: PATIENT CARE DELIVERY IN NURSING (COMMUNITY CARE) (AOP)
Course Description: This module is to equip the learner with knowledge and skills in planning and carrying out the plan of care to better support therapist and nurses in the healthcare settings.
Training Company: Hmi Institute Of Health Sciences Pte. Ltd.

Course Title: CoC in Basic First Aid + AED
Course Description:
Participants will learn about the following:
1) Essentials of First Aid
2) Unconscious Casualty (Non-Cardiac Arrest)
3) Respiratory Issues
4) Bleeding, Wounds and Bandaging
5) Musculoskeletal Injuries
6) Burn Injuries and Transportation Techniques
7) CPR (Hands-Only) + AED

Course Title: CoC in Standard First Aid (Blended Learning)
Course Description:
Participants will learn about the following:
1) Essentials of First Aid
2) Unconscious Casualty (Non-Cardiac Arrest)
3) Respiratory Problems
4) Shock, Bleeding and Wounds
5) Musculoskeletal Injuries
6) Burn Injuries
7) Other First Aid Knowledge
8) CPR (Hands-Only)+AED

Course Title: NSA e-learning-Can I Hold You a While Longer?
Course Description: This course enable learners to learn about dementia and its impact on the family, discover resources and redefine their caregiving role.

Course Title: Professional Certificate in Essential Health Coaching for Healthy Ageing (Classroom and Synchronous E-Learning)
Course Description:
Course Description:
This landmark initiative, built on a foundation of a groundbreaking research and born from a three-year (2018-2020) strategic research project led by Professor Theng Yin Leng, equips and empowers learners with essential skills to promote holistic wellbeing and healthy ageing. 

The Professional Certificate in Essential Health Coaching for Healthy Ageing is a groundbreaking programme that empowers healthcare professionals, caregivers, and support staff to revolutionize the way they support individuals in achieving holistic wellbeing and healthy ageing. In today's complex healthcare landscape, the need for effective health coaching has never been more pressing. With the rise of chronic diseases, mental health concerns, and an ageing population, healthcare systems are grappling with the challenges of providing person-centred, sustainable care.

This programme is a transformative opportunity for anyone passionate about delivering exceptional care and support. By combining cutting-edge research, expert insights, and practical skills, this programme equips learners with the confidence and expertise to drive meaningful change in the lives of others. Whether you're a healthcare professional, caregiver, or support staff, this programme will empower you to make a lasting impact, foster deeper connections with those you support, and contribute to a more compassionate and effective healthcare system.

Course Title: Managing Challenging Cases in Elderly Patients (Classroom and Practical Training)
Course Description:
This course enables the oral healthcare professionals to provide appropriate care to allow patients to retain their teeth in function. In particular, this course targets the conditions which often affect the geriatric population. Retention of the dentition is known to improve the quality of life and the longevity of the patients. This would also help in bringing down the financial burden on the patients and the health system in Singapore. 

This course is intended for general dental practitioners and encompasses both dental health services and care for the elderly.

Course Title: Master of Science in Sustainable Healthcare
Course Description:
The Master of Science in Sustainable Medicine (hereafter MScSM) is a new programme organised and offered by the Centre for Sustainable Medicine with support from other NUS faculties and schools to equip health professionals from Singapore and across the world with the foundational knowledge and core competencies to be at the forefront of the net zero transition in healthcare. The programme is designed primarily with medical professionals, healthcare administrators, health policymakers, industry professionals, and health educators in mind, but individuals without prior healthcare experience who plan to join the industry are also encouraged to apply.

The programme will be offered as a 12-month full-time or 24-month part-time postgraduate single degree coursework programme consisting of 10 courses (4 credits per course, of which 4 are compulsory instructional courses, 2 are dedicated to capstone research, and remaining 4 are elective courses). The programme will be implemented in August 2025, Semester 1 of the Academic Year 2025/26.

Course Title: Precision Medicine Toolkit for Healthcare Analytics (Synchronous e-Learning)
Course Description:
This short course is designed to empower participants with the skills necessary to advance precision medicine through targeted programming and analytics. With a focus on real-world applications, participants will explore how data-driven techniques enhance personalized healthcare solutions. Through hands-on experience, learners will discover how to effectively utilize programming tools and analytical methods to interpret and manage biomedical data, ultimately contributing to more precise and individualized patient care strategies.

Fundamentals in precision medicine programming

a. Programming concepts with Python

b. Data structure and algorithms

c. Data analysis and visualization with Python

Precision analytics

a. Fundamentals of R-programming

b. Data manipulation

c. Biostatistics

Rapid automation in biomedicine informatics

a. Shell scripting in Linux

b. Text processing and data manipulation

c. System and software management

d. Automation with bash scripting

This course provides practical skills and knowledge, essential for those looking to apply programming and analytics in the field of precision medicine.

Applicants must hold a Bachelor’s degree or be in the final stages of completing one. Candidates with alternative qualifications and relevant experience may also be considered on a case-by-case basis, pending approval from the school.

There are 4 hours of independent work to be completed outside of course hours, which count toward assessment.

Course Title: Skills Training in Health Coaching
Course Description: This is the first health coaching course to go beyond classroom learning, and gives participants the chance to practise their health coaching skills in patient interactions. This course provides participants with the opportunity to witness live health coaching sessions conducted with a variety of patients within a hospital setting. Participants will then be given the chance to conduct health coaching sessions with supervision and feedback by trained health coaches. This further supports the development of participants' health coaching skills, and empowers them to apply their skills in a real world setting.

Course Title: Microbiology, Immunology and Infectious Diseases (Classroom, Asynchronous, Synchronous e-Learning)
Course Description:
1. Demonstrate basic essential in-depth knowledge of microbiology and fundamentals of immunology. 
2. Demonstrate understanding of mechanisms of microbial pathogenesis. 
3. Demonstrate understanding of common infectious diseases caused by bacterial, viral, fungal and parasitic infections. 
4. Demonstrate understanding of antibiotics and antimicrobial resistance. 
5. Demonstrate understanding of vaccinations for healthcare workers and immunization strategies for diverse populations. 
6.  Apply the knowledge of microbiology, immunology and microbial pathogenesis to diagnose, treat and prevent bacterial, viral, fungal and parasitic infections. 
7. Apply knowledge of microbiology, immunology, vaccination and outbreak control to implement strategies for containment and prevention of outbreaks.

Course Title: Management of Deep Caries - Vital Pulp Therapy or Root Canal Treatment (Classroom and Practical Training)
Course Description:
Through the course, participants will:
1. appreciate the clinical and histological presentation of caries and endodontic diseases
2. understand the challenges of diagnosing pulpal condition, the available treatment modalities and the factors affecting the case selections
3. apply the use of bioceramic material and rotary instruments in safe and efficient manners

Course Title: Transforming Digital Health: Innovations in AI and Informatics
Course Description:
Dive into the forefront of healthcare evolution with our immersive two-day course, "Transforming Digital Health: Innovations in Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Informatics." 

This dynamic program is designed for healthcare professionals, administrators, innovators, health informaticians, data scientists, policymakers, healthcare consultants, and researchers eager to harness the power of digital transformation in healthcare. Gain a comprehensive overview of digital health advancements, including AI, machine learning, data analytics, and informatics, focusing on their profound impact on healthcare delivery, practical applications, and data-driven decision-making.

Participants will explore the critical importance of interoperability in digital health systems and learn best practices for integrating new technologies seamlessly with existing healthcare infrastructures. Discover innovative ways to use digital tools to enhance patient engagement, improve patient experience, and foster better health outcomes. The course also emphasizes fostering collaboration between health and technical professionals to drive innovation and implement effective digital health solutions. Uncover strategies to revolutionize patient care, streamline operations, and ensure sustainable adoption of digital health technologies. By the end of this course, you will be well-equipped to lead and innovate at the vanguard of digital health, where technology meets transformative impact.

Course Title: Research Methods in Translational Medicine (From Target to Treatment)
Course Description: The first part of this course  focuses on the process of target discovery to lead development. From basic science research to large dataset analysis, learners will be brought through current and emerging experimental techniques to discover trends and patterns to improve the target development process. The second part of the course will delve into clinical research development where learners will learn regarding evidence-based methods to enhance trial design to test drug safety and efficacy, while maintaining ethical standards. Overall, learners will evaluate existing research literature to identify gaps, formulate research questions, and design studies to improve healthcare.

Course Title: Implementing Net Zero Healthcare
Course Description: Combining conceptual frameworks and insights from policy and industry experts, this course equips students with the theoretical knowledge and practical skills needed to drive change at multiple levels — from their immediate professional environment, through to broader societal and political landscapes. Students will learn to strategically navigate and address organisational challenges, empowering them to influence, innovate, and lead in shaping the future of healthcare sustainability.

Course Title: Net Zero Analytics (Synchronous and Asynchronous e-Learning)
Course Description: Learners will develop the ability to measure and understand  data, and analyse the carbon footprint of clinics, hospitals and health systems, with a focus on life cycle assessments as an analytical technique. Teaching will cover the design and implementation of these methods, focusing on how these analytical techniques can be used to drive clinical practice and policy towards net zero healthcare, and how to critically appraise published environmental impact assessments.

Course Title: Principles of Sustainable Healthcare Practice
Course Description: This course takes a deep dive into the complex field of sustainable healthcare. Learners will gain an understanding of the frameworks used to guide climate mitigation and adaptation interventions in healthcare .Structured to establish the critical links between climate change, human health, and health systems, the course covers key interventions and solutions for major carbon hotspots throughout a health system so that learners will drive healthcare decarbonisation, effective resource stewardship and the transformation of healthcare practices.

Course Title: Global Issues in Infection Prevention and Control (Classroom, Synchronous and Asynchronous e-Learning)
Course Description: This advanced course is designed to provide participants with a comprehensive understanding of global challenges in infection prevention and control. Participants will explore the unique considerations, emerging issues, and best practices related to infection prevention on a global scale. The course also emphasises the integration of quality improvement methodologies to enhance healthcare practices at various levels of healthcare services delivery. Participants will also learn about global health issues including responding to international health emergencies and infection prevention in different settings.

Course Title: Transformative Leadership Development in Healthcare (Classroom, Synchronous, and Asynchronous e-Learning)
Course Description:
1. Demonstrate understanding of various leadership theories and styles applicable to healthcare.
2. Apply the principles of transformational leadership to drive changes in healthcare settings. 
3. Apply strategic planning to encourage and implement innovative practices to enhance patient safety, quality of care, continuous improvement and efficiency that align with organizational goals.
4. Develop personalized professional development plans and strategies for career advancement relevant to healthcare settings. 
5. Explore effective strategies to support continuous learning in healthcare. 
6. Demonstrate understanding of the importance of mentorship and networking skills in professional development and relationship respectively, within and beyond the healthcare organization

Course Title: Driving Innovation in Healthcare
Course Description:
The course aims to prepare individuals to thrive in today’s evolving healthcare workplace and offers opportunities to create new thinking for a new healthcare industry. 

With the theme of incubating ideas to solve specific business challenges and framing innovation, this course takes a transdisciplinary approach to morph data into insight, shaping policy, product, and systems to create a better human experience. This course breaks down the different types of innovation and applies them in key business areas in healthcare. Combining both business and public healthcare perspectives, the course strikes a balance between weighing industry standards alongside Design Thinking and user-driven innovation.

•	Strategy Innovation: Applying Design Thinking to Information Technology and Digital Transformation plans as an organisation strategy innovation.

•	Medical Technology – Applying Design Thinking to guide product innovation and development. 

•	Healthcare Processes – Applying Design Thinking to enhance and improve efficiency of work processes as a form of process innovation. 

•	Patient Care – Applying Design Thinking to enhance patient care models as a form of service innovation.

•	Information Technology, Digital Transformation: Design Thinking may be applied to Digital Transformation as an organizational strategy for innovation.

•	Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning and Robotics: Design Thinking can be used in upstream processes to understand User Experience and scope pain points before Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning and Robotics are applied during solutioning. Design Thinking elements are also present throughout the iteration and prototyping phase of solution design.

Course Title: Commercialization and Entrepreneurship in Translational Medicine
Course Description:
This course will expose learners to key issues related to the entrepreneurial aspect of discovery in translational medicine, including protecting intellectual property, working with industry partners, and seeking and accepting funding to develop businesses. 
Learners will learn to maximize the value and utility of data while ensuring ethical and responsible practices. They will explore the stages of drug discovery, understand the dynamics between pharma, biotech, and contract research organisations, and learn about intellectual property management. Additionally, the course covers academia-industry collaborations, market research techniques, and starting a biotech, ensuring innovations meet unmet healthcare needs impacts society.

Course Title: Climate, Environment & Health
Course Description: This course offers learners a comprehensive look at the intricate relationships between climate, environmental change, and health. Delivered intensively over two weeks by leading climate and health experts, it covers topics ranging from environmental epidemiology and sustainable food systems to global governance and the economic frameworks of climate change. Learners will gain valuable insights into policy-focused approaches for addressing climate-related health challenges, including adaptation and mitigation strategies.

Course Title: De-Risking Discoveries
Course Description: In this course, learners will understand the diversity of stakeholders in the translational medicine (TM) continuum and the value of effective interactions between them, while prioritizing the consideration of the risks each stakeholder faces. They will understand the services, contributions, and perspectives of various stakeholders, developing a deeper appreciation of integrated approaches in translating innovations. This will enhance their ability to collaborate effectively with stakeholders. Learners will also combine data insights with dynamic visuals to enhance data interpretation and storytelling in presenting the TM pathway. These skills will be honed through class discussions and stakeholder interactions throughout the course.

Course Title: AI and Ethics in Health: From Clinical Care to Population Health
Course Description:
Explore the ethical dimensions of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in healthcare with our comprehensive two-day course, "AI and Ethics in Health: From Clinical Care to Population Health." 

This course is meticulously designed for healthcare professionals, policymakers, and technologists who are keen to understand and navigate the ethical implications of AI within diverse healthcare environments. Participants will delve into ethical theories and frameworks pertinent to AI in healthcare, discussing critical issues related to data privacy, security, bias, and fairness. The course emphasizes the importance of accountability and transparency in AI-driven healthcare decisions.

Additionally, participants will evaluate the ethical use of AI in various clinical applications, including diagnostics, treatment, personalized medicine, mental health, and disability assistance, while investigating the role of AI in health promotion, addressing health inequalities, emergency response, and public health surveillance. The course also covers the ethical considerations of AI in biosecurity, bioterrorism detection, and pandemic preparedness, as well as strategies for ensuring sustainable AI practices in healthcare. By the end of the course, participants will be well-equipped to ensure that AI technologies are applied ethically and effectively, from individual patient care to broader population health initiatives.

Course Title: Essential Biology for Precision Healthcare and Medicine (Synchronous e-Learning)
Course Description:
By the end of this course, learners will be able to:
a. Understand the biochemical structure and function of proteins, carbohydrates, lipids, and nucleic acids, and their roles in cellular processes.
b. Describe the mechanisms of cell division, including mitosis and meiosis, and understand the significance of recombination in genetic diversity.
c. Explain the processes of deoxyribonucleic acid (DNA) replication, transcription, and translation, and how they contribute to protein synthesis.
d. Analyze the regulation of gene expression at the epigenetic and transcriptional levels, including DNA methylation, histone modifications, and the role of non-coding ribonucleic acids (RNA), such as microRNA (miRNA), long non-coding RNA (lncRNA), and circular RNA (circRNA).
e. Master the fundamental techniques in genetic engineering, including gene cloning and polymerase chain reaction (PCR), and apply these techniques to amplify and quantify DNA and RNA.
f. Utilize quantitative techniques such as real-time PCR and reverse-transcription PCR to assess specific genetic regions and transcripts for research and diagnostic purposes.

Course Title: Monsoon School Certificate Course in Translational Medicine (Classroom and Asynchronous e-Learning)
Course Description: This course provides a high-level overview of the translational medicine (TM) landscape and explores the interactions between the various stakeholders. Learners will engage in personal and professional development to become leaders in TM, all while exploring systems thinking to foster a culture of continuous improvement and learning. Learners will be encouraged to promote their innovation by understanding and leveraging system dynamics within the structures in the TM pathway. By the end of this course, learners will be able to consider the entire TM system rather than individual parts when solving problems, thereby addressing root causes of issues than just symptoms.

Course Title: WSQ Service Excellence in Healthcare: Fostering Positive Customer and Patient Experiences (L2)
Course Description:

This sequence ensures that learners gain a strong foundation, practical application, safety aware-ness, and emotional resilience, making them well-prepared for dementia support roles.

Course Title: Infection Prevention and Control Course
Course Description:
This course is designed to equip learners with the essential skills to support and maintain their organization's infection prevention and control processes. Participants will learn to apply standard infection prevention procedures effectively and promptly, ensuring they meet organizational standards.
Key learning outcomes include:
•	Practicing infection prevention and control measures in a timely and efficient manner.
•	Recognizing and responding to high-risk contamination situations, helping to maintain a safe and healthy work environment for all.
By the end of the course, learners will be well-prepared to contribute to their organization’s infection control efforts, ensuring the safety and well-being of both staff and clients.

Course Title: Small Parts Ultrasound II (Classroom & Asynchronous e-Learning)
Course Description:
Ultrasound plays a crucial role in the evaluation and management of patients with chronic and systemic diseases of the endocrine system such as thyroiditis and other diseases such as breast cancer which are facing rising incidence as the ageing population grows. This course is introduced in response to the industry demand and government healthcare policy in expanding capacity in skills upgrading of the current healthcare workforce.

This intensive “hands on” training program aims to provide participants with the theoretical knowledge and practical skills in small parts, namely breast ultrasound and lumps and bumps imaging. Breast intervention work, breast Magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) and mammography as well as other advanced imaging techniques such as elastography and contrast-enhanced ultrasound will also be covered.

At the end of this course, participants will be able to recognise the normal and abnormal ultrasound findings and will be guided in the competent production, interpretation and assessment of these small parts ultrasound images.

Topics will include gross anatomy and physiology, embryology, clinical indications, equipment and patient preparation, techniques and sonographic protocol in the evaluation of breast, lymph nodes and soft tissue masses; and their normal sonographic anatomy and abnormal imaging findings.

2. It will focus on indications, anatomy, scanning techniques, normal and abnormal findings in a pelvic ultrasound examination.
3.The use of ultrasound images will deepskill the radiography healthcare team in providing clear and accurate diagnosis of illnesses or injuries sustained in the pelvic region of patients to proceed with the most appropriate treatment.

Course Title: Psychological First Aid +

Course Title: Laboratory on Basic Counselling Skills
Course Description: This counselling skill-based course to be conducted in the training laboratory and classroom is to enable our students in the Foundation Studies to: • Acquire the theoretical framework and basic counselling skills in the stages of the counselling process. • Practise the micro-counselling skills through role-plays and live supervision. • Develop qualities of an effective counsellor through the training model of the Person-of-the-Therapist (POTT) • Show cultural sensitivity and adaption within the multicultural context of Singapore. • Demonstrate the skills needed to provide and receive feedback on counselling skills and interventions. • Critically examine and evaluate the impact of personal values and beliefs on the counselling process, including ethical issues in counselling.

Course Title: Planning and Managing Healthcare Facilities

Course Title: Certificate in Phlebotomy
Course Description: Phlebotomy is an invasive procedure that requires theoretical knowledge and practical skills in collecting venous blood in a safe, accurate and reliable manner for clinical laboratory investigations and therapeutic assessments. This course is for final-year polytechnic students from the Biomedical Science or related courses, medical technologists, nurses, healthcare workers and individuals who aspire to acquire phlebotomy skills for their job functions and future employment. As the syllabus of this phlebotomy course is designed as a pre-requisite for International Phlebotomy Technician examination conducted by the American Society for Clinical Pathology [PBT) (ASCPi)], graduates of this phlebotomy course can subsequently proceed to acquire this international qualification.

Course Title: Psychological First Aid (Basic) - Bridging Over Troubled Waters
Course Description:
This course will help you gain knowledge and skills to attend to a person in distress. Psychological First Aid is a simple and cost-effective approach to help others who are experiencing psychological distress.  In building people's own capacity and emotional resilience to recover psychologically, the psychological first aider can help reduce initial distress and promote flexible coping to overcome the crisis. Research shows that psychological first aid reduces the risk of mental health deterioration post-crisis. It has widespread appeal as it can be safely delivered by individuals without professional mental health training.  

Background/Rationale: Psychological First Aid is a caring and compassionate approach to assisting people coping with stressful or traumatic life events. The core aim is to build people’s own emotional capacity to recover psychologically by reducing initial distress, meet immediate needs, promote flexible coping and encourage adjustment by identifying their own strengths and abilities. Psychological First Aid is based on an understanding that people affected by psychological distress will experience a range of early behavioural reactions that may interfere with their ability to cope. It provides calm and hopeful support to help people feel safe, connected to others, able to help themselves, and gain access to effective mental health professional services.  As this course requires participants to engage in active role plays with scenarios of persons-in-distress, and respectful facilitation of open discussions of personal stories and challenges, confidentiality is required for course to be effectively run.

Course Title: Nutrition for Weight Management
Course Description:
In a world filled with conflicting diet advice and quick-fix weight-loss solutions, achieving and maintaining a healthy weight can feel overwhelming. This two-day course is designed specifically for nutrition and health educators, nutrition executives, and health coaches, equipping them with the scientific knowledge and practical strategies needed to support clients and communities effectively.
Rooted in evidence-based nutrition, this course provides an in-depth exploration of the physiological and metabolic mechanisms that influence body weight.
Through case studies, interactive discussions, and hands-on activities, participants will develop the skills to critically assess weight management strategies, debunk myths, and deliver personalised, sustainable interventions tailored to diverse populations.
This course is ideal for professionals looking to enhance their expertise and confidently educate others on healthy, science-backed approaches to weight management.
